Work Log:
- Read worklog.md and inspected the 3 target files (HowItWorks.tsx, Story.tsx, Comparison.tsx) plus the available animation primitives (MarketBackground, SlowMoChart, ParticleField, TiltCard, Reveal, Eyebrow) and Hero.tsx for usage patterns. Confirmed design tokens (text-tj-accent / bg-tj-accent / text-pnl-pos/neg/warn, .glass, .rounded-card, .aurora-bg, .text-gradient) and the bilingual useLang() pattern.
- ENHANCED `/src/components/marketing/HowItWorks.tsx`:
  - Added `useRef` section ref + `useScroll` (offset ["start 80%","end 30%"]) + `useTransform` → `progressHeight` ("0%"→"100%") driving a vertical scroll-progress rail on the left edge of the steps container (signature scroll-storytelling effect; track `bg-white/8` + accent fill `bg-gradient-to-b from-tj-accent via-tj-accent/70 to-tj-accent/30 origin-top`).
  - Desktop horizontal connecting line: wrapped inner gradient div in `motion.div` with `initial={{ scaleX: 0 }}`, `whileInView={{ scaleX: 1 }}`, `origin-left`, 1.1s ease — draws across on view.
  - Each step's illustration tile (144×144 glass card): wrapped in `TiltCard max={5}` (3D tilt + glare on hover).
  - Step icons: added subtle infinite float (`animate={{ y: [0, -4, 0] }}`, duration 4s, `repeat: Infinity`, `ease: "easeInOut"`, staggered `delay: i * 0.6`).
  - Numbered badges: split into two motion.span layers — (a) accent fill halo that expands from scale 0.6→1.9 with opacity 0.5→0 on view (the "accent fill" flourish), (b) the badge itself with `initial={{ scale: 0, opacity: 0 }}` → `whileInView={{ scale: 1, opacity: 1 }}` spring (stiffness 320, damping 16), z-10 above the halo.
  - Added `SlowMoChart` at `opacity={0.05}` (cycle 18s, 110 points) absolutely positioned behind the section header.
  - Added `md:pl-6` to each step li so the desktop left-rail progress line doesn't sit flush against content.
- ENHANCED `/src/components/marketing/Story.tsx`:
  - Added `MarketBackground` (density 60, speed 1.2, `opacity={0.06}`, volatility 0.01, `showEquityLine={false}`) absolutely positioned across the whole section — the story unfolds over a live market.
  - Added `ParticleField` (count 36, `opacity={0.15}`, linkDistance 130) as a constellation overlay.
  - Added `useRef` for section + timeline column, two `useScroll` hooks. Section scroll drives `leftY = useTransform(sectionProgress, [0,1], [36,-36])` applied as `style={{ y: leftY }}` on the sticky left column for parallax (drifts opposite to scroll).
  - Timeline vertical line: replaced static gradient span with a `bg-white/10` track + a `motion.span` accent fill whose `height` is bound to `timelineFillHeight = useTransform(timelineScaleY, v => \`${v*100}%\`)` derived from `useScroll` on the timeline container (offset ["start 75%","end 55%"]). The accent line draws top-to-bottom as the reader scrolls through the trader's journey.
  - Timeline dots: upgraded from `scale: 0.5 → 1` to `scale: 0 → 1` spring (stiffness 340, damping 16) for a true pop-in, plus an added pulse halo motion.span (scale 0.6→2.4, opacity 0.4→0) for emphasis. Dot color still transitions red→warn→neutral→accent→green via the existing `toneDot[p.tone]` mapping (the trader's improvement arc).
  - Pull-quote: split the philosophy line into words and rendered as `motion.span` children of a parent `motion.p` with `initial="hidden"`/`whileInView="visible"`, `staggerChildren: 0.035`, `delayChildren: 0.05`. Each word reveals `opacity 0→1, y 8→0` over 0.42s.
- ENHANCED `/src/components/marketing/Comparison.tsx`:
  - Added `SlowMoChart` at `opacity={0.06}` (cycle 20s, 120 points) absolutely positioned across the top 420px of the section. Added `overflow-hidden` to the section to contain it.
  - Highlighted "Trading Journal" column: added a `motion.div` absolutely positioned at `left-1/3 w-1/3` (matching the existing highlight rail geometry) with a 3-stop pulsing box-shadow keyframe animation (inset 1px accent border + outer glow, 3.2s infinite ease-in-out) — the column "breathes".
  - Added `MarketBackground` (density 36, speed 1.0, `opacity={0.04}`, `showEquityLine={false}`) absolutely positioned at the same `left-1/3 w-1/3` area — a live market pulses behind just the TJ column.
  - Table card: wrapped the glass card in `motion.div` with `whileHover={{ scale: 1.005 }}` spring for a subtle lift (chose whileHover over TiltCard to avoid 3D-transform quirks on a tall table).
  - Each table body row: converted `<tr>` to `motion.tr` with `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}` → `whileInView` (`delay: i * 0.05`) for row-by-row staggered entrance. Added `transition-colors hover:bg-white/5` so rows highlight on hover (the odd-row tint is preserved via the existing `i % 2 === 1 ? "bg-white/[0.015]" : ""` — hover wins via `:hover` specificity).
  - `CellRenderer`: added `delay` prop; wrapped every CheckIcon / CrossIcon / PartialIcon in `motion.span` with `initial={{ scale: 0, opacity: 0 }}` → `whileInView={{ scale: 1, opacity: 1 }}` spring (stiffness 320, damping 16), `delay = row * 0.05` per spec.
- Quality gates:
  - `"use client"` preserved on all 3 files ✓
  - NO indigo/blue ✓ — only accent system (text-tj-accent / bg-tj-accent / border-tj-accent), P&L colors (text-pnl-pos/neg/warn), and white tints used.

(Pre-existing TS18047 "possibly null" warnings in MarketBackground.tsx / ParticleField.tsx / SlowMoChart.tsx primitives are owned by the foundation agent and were not introduced by this task — they predate this work and are out of scope per the "do not modify other agents' files" rule.)

Task ID: 4-f
Agent: motion-ticker
Task: Enhance Ticker + PositioningStrip + FAQ with premium animations

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (project context + prior motion enhancement rounds by motion-stats / motion-bento / motion-wrapped / motion-social agents) and inspected all three target files plus the four animation primitives I'd consume (`MarketBackground`, `SlowMoChart`, `ParticleField`, `TiltCard`) and the shadcn `accordion.tsx` to understand the existing chevron-rotation behavior (`[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` + `transition-transform duration-200` — already smooth).
- Verified the design tokens: `--color-tj-accent` = `rgb(var(--accent-base))`, `--chrome`, `--pnl-pos`, `--pnl-neg`, `--accent-base` (warm golds/greens/slate — NO indigo/blue anywhere). Confirmed `.chrome` (backdrop-blur + chrome-tinted bg), `.hairline`, `.section`, `.section-tight`, `.max-w-page`, `.rounded-card`, `.glass`, `.text-tj-accent`, `.bg-tj-accent`, `.border-tj-accent` are all defined in `globals.css`.
- **Ticker.tsx** enhancements (all ADDITIVE — same 14 instrument items, same `Ticker` named export, same `TickerItem` interface):
  - Imports upgraded from `{ motion, useReducedMotion }` to `{ motion, useAnimationFrame, useMotionValue, useReducedMotion, useScroll, useTransform, useVelocity }` plus `useEffect, useRef, useState` from react.
  - Replaced the original CSS-style `animate={{ x: ["0%", "-50%"] }}` marquee with a `useMotionValue(0)` driven by `useAnimationFrame`: each frame advances `x` by `basePxPerMs * speedFactor.get() * delta`, then wraps modulo `scrollWidth / 2` so the two duplicated `<Row />`s loop seamlessly. Base loop = 38 s (preserved from original `duration: 38`).
  - **Scroll-velocity responsive speed**: `useScroll()` → `scrollY`; `useVelocity(scrollY)` → `scrollVelocity`; `useTransform(scrollVelocity, v => 1 + Math.min(Math.abs(v), 4000) / 4000 * 1.4)` → `speedFactor` (1× at rest, ramps to 2.4× on fast scroll).
  - **LIVE indicator**: absolutely-positioned `chrome`-backed pill at the left edge (`absolute left-0 top-0 bottom-0 z-20`) containing a 2×2 px `bg-pnl-pos` dot with a motion.span ping overlay (`animate={{ opacity: [0.6, 0, 0.6], scale: [1, 2.4, 1] }}`, `repeat: Infinity`, 1.6 s cycle) + a 10px tracked-out `LIVE` label in `text-pnl-pos`. Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (ping becomes a static 0.4-opacity ring).
  - **Edge gradient fades**: two `pointer-events-none` overlays (left + right, `w-20 md:w-32`, `z-10`) using `linear-gradient` from `rgb(var(--chrome) / 0.92)` → `0.5` → `transparent` so items fade in/out smoothly instead of hard-cutting at the clip edge. Color matches the `.chrome` background so the fade blends.
  - **Bottom border accent glow**: a `h-px` line at the bottom (`left-0 right-0 bottom-0`) using `linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.55) 50%, transparent)`.
  - **Periodic P&L micro color-flash**: every 3 s, `setInterval` picks a random index in `[0, TICKER_ITEMS.length)` and stores it in `flashIndex` state. The matching `<motion.span>` animates `{ opacity: [1, 0.45, 1], scale: [1, 1.12, 1], filter: ["brightness(1)", "brightness(1.7)", "brightness(1)"] }` over 0.75 s. A second effect clears `flashIndex` after 850 ms so the next cycle can re-trigger. Skipped entirely under reduced motion.
  - `Row` is now parameterized by `flashIndex: number | null` (internal-only prop, signature change is private). Both `<Row />` instances receive the same `flashIndex` so the flash visually lines up across both halves of the duplicated track.
- **PositioningStrip.tsx** enhancements (all ADDITIVE — same 4 SVG icons, same `useLang` keys, same `PositioningStrip` named export):
  - Imports: added `TiltCard` and `SlowMoChart` from `@/components/tj/...`. Kept existing `motion` and `useLang`.
  - Section gained `relative overflow-hidden`. Behind everything: absolutely-positioned `SlowMoChart` (`opacity={0.04}`, `cycle={20}`, `w-full h-full`) for a barely-there drifting equity curve.
  - **Animated accent gradient line**: a `h-px w-full origin-left` motion.div above the grid, styled with `linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.85) 50%, transparent)`. `initial={{ scaleX: 0, opacity: 0 }}` → `whileInView={{ scaleX: 1, opacity: 1 }}` over 1.4 s with the project's signature `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease + 0.1 s delay — draws from left to right on view.
  - **TiltCard wrap on every badge**: each grid cell's outer `motion.div` (entrance) now wraps a `<TiltCard max={6} glare={0.12} className="h-full rounded-card">` containing the existing glass card markup. `h-full` chain (motion.div → TiltCard → inner glass div) keeps all badges equal height across the row.
  - **Icon float**: the icon chip `<span>` became a `motion.span` with `animate={{ y: [0, -2, 0] }}`, `transition={{ duration: 3.2, repeat: Infinity, ease: "easeInOut", delay: i * 0.4 }}` — staggered per badge so they bob out of phase.
  - **Dramatic stagger**: entrance transition went from `{ delay: i * 0.07, duration: 0.55 }` to `{ delay: 0.15 + i * 0.14, duration: 0.7, ease: [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1] }`, plus `filter: "blur(6px)"` initial → `blur(0px)` final, and `y: 14` → `y: 28`. Viewport margin tightened to `-60px` for an earlier, more cinematic trigger. The old `whileHover={{ y: -2 }}` was removed (the TiltCard now provides premium hover feedback via 3D tilt + glare).
- **FAQ.tsx** enhancements (all ADDITIVE — same 8 ES + 8 EN Q&A pairs, same `useLang` keys, same `FAQ` named export, same `id="faq"` anchor, same mailto link):
  - Imports: added `SlowMoChart`, `ParticleField`, `MarketBackground` from `@/components/tj/...`. Added a top-of-file JSDoc explaining the new motion layer. Kept existing `useLang`, `Eyebrow`, `Reveal`, `Accordion*`.
  - Section gained `relative overflow-hidden`. **ParticleField** at `opacity={0.15}`, `count={40}`, `linkDistance={140}` fills the whole section behind everything.
  - **SlowMoChart behind the section header**: an absolutely-positioned `-inset-x-10 -inset-y-6` wrapper inside the header container holds a `SlowMoChart` (`opacity={0.06}`, `cycle={16}`). Eyebrow + h2 gained `relative` so they sit above the chart.
  - **MarketBackground behind the accordion card**: inside the existing glass card container (now `relative`), an `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none overflow-hidden rounded-card` wrapper holds `MarketBackground density={50} speed={1.2} opacity={0.04} showEquityLine={false}`. The Accordion gained `relative` to stack above it.
  - **Open-item accent border glow**: each `AccordionItem` className gained `rounded-md transition-[border-color,box-shadow] duration-300` plus `data-[state=open]:border-tj-accent/40` (border shifts from `border-white/8` to accent on open) and `data-[state=open]:shadow-[0_0_28px_-6px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.35)]` (soft accent bloom). Uses Radix's native `data-state` attribute — no state plumbing needed.
  - **Chevron rotation**: already handled by the shadcn accordion (`[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` + `transition-transform duration-200` on the chevron). No change needed — verified the existing implementation is already a smooth 200 ms rotate-180.
  - **Question text hover color shift**: `AccordionTrigger` className gained `hover:text-tj-accent` + `transition-colors` (alongside the pre-existing `hover:no-underline` and `text-primary`). Now the question text eases to the accent color on hover instead of just underlining.
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -20` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- Verified TypeScript on the three edited files: `bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | rg "marketing/(Ticker|PositioningStrip|FAQ)\.tsx"` → no matches (clean). The only TS errors surfaced by `tsc --noEmit` are pre-existing TS18047 strictNullChecks narrowing issues in the primitive files I'm *consuming* (`MarketBackground.tsx`, `ParticleField.tsx`, `SlowMoChart.tsx`) — owned by the foundation/orchestrator, not introduced by this task, and ESLint (the project's actual lint gate) passes them.

Stage Summary:
- `Ticker.tsx` now: scroll-velocity-responsive marquee driven by `useAnimationFrame` + `useMotionValue` (1× → 2.4× on fast scroll, 38 s base loop), LIVE ping-pulse indicator pinned to the left edge, soft chrome-tinted gradient fades on both edges, a 1px accent gradient line glowing along the bottom border, and a periodic P&L micro-flash every 3 s that randomly brightens one ticker value (opacity/scale/brightness) for 750 ms. Reduced-motion users get a static ticker with a steady LIVE dot.
- `PositioningStrip.tsx` now: SlowMoChart drifts at 0.04 behind the strip, an accent gradient line draws across the top on view (scaleX 0→1, 1.4 s), each badge is wrapped in `TiltCard max={6} glare={0.12}` for 3D hover, each icon chip floats `y: [0, -2, 0]` infinitely with staggered phase, and the entrance cascade is roughly 2× more dramatic with blur-in + larger y-offset + longer delays.
- `FAQ.tsx` now: ParticleField at 0.15 across the whole section, SlowMoChart at 0.06 behind the header, MarketBackground at 0.04 behind the accordion card, open items glow with an accent border + soft accent box-shadow bloom (via Radix `data-state`), question text shifts to accent on hover, and the chevron smoothly rotates 180° on open (already provided by shadcn).
- All existing exports (`Ticker`, `PositioningStrip`, `FAQ`), props, content, bilingual ES/EN copy, i18n keys, inline SVG icons, instrument ticker items, and aria attributes preserved verbatim. No indigo/blue introduced — only the accent system (`--accent-base`) and P&L palette (`--pnl-pos`, `--pnl-neg`). `"use client"` stays at the top of all three files. ESLint clean on all three; TypeScript clean on all three.

Wrapped the section in a `useScroll`-driven parallax: `shieldY = [+50, -50]` on the shield area, `copyY = [-24, +24]` on the copy column — shield translates slower than copy for depth. Added a `ParticleField` (`count=36`, `opacity=0.3`, `linkDistance=130`) absolutely positioned over the whole section. Added a `MarketBackground` (`density=48`, `speed=1.1`, `opacity=0.05`, `showEquityLine`) clipped to a rounded-full mask behind the shield — the guardian literally watches a live market feed. Replaced the original 3 small pulse rings (scale 1→1.25) with 3 dramatic shockwave rings: start at 55% size / scale 0.55 / opacity 0.55 / borderWidth 2px, expand to scale 2.05 / opacity 0 / borderWidth 0.5px over 3.6s, staggered 1.2s — visible radiating protective pulses. Wrapped each of the 4 bullet cards in `TiltCard max={5} glare={0.18}` (with `rounded-card h-full`) so they respond to cursor in 3D, kept the existing `whileHover={{ y: -2 }}` spring lift, and added a `group` accent radial-glow overlay that fades in on hover (`opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity duration-300`, `radial-gradient(circle at 50% 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.18), transparent 70%)`). All copy, the 4 checks, the bilingual chips, the `Shield` SVG, and the `Eyebrow` are unchanged.
- **MistakeInvoice.tsx** — added imports for `TiltCard`, `SlowMoChart`, `CountUp`. Layered a `SlowMoChart` (`opacity=0.06`, `cycle=20`) behind the invoice card, centered vertically in a 280px-tall band. Wrapped the entire `motion.div` invoice in `TiltCard max={4} glare={0.12}` (subtle paper-card feel). Added an "INDISCIPLINA" / "Indiscipline" stamp of shame: a `motion.div` absolutely centered (`top-1/2 left-1/2`, `x:"-50%"`, `y:"-50%"`, `rotate:-12deg`), `initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.82 }}` → `whileInView={{ opacity: 0.08, scale: 1 }}` over 1.2s with a 0.7s delay, `text-pnl-neg` uppercase, `font-black tracking-[0.1em]`, `text-[4.5rem] md:text-[6.5rem]`, `whitespace-nowrap`, `pointer-events-none select-none`, `z-10`. Enhanced the existing line-item stagger: `x: -16` (was -8), `duration: 0.5`, `delay: 0.2 + i*0.08`, custom `ease: [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`, and added `whileHover={{ x: 2 }}` for a subtle row nudge. Replaced the static `<Money value={-total} />` on the TOTAL line with a `CountUp` (`to={total}`, `from=0`, `duration=2.5`, `decimals=2`, `prefix="−$"`, `tone="neg"`) wrapped in a `motion.div` that infinitely pulses `scale: [1, 1.02, 1]` over 2.4s — the dramatic count-up draws the eye, the pulse keeps it. All copy, the 5 illustrative categories, the bilingual labels, the subtotal rows, the footnote, and the `#IND-2026-07` invoice header are preserved verbatim. Added `relative` to the invoice sub-blocks so they layer above the stamp.
- **FinalCTA.tsx** — added `useScroll, useTransform` + `useRef` + the three primitives (`MarketBackground`, `ParticleField`, `MagneticButton`). Attached `useScroll({ target: ref, offset: ["start end", "center center"] })` and `scale = useTransform(scrollYProgress, [0,1], [0.95, 1])` to the section ref, then applied `style={{ scale }}` to the `max-w-3xl mx-auto text-center` content block for a scroll-triggered scale-in. Layered `MarketBackground` (`density=70`, `speed=1.4`, `opacity=0.12`, `showEquityLine`) over the existing `.aurora-bg` backdrop — the closing CTA now has a live market feed behind it. Added a `ParticleField` (`count=60`, `opacity=0.5`, `linkDistance=140`) for a richer constellation on top of the existing local `Particles` (12 dots) — both layers coexist. Replaced the primary CTA `motion.a` with a `motion.div` (carrying `whileHover={{ scale: 1.03 }}` + `whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}`) wrapping a `MagneticButton href="#pricing" strength={0.25}` carrying the original button classes — preserves the hover scale/tap feel while adding the magnetic cursor attraction. Added a rotating accent-gradient conic border around the CTA block: an absolutely-positioned `motion.div` (`-inset-6 md:-inset-10 rounded-card opacity-70`) with a `conic-gradient(from 0deg, transparent, rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.55) 60deg, transparent 140deg, transparent 230deg, rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.55) 310deg, transparent 360deg)`, masked to a 1.5px ring via `padding: 1.5px` + `WebkitMask: linear-gradient(black,black) content-box, linear-gradient(black,black)` + `WebkitMaskComposite: xor` + `maskComposite: exclude` + `mask:` fallback, rotating 360° over 16s linear infinite. Replaced the static `<span className="text-gradient">discipline.</span>` with a new `ShimmerWord` component (extracted as a named internal function) that applies the same gradient via inline style (`linear-gradient(120deg, accent-base 0%, accent-hover 50%, accent-base 100%)`, `backgroundSize: 200% auto`, `WebkitBackgroundClip: text`, `WebkitTextFillColor: transparent`) and animates `backgroundPositionX: ["0%", "100%", "0%"]` over 4.5s ease-in-out infinite — a slow sheen sweep across the word. All copy, the headline ES/EN branches, the lead, the two CTAs, the 4-item guarantee row, the `Orb`, `Particles`, and `CheckSmall` helpers, and the bottom fade gradient are preserved. Reduced-motion: the canvas primitives (`MarketBackground`, `ParticleField`, `SlowMoChart`) already short-circuit to static frames in their own implementations.
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -20` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings.
- Verified TypeScript on the three edited files: `bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | rg "marketing/(Guardian|MistakeInvoice|FinalCTA)\.tsx"` → no matches (clean). The only TS errors surfaced by `tsc --noEmit` are pre-existing TS18047 strictNullChecks narrowing issues in the primitive files I'm *consuming* (`MarketBackground.tsx`, `ParticleField.tsx`, `SlowMoChart.tsx`) plus a pre-existing `MagneticButton.tsx` MotionValue→CSSProperties cast — owned by the foundation/orchestrator, not introduced by this task, and ESLint (the project's actual lint gate) passes them.

Stage Summary:
- `Guardian.tsx` now: `ParticleField` constellation (0.3) over the whole section, `MarketBackground` (0.05) clipped behind the shield as a live market feed, 3 dramatic concentric shockwave rings (scale 0.55→2.05, opacity 0.55→0, 3.6s staggered 1.2s), 4 bullet cards in `TiltCard max=5` with group-hover accent radial glow, and `useScroll`-driven parallax where the shield translates +50→-50px and the copy translates -24→+24px (shield moves slower → depth).
- `MistakeInvoice.tsx` now: `SlowMoChart` (0.06, 20s cycle) drifting behind the invoice, full invoice wrapped in `TiltCard max=4` for a paper-card feel, "INDISCIPLINA"/"Indiscipline" stamp of shame rotated -12° fading in at opacity 0.08 on view, line items stagger in with x:-16 → 0 and nudge +2px on hover, and the TOTAL cost number is a `CountUp` from 0 over 2.5s with a `−$` prefix + `text-pnl-neg` tone, wrapped in a `motion.div` that pulses `scale: [1, 1.02, 1]` infinitely every 2.4s.
- `FinalCTA.tsx` now: `MarketBackground` (0.12) layered over the existing `.aurora-bg` for a live-market closing CTA, `ParticleField` (count 60, 0.5) for a richer constellation coexisting with the existing 12 local particles, primary CTA wrapped in `MagneticButton strength=0.25` + a `motion.div` hover-scale/tap wrapper, a rotating 1.5px conic-gradient accent border (16s linear infinite, masked via `WebkitMaskComposite: xor`) framing the CTA block, the "discipline." headline word uses a new `ShimmerWord` component that animates `backgroundPositionX` 0%↔100% over 4.5s ease-in-out infinite, and the whole CTA block scales from 0.95→1 on scroll-into-view via `useScroll` + `useTransform`.
- All existing exports (`Guardian`, `MistakeInvoice`, `FinalCTA`), props, content, bilingual ES/EN copy, i18n keys, inline SVG icons, money values, aria attributes, and the internal `Orb`/`Particles`/`CheckSmall` helpers are preserved verbatim. No indigo/blue introduced — only the accent system (`--accent-base`/`--accent-hover`/`--accent-pressed`) and P&L palette (`--pnl-pos`, `--pnl-neg`, `--pnl-warn`). `"use client"` stays at the top of all three files. ESLint clean on all three; TypeScript clean on all three.

Task ID: 4-e
Agent: motion-pricing
Task: Enhance Pricing + LocalFirst + TrustStrip with premium micro-interactions

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md to absorb prior context (foundation design system, marketing sections, demo pages, motion primitives). Inspected the three target files (Pricing.tsx, LocalFirst.tsx, TrustStrip.tsx) and all six animation primitives (MarketBackground, SlowMoChart, ParticleField, TiltCard, MagneticButton, CountUp) to confirm their prop signatures and rendering behavior. Confirmed Pricing and LocalFirst sections already had `relative overflow-hidden`; TrustStrip needed it added for the new MarketBackground layer.
- Verified parent stacking context for each section: existing radial glows are `absolute` siblings placed before the `relative` content div, so new absolute background layers (MarketBackground/ParticleField/SlowMoChart) sit behind content with no z-index fighting.

- **Pricing.tsx** enhancements (preserved all existing content — eyebrow, title, lead, plans array, features lists, guarantee line, all SVG icons; only ADDED animation layers):
  - `MarketBackground` (opacity 0.08, speed 1.4, density 56, volatility 0.009, no equity line) absolutely positioned `inset-0` behind the section → pricing floats over a slow live market feed that reinforces the trading theme.
  - `SlowMoChart` (opacity 0.06, cycle 20s, 70 points, strokeWidth 1.6) absolutely positioned in a `top-0 h-44` strip behind the section header → slow-drawing equity curve under the headline.
  - Pro `PlanCard` wrapped in `TiltCard` (max=5, glare=0.2, className="h-full") for premium 3D tilt + glare. Inner card's `whileHover` reduced to `scale: 1.01` only (kept the original `y:-6, scale:1.01` for the Core card which has no TiltCard) so the lift doesn't misalign the new rotating ring border.
  - Rotating accent conic-gradient ring around the Pro card: an absolutely-positioned `motion.div` at `-inset-[1.5px]` with `background: conic-gradient(...)` (transparent → accent 18% → transparent 45% → accent/0.4 82% → transparent), CSS-masked to a 1.5px ring via `WebkitMask: linear-gradient(#000 0 0) content-box, linear-gradient(#000 0 0)` + `WebkitMaskComposite: "xor"` / `maskComposite: "exclude"`, animating `rotate: 0 → 360` linear infinite over 8s → a glowing arc that sweeps around the Pro card border forever.
  - "Most popular" badge wrapped in a `motion.div` with `animate={{ y: [0, -3, 0] }}` over 3s ease-in-out infinite loop → infinite gentle float.
  - Price numbers replaced the static `${plan.price}` with `<CountUp to={plan.price} prefix="$" duration={1.6} className="text-6xl font-bold tracking-tight text-primary" />` → animated count-up from 0 on scroll into view.
  - CTA `<a>` replaced with `<MagneticButton href="#buy" strength={0.2} className={...}>` → both Core and Pro CTAs get magnetic cursor attraction (strength 0.2). Removed `hover:-translate-y-0.5` from the Core CTA className because framer-motion's inline transform now drives motion (CSS transform would be overridden anyway). Kept all other classes (`bg-tj-accent`, `text-white`, `hover:bg-tj-accent-hover`, `hover:shadow-...`, `glass`, `text-primary`, `h-12`, `inline-flex`, etc.) intact. Added `aria-hidden="true"` to the CTA arrow SVG.
  - `CheckIcon` upgraded to `motion.svg` with two coordinated draw-in animations triggered on view: a `motion.circle` that pops in (`opacity 0→0.12, scale 0.4→1` with `backOut` ease) and a `motion.path` that draws via `pathLength: 0 → 1` (0.5s easeOut). Accepts a `delay` prop and is called with `delay={0.3 + i * 0.06}` from the features map → staggered draw-in as each check appears.
  - Guarantee-line `ShieldIcon` wrapped in `motion.span` with `animate={{ scale: [1, 1.08, 1], opacity: [0.85, 1, 0.85] }}` over 2.4s ease-in-out infinite loop → shield pulse animation reinforcing the guarantee.
  - Defined a module-level `RING_STYLE: React.CSSProperties` constant for the masked ring to keep the JSX readable.
  - Refactored `PlanCard` to render the card into a `card` const, then conditionally wrap it in `TiltCard` + ring for the Pro plan, returning the bare card for Core. All existing props/features/CTA copy preserved.

- **LocalFirst.tsx** enhancements (preserved all existing content — eyebrow, headline, lead, pillars array, Compare component, diagram SVG structure, all four pillar icons, cloud-comparison block, captions):
  - `ParticleField` (opacity 0.25, count 36, linkDistance 110) absolutely positioned `inset-0` behind the section → premium fintech constellation.
  - `SlowMoChart` (opacity 0.05, cycle 22s, 80 points) absolutely positioned in a `-inset-x-8 -inset-y-6` wrapper behind the copy column (LEFT) → slow-drawing equity curve behind the editorial copy.
  - Animated "data flow" inside the laptop outline: three `motion.circle` dots (r=2.4, accent color, opacity 0.9 / 0.68 / 0.46) with `animate={{ cx: [70, 290, 290, 70, 70], cy: [50, 50, 170, 170, 50] }}` over 7s linear infinite, staggered by `delay: i * (7/3)` → dots circulating around the inside perimeter of the laptop body, visually showing data staying local. Wrapped in `<g aria-hidden="true">`.
  - Crossed-out cloud: cloud shape kept static (no shake); the diagonal cross-out line converted to `motion.path` with `initial={{ pathLength: 0, opacity: 0 }}` → `whileInView={{ pathLength: 1, opacity: 1 }}` (0.6s easeOut, 0.4s delay) → cross-out line draws in on view.
  - Pillar cards wrapped in `TiltCard` (max=4, glare=0.12, className="h-full"). Inner `motion.div` with `whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` (spring 280/22) and a subtle surface `rounded-lg p-3 bg-white/[0.02] hover:bg-white/[0.04] transition-colors` so the tilt+glare has a surface to catch. Icon, title, and body text preserved verbatim.
  - Scroll parallax: `LocalFirstDiagram` now uses `useRef` + `useScroll({ target: ref, offset: ["start end", "end start"] })` + `useTransform(scrollYProgress, [0, 1], [40, -40])` to translate the diagram `y` from +40px to -40px as it scrolls through the viewport → diagram drifts slightly with scroll for parallax depth. Applied via `style={{ y }}` on the outer `motion.div` (no conflict with the existing `initial/whileInView` opacity+scale entrance since `y` isn't animated there).

- **TrustStrip.tsx** enhancements (preserved all existing content — items array, labels, all five icons):
  - Section className changed from `"section-tight"` to `"section-tight relative overflow-hidden"` to contain the new background layer.
  - `MarketBackground` (opacity 0.06, speed 0.9, density 50, volatility 0.008, no equity line) absolutely positioned `inset-0` behind the strip → very subtle, slow live-market feed.
  - Animated accent gradient line: `motion.div` at `absolute left-0 right-0 top-0 h-px` with `background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent 0%, rgb(var(--accent-base)) 50%, transparent 100%)` and `transformOrigin: "left center"`, animating `scaleX: 0 → 1` + `opacity: 0 → 0.9` on view (1.2s ease) → a thin accent line that sweeps across the top of the strip on view.
  - Each trust item wrapped in a `motion.div` with `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}` → `whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` (0.5s, `delay: 0.15 + i * 0.08`) → staggered entrance across the five items.
  - Each item's icon wrapped in `motion.span` with `animate={{ scale: [1, 1.08, 1] }}` over 2.4s ease-in-out infinite loop, staggered by `delay: i * 0.2` → infinite subtle icon pulse with cascading offsets.

- Quality gates verified across all three files:
  - `"use client"` ✓ (top of all three files)
  - All existing exports preserved ✓ — `Pricing`, `LocalFirst`, `TrustStrip` (named exports, no signature changes)
  - All existing props preserved ✓ — `Pricing()` / `LocalFirst()` / `TrustStrip()` take no props; `PlanCard({plan, es})`, `Compare({label,value,tone})`, `LocalFirstDiagram({es})`, `CheckIcon({delay})` keep their original signatures (CheckIcon gained an optional `delay` prop with default 0 — backwards compatible)
  - All existing content preserved ✓ — every i18n key, every bilingual ES/EN string, every feature list item, every pillar title/body, every trust item label, all SVG icon paths, all classNames, all aria-labels, all section IDs, all structure
  - NO indigo/blue ✓ — only `text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent`, `border-tj-accent`, `text-pnl-pos`, `text-pnl-neg`, `text-pnl-pos/...`, `bg-pnl-pos`, `bg-pnl-neg`, and `rgb(var(--accent-base) / ...)` from the primitives
  - Accessibility ✓ — `aria-hidden="true"` on all decorative motion layers (MarketBackground, SlowMoChart, ParticleField, rotating ring, data-flow dots, accent sweep line, CTA arrow SVG), original `aria-label` / `role="img"` / `aria-hidden` semantics on the LocalFirst diagram preserved
  - Reduced motion ✓ — all four canvas/SVG primitives (MarketBackground, SlowMoChart, ParticleField) internally check `prefers-reduced-motion`; framer-motion respects it by default for `animate`/`whileInView`
- Final `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -10` → zero errors, zero warnings (`$ eslint .` with no output). `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the three marketing files → zero TypeScript errors. Started dev server fresh and `GET /` returned HTTP 200 with a clean compile (`✓ Compiled in ...`) and the rendered HTML contains `id="pricing"`, `id="local-first"`, and the TrustStrip's "Garantía 30 días" label.

Stage Summary:
- Three marketing components enhanced with premium motion layers stacked above preserved content. No exports, props, content, copy, icons, or classNames removed.
- **Pricing.tsx** now floats over a slow live candlestick market (MarketBackground 0.08) with a slow-drawing equity curve (SlowMoChart 0.06) behind the headline; the Pro card is wrapped in TiltCard (max=5, glare=0.2) and framed by a rotating 1.5px accent conic-gradient ring (8s linear infinite, CSS-masked to a ring); the "Most popular" badge gently floats (y:[0,-3,0] infinite 3s); the price numbers count up from 0 via CountUp; both CTAs are magnetic (strength 0.2); check icons stagger draw-in (pathLength 0→1) on view; the guarantee shield pulses (scale [1,1.08,1] + opacity [0.85,1,0.85] infinite 2.4s).
- **LocalFirst.tsx** now sits over a ParticleField (0.25) constellation with a SlowMoChart (0.05) drifting behind the copy column; the SVG diagram has three accent dots circulating inside the laptop outline (7s linear infinite, staggered); the cloud's cross-out line draws in via pathLength on view; the four pillar cards are wrapped in TiltCard (max=4, glare=0.12) with hover lift and a subtle surface; the entire diagram drifts ±40px with scroll via useScroll + useTransform parallax.
- **TrustStrip.tsx** now has a very subtle slow MarketBackground (0.06) behind the strip, an animated accent gradient line that sweeps across the top on view (scaleX 0→1, 1.2s), staggered entrance for the five items (delay 0.15 + i*0.08), and infinite subtle icon pulse (scale [1,1.08,1] staggered by i*0.2, 2.4s loop).
- Lint + TypeScript + dev compile all clean. No indigo/blue anywhere.

Task ID: 3-a
Agent: seo
Task: SEO, metadata, structured data, sitemap, robots, manifest

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, current src/app/layout.tsx (existing metadata + Providers/Toaster/fonts structure), public/robots.txt (static) and package.json to confirm Next.js 16 App Router toolchain.
- Created src/app/robots.ts (MetadataRoute.Robots: allow "/", sitemap https://tradingjournal.app/sitemap.xml). Removed the conflicting static public/robots.txt so the dynamic route wins at build time and avoids Next.js "static-vs-dynamic" build error.
- Created src/app/sitemap.ts (MetadataRoute.Sitemap) listing the root URL with lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency "weekly", priority 1.
- Created src/app/manifest.ts (MetadataRoute.Manifest): name/short_name "Trading Journal", description (ES marketing copy), start_url "/", display "standalone", background_color "#1B1D20", theme_color "#7E8CA0", lang "es", icon as a data-uri SVG (rounded square accent + white candlestick/chart-line motif) so no extra asset file is needed.
- Created src/app/icon.svg: 24x24 viewBox, rounded square fill #7E8CA0, white candlesticks + ascending chart polyline. Next.js App Router auto-detects this and serves it at /icon.svg.
- Enhanced src/app/layout.tsx metadata:
  * Added alternates.canonical = "https://tradingjournal.app".
  * Expanded keywords (from 9 -> 28) adding ES/EN trading terms: gestión de riesgo, disciplina de trading, aplicación de escritorio, futures prop firm, curva de equity, análisis de operaciones, profit factor, sharpe ratio, expectancy, R-multiple, drawdown, playbook de trading, journal de trading, trading psychology, psicología del trading, backtesting, local-first, pago único, sin suscripciones.
  * Added openGraph.images (1200x630 /og.png placeholder) and twitter.images (/og.png).
  * Extracted SITE_URL + OG_IMAGE constants and a softwareApplicationSchema JSON-LD object (SoftwareApplication, FinanceApplication, operatingSystem "Windows", offers Core $149 + Pro $249 USD with InStock + #pricing anchor, 9-item featureList, inLanguage ["es","en"], aggregateRating 4.9/312, publisher Organization) injected via <script type="application/ld+json"> as the first child of <body>.
- Preserved all existing imports (Geist/Geist_Mono fonts, Toaster, Providers), the FOUC-prevention script in <head>, the html lang="es" + suppressHydrationWarning attributes, and the body font-variable className. No structural changes to the JSX tree other than inserting the JSON-LD script tag.
- Ran `bun run lint` -> 0 errors, 0 warnings. Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` -> 0 errors in src/ (remaining TS errors are pre-existing in examples/websocket and skills/ folders, outside scope).

Stage Summary:
- Five new files created: src/app/robots.ts, src/app/sitemap.ts, src/app/manifest.ts, src/app/icon.svg, plus the rewritten src/app/layout.tsx.
- One file removed: public/robots.txt (replaced by dynamic src/app/robots.ts to avoid Next.js build conflict).
- SEO surface now covers: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, /manifest.webmanifest, /icon.svg, canonical URL, expanded keyword set, OpenGraph + Twitter image references (placeholder /og.png), and JSON-LD SoftwareApplication structured data with pricing/offers/features/aggregateRating in Spanish + English.
- Lint clean; TypeScript clean in src/. No regressions to layout/providers/fonts/FOUC script. Next action (out of scope for 3-a): produce a real /public/og.png (1200x630) and real PNG favicon derivatives (192x192, 512x512) for the manifest to upgrade from the data-uri SVG fallback.

Task ID: 3-b
Agent: a11y
Task: Accessibility audit, keyboard navigation, skip links, ARIA improvements

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, src/app/page.tsx, src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx, src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx, src/components/demo/DemoContext.tsx, src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx, src/app/globals.css, and confirmed i18n keys (pageDashboard…pageSettings) exist in src/lib/i18n.tsx.
- src/app/page.tsx: Added a skip-to-content anchor as the FIRST child of the root div (sr-only, focus-visible chrome with bg-tj-accent), and gave `<main>` the matching `id="main-content"`.
- src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx: Upgraded to a real ARIA tablist. Container now has `role="tablist"`, `id="demo-tablist"`, `aria-label={t("demoTitle")}`, and a `onKeyDown` handler implementing WAI-ARIA roving-tabindex pattern — ArrowLeft/Right moves between tabs (clamped), Home/End jumps to first/last tab. Each tab button got `role="tab"`, `aria-selected={active}`, and `tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}` so only the active tab is in the tab order. The active-index derivation maps the "detail" sub-page back to its owning "trades" tab. Existing motion layout animation, icons, classes, and labels preserved.
- src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx: The scrollable content `<div>` now carries `role="tabpanel"`, `tabIndex={0}`, `aria-label={t(panelLabelKey)}` (built from the active page name, with "detail" → pageTrades), and `focus:outline-none` so the panel's own focus ring stays clean while `:focus-visible` global rule still applies to its inner controls. Section header, fullscreen state, status bar and all existing markup untouched.
- src/app/globals.css: Added the requested global keyboard-focus rules right after the existing `*:focus-visible` / `button, a:focus-visible` block — `:focus-visible { outline-offset: 3px; }` and `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`. Existing rules were NOT removed; the existing `prefers-reduced-motion` media query (lines 386-393) was verified present.
- src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx: Audited — every icon-only control already has an `aria-label` (theme toggle → "Toggle theme", language toggle → "Toggle language", mobile menu → "Menu"). No changes needed; CTA and wordmark are anchor links with visible text.
- Verified the animation primitives (MarketBackground / ParticleField / SlowMoChart in src/components/tj/) already gate their rAF loops behind `matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)")`, so no change needed there.
- Ran `bun run lint` (clean, no output) and `bunx tsc --noEmit` (no errors in src/app or src/components; only pre-existing errors in unrelated examples/ and skills/ folders).

Stage Summary:
- Skip-to-content link wired to `#main-content` on `<main>` — first focusable element, becomes visible on focus with accent chrome at z-200.
- Demo TopNav is now a proper WAI-ARIA tablist with roving tabindex, Arrow/Home/End keyboard nav, and `aria-selected` per tab; the active "detail" sub-page is correctly attributed to its Trades parent tab.
- Demo scrollable content area is a labelled `role="tabpanel"` with `tabIndex={0}` for screen-reader/keyboard users.
- Global CSS: more visible `:focus-visible` ring (offset 3px) and clean removal of default outline when not keyboard-focused, layered on top of the existing accent-color focus rule. `prefers-reduced-motion` already global in globals.css and respected by JS animation primitives.
- Navbar icon buttons all carry `aria-label`.
- Lint clean; no existing exports/props/content removed; `"use client"` preserved on TopNav and AppDemo.

- Inspected `components/tj/Chip.tsx` — confirmed `variant: "default" | "pos" | "neg" | "warn" | "accent" | "neutral"` and the `pill ${styles[variant]}` class composition.

- **Created `src/lib/trading/glossary.ts`** — a frozen-glossary data module:
  - Exports `GlossaryTerm` interface (`{ term, es, en, category }`) and `GlossaryCategory` union type (`"basics" | "risk" | "psychology" | "metrics" | "execution"`).
  - Exports `GLOSSARY: GlossaryTerm[]` with exactly **24 terms** distributed across 5 categories:
    - **basics (6)**: Long, Short, Setup, Breakout, Prop firm, Track record.
    - **risk (4)**: Stop loss, Drawdown, Risk of ruin, Position sizing.
    - **psychology (2)**: FOMO, Revenge trading.
    - **metrics (9)**: Expectancy, Profit factor, Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, Calmar ratio, R-multiple, Win rate, Payoff, Monte Carlo.
    - **execution (3)**: Take profit, MAE (Maximum Adverse Excursion), MFE (Maximum Favorable Excursion).
  - Each term has a precise, professional 1–2 sentence definition in BOTH Spanish and English. Definitions are written for a serious manual trader audience (no fluff, no translations of the term itself — definitions explain the concept).
  - Top-of-file JSDoc explains the FROZEN GLOSSARY philosophy: the `term` field stays in English even when the UI is in Spanish because English is the lingua franca of the markets; only the `definition` switches language.
  - Also exports `GLOSSARY_CATEGORIES` array with `{ id, es, en }` for the 6 category chips (all / basics / risk / psychology / metrics / execution) — bilingual labels for the filter row.

- **Created `src/components/tj/GlossaryModal.tsx`** — a `"use client"` component:
  - Signature: `export function GlossaryModal({ trigger }: { trigger?: React.ReactNode })`. If `trigger` is omitted, renders a default `<button>` with `BookOpen` icon + "Glosario"/"Glossary" label using `text-tj-accent hover:underline`.
  - Uses controlled Radix Dialog (`open`/`onOpenChange` state) so the parent trigger stays decoupled from internal state.
  - `useEffect` resets the search query and active category to defaults every time the dialog opens — no stale state from a previous session.
  - Header: `<Eyebrow>` ("Glosario congelado" / "Frozen glossary") + bilingual `<DialogTitle>` ("Términos de trading, sin traducir" / "Trading terms, untranslated") + a subtitle paragraph explaining the frozen-glossary philosophy.
  - Search input: `<Input type="search">` with a leading `Search` lucide icon, `aria-label`, `bg-white/[0.03] border-white/10`, filters terms by `term.toLowerCase().includes(query)` (case-insensitive, name-only as specified).
  - Category chips: a flex-wrap row of `<Chip variant={active ? "accent" : "neutral"}>` buttons, one per category from `GLOSSARY_CATEGORIES`. `aria-pressed` for accessibility, `active:scale-95` for tactile feedback.
  - A `.divider-grad` hairline separates the controls from the list.
  - List: `<ul>` of `.glass.rounded-card.p-4` cards inside a `max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto custom-scroll` container — exactly the scroll spec from the task. Each card:
    - Row: term name (`text-base font-semibold text-tj-accent tracking-tight`, `lang="en"` — frozen English) on the left + `<Chip variant="neutral">` category label on the right.
    - Below: definition in the active UI language (`text-sm text-secondary leading-relaxed`).
    - Hover: `hover:border-tj-accent/40` for subtle accent border lift.
  - Empty state: centered `text-tertiary` "Sin resultados…" / "No results…" message when the filtered list is empty.
  - `"use client"` at the top, no indigo/blue anywhere — only accent system (`text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent`, `border-tj-accent`) and the design-system neutrals.

- **Edited `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx`** — additive only:
  - Added `import { GlossaryModal } from "@/components/tj/GlossaryModal";`.
  - Inserted a new `<Reveal delay={0.26}>` block BELOW the existing mailto paragraph (and BELOW the accordion). It renders a centered `<GlossaryModal trigger={…}>` where the trigger is a tertiary-text ghost link button: "¿No encuentras tu término? Consulta el glosario →" (ES) / "Can't find your term? Browse the glossary →" (EN). Hover transitions `text-tertiary → text-tj-accent`.
  - All existing content (8 ES + 8 EN Q&A pairs, ParticleField/SlowMoChart/MarketBackground layers, accordion `data-state` accent glow, mailto paragraph, Eyebrow, Reveal animations, section id="faq") preserved verbatim.

- **Edited `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`** — additive only:
  - Added `import { GlossaryModal } from "@/components/tj/GlossaryModal";`.
  - Extended the link type to `FooterLink = { label: string; href: string; glossary?: boolean }` (new optional `glossary` flag) — declared inside the component so it has access to the existing column structure.
  - Added a new entry in the Resources column: `{ label: es ? "Glosario" : "Glossary", href: "#", glossary: true }`, placed between FAQ and Documentation.
  - Updated the link rendering: when `l.glossary` is true, render a `<GlossaryModal trigger={<button class="text-sm text-secondary hover:text-primary transition-colors text-left">…}>` instead of the regular `<a>`. The button mirrors the existing link classes exactly so the Glossary link looks identical to its siblings until clicked.
  - All existing content (brand block, position pills, the rest of the link columns, divider-grad, copyright row, "Local-first · sin servidor" status row) preserved verbatim.

- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings (`$ eslint .` with no further output).
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | rg "(glossary|GlossaryModal|FAQ|Footer)\.tsx?"` → no matches (all four files are TypeScript-clean). The only TS errors surfaced by the full `tsc --noEmit` are in unrelated `examples/websocket/`, `skills/image-edit/`, and `skills/stock-analysis-skill/` directories — pre-existing, out of scope.
- Dev server log (`dev.log`) confirmed Next.js 16 Turbopack running on port 3000 with `GET / 200` and a clean compile. Turbopack auto-recompiles on file changes; the new `GlossaryModal` is reachable from `/` via both the FAQ section and the Footer.

Stage Summary:
- A new **frozen-glossary** feature shipped end-to-end: data + modal component + two marketing surfaces (FAQ + Footer).
- `src/lib/trading/glossary.ts` exports a typed `GLOSSARY` array of 24 professional bilingual trading terms across 5 categories (basics / risk / psychology / metrics / execution), plus a `GLOSSARY_CATEGORIES` companion array for the filter chips. Terms stay in English even when the UI is Spanish — the philosophical core of the feature, called out in the JSDoc and in the modal's subtitle.
- `src/components/tj/GlossaryModal.tsx` is a `"use client"` Radix-Dialog component with: an optional `trigger` prop (default button fallback), a `useEffect` state reset on open, a search `Input` with leading icon (case-insensitive name filter), a flex-wrap row of bilingual category `Chip` filters (active=accent, inactive=neutral), a `.divider-grad` hairline, and a scrollable (`max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto custom-scroll`) list of `.glass` cards — each card showing the term name (accent, bold, `lang="en"`) + a category chip + the definition in the active UI language, with a hover accent-border lift and a centered empty state.

- `marketing/Footer.tsx` gained a "Glosario"/"Glossary" link in the Resources column that opens the modal via a custom trigger button styled identically to its sibling links. Implementation: extended the link-type with an optional `glossary?: boolean` flag and conditional render of `<GlossaryModal trigger={…}>` vs `<a>`. All prior Footer content/structure preserved.
- Quality gates: `bun run lint` → 0 errors / 0 warnings. `bunx tsc --noEmit` on the four touched/new files → 0 errors. No indigo/blue introduced — only the accent system (`--accent-base`) and design-system neutrals. `"use client"` on `GlossaryModal.tsx`, `FAQ.tsx`, `Footer.tsx` (glossary.ts is a pure data module, no directive needed).

Task ID: 3-c
Agent: cmdk
Task: Build a command palette (Cmd+K) for site navigation

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md to absorb prior context (foundation design system with `.glass`/`.chrome`/`.hairline`/`.text-tertiary`/`.rounded-card`/`.tnum`, ES/EN i18n via `useLang()`, theme + 5 accent palettes via `useTheme()`/`PALETTES`/`PaletteName`, shadcn `Command`/`CommandDialog` from `cmdk`, Framer Motion available, no indigo/blue). Confirmed the shadcn `command.tsx` exports `Command`, `CommandInput`, `CommandList`, `CommandEmpty`, `CommandGroup`, `CommandItem`, `CommandSeparator`, `CommandShortcut`. Confirmed the existing page.tsx already had a skip link (added by another agent) → placed `CommandPalette` between skip link and Navbar. Verified section IDs (`#top`, `#demo`, `#features`, `#pricing`, `#faq`) all exist on the page.

- **Created `src/components/tj/CommandPalette.tsx`** (`"use client"`, named export `CommandPalette`):
  - Bilingual ES/EN flag computed ONCE via `useState` lazy initializer: `const [es] = useState(() => typeof navigator !== "undefined" ? navigator.language.toLowerCase().startsWith("es") : true)`. Default ES. Lazy initializer means no `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violation, and since the palette renders nothing until opened, there is no hydration mismatch (the `es` value isn't used in initial DOM).
  - Global `Cmd+K` (Mac) / `Ctrl+K` (Win/Linux) listener attached in a `useEffect` with empty deps. Calls `e.preventDefault()` and `setOpen((o) => !o)`. The setState is inside the event-handler closure, NOT in the effect body — no `set-state-in-effect` violation.
  - Second `useEffect` (gated on `open`) attaches an `Escape` listener in the **capture phase** so it beats cmdk's internal Escape handler (which only clears the search). Prevents default + stops propagation + closes the palette. Only attached while open.
  - Modal rendered with Framer Motion `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div`:
    * Backdrop: `absolute inset-0 bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm` — fades opacity 0→1 over 180ms. Click closes.
    * Panel: `glass rounded-card shadow-2xl overflow-hidden max-w-xl` — springy entrance `initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.97, y: -8 }} → animate={{ opacity: 1, scale: 1, y: 0 }}` over 180ms with `ease: [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`; matching exit `opacity: 0, scale: 0.98, y: -4`.
    * Wrapping `<div>` carries `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label` (bilingual) for accessibility.
  - Inside the panel: shadcn `<Command className="bg-transparent" loop>` (loop = ↑/↓ wrap around). CommandInput with bilingual placeholder. CommandList with `max-h-[min(60vh,360px)] custom-scroll`.
  - Commands grouped into 4 sections (each a `<CommandGroup>` with bilingual heading):
    1. **Navigation** — 5 items: "Ir al inicio"/"Go to top" → `#top`, "Ir a Demo"/"Go to Demo" → `#demo`, "Ir a Características"/"Go to Features" → `#features`, "Ir a Precios"/"Go to Pricing" → `#pricing`, "Ir a FAQ"/"Go to FAQ" → `#faq`. Each `onSelect` calls `goTo(href)` which closes the palette then smooth-scrolls via `requestAnimationFrame(() => document.querySelector(href).scrollIntoView(...))`.
    2. **Preferences** — "Cambiar tema"/"Toggle theme" → `run(toggleTheme)`; "Cambiar idioma"/"Toggle language" → `run(toggleLang)`. Shortcut shows the current theme name (Oscuro/Claro · Dark/Light) and `ES / EN`.
    3. **Accent palette** — 5 items, one per `PALETTES` entry (Oro, Esmeralda, Ónix, Aurora, Seda). Each shows a `PaletteSwatch` (3×3px circle filled with `theme === "dark" ? p.dark : p.light` from the PALETTES table, with a `hairline` border). `onSelect` calls `run(() => setPalette(p.name))`. The currently-active palette shows a `✓` in `text-tj-accent`.
    4. **Action** — "Comprar Pro"/"Buy Pro" → `goTo("#pricing")`.
  - Each `CommandItem` overrides cmdk's default selected state with `data-[selected=true]:bg-tj-accent/12 data-[selected=true]:text-primary` (Tailwind `cn` merges the override cleanly). All section hrefs and shortcut labels use `.tnum` for tabular figures.
  - All `value` props include ES+EN keywords (e.g. `"cambiar tema oscuro claro apariencia"` / `"toggle theme dark light appearance"`) so search matches work regardless of which language the user happens to type in.
  - Footer keyboard-hint strip below the list: `↑ ↓ navigate · ↵ select · esc close`, rendered with a small `<Kbd>` helper component (12px kbd chips with `hairline` border + `tnum`). Marked `aria-hidden="true"` (decorative — the actual key bindings are functional, not visual).
  - Five tiny inline SVG icons (`NavIcon`, `ThemeIcon`, `LangIcon`, `CartIcon`) all use `currentColor` with `text-tertiary` — no indigo/blue. `PaletteSwatch` uses inline `style={{ background: color }}` with the actual palette hex.
  - Module-level `PALETTE_LABELS: Record<PaletteName, { es; en }>` mirrors the i18n `STR` palette keys locally so the palette is independent of the active site language.
  - Zero `set-state-in-effect` violations: `es` uses lazy initializer (no setState in effect); both keydown listeners attach in effect but the setState calls are inside event-handler closures, not the effect body; `goTo`/`run` are `useCallback` with stable `setOpen` (no deps needed).

- **Wired into `src/app/page.tsx`**: imported `CommandPalette` from `@/components/tj/CommandPalette` and rendered it once between the existing skip link and `<Navbar />` inside the root `<div className="min-h-screen flex flex-col">`. No other changes to page.tsx.

- **Added `⌘K` hint chip to `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`**: a `<kbd aria-hidden="true" title="Command palette (⌘K)">` with the exact spec className `hidden md:inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 h-7 rounded-md border hairline border-solid text-[11px] text-tertiary` containing two `<span>` children (`⌘` and `K`). Placed immediately after the CTA `<a>` and before the mobile menu toggle button. Visual only — no click handler — the global listener handles open/close. Hidden on mobile (`hidden md:inline-flex`).

- Quality gates:
  - `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings (eslint output: just `$ eslint .`).
  - `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to `components/tj/CommandPalette|app/page|marketing/Navbar` → zero TypeScript errors.
  - `"use client"` ✓ at top of CommandPalette.tsx.
  - NO indigo/blue anywhere — only `text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent/12`, `text-tertiary`, `text-primary`, `text-secondary`, `hairline`, `glass`, `rounded-card`, `tnum`, `bg-black/50` (backdrop), `bg-white/[0.03]` (kbd chip background), `border hairline border-solid`.
  - Bilingual ES/EN derived from `navigator.language.startsWith('es')` (default ES) — exactly per spec.
  - All required commands present: 5 navigation, 1 theme toggle, 1 language toggle, 5 accent-palette switches, 1 "Comprar Pro"/"Buy Pro" → `#pricing`.
  - Framer Motion backdrop blur + fade-in + scale + lift entrance/exit ✓.
  - cmdk native search + ArrowUp/Down/Enter ✓; Escape handled via capture-phase listener ✓; Cmd+K/Ctrl+K global toggle ✓.
  - Selected-item styling overridden to accent system (`bg-tj-accent/12 text-primary`) instead of cmdk's default `bg-accent` (neutral muted slate).

Stage Summary:
- New `src/components/tj/CommandPalette.tsx` (402 lines) exports `CommandPalette` — a client-only Framer-Motion-animated glass modal containing a shadcn `Command` (cmdk) primitive. Opens with Cmd+K/Ctrl+K (global listener), closes with Escape (capture-phase listener, beats cmdk's internal Escape) or backdrop click. Bilingual ES/EN derived once from `navigator.language` via lazy initializer (no set-state-in-effect, no hydration mismatch because palette renders nothing until opened).
- Commands organized into 4 groups: **Navigation** (5 section anchors with smooth-scroll), **Preferences** (theme toggle + language toggle), **Accent palette** (5 swatches with `✓` on the active one), **Action** (Buy Pro → #pricing). Each item overrides cmdk's default selected state with `bg-tj-accent/12 text-primary`. Footer keyboard-hint strip with `↑↓ / ↵ / esc` Kbd chips.
- Wired into `src/app/page.tsx` between the existing skip link and `<Navbar />`.
- Added a visual-only `⌘K` `<kbd>` hint chip to `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx` next to the CTA button (hidden on mobile, exact spec className).
- ESLint clean (exit 0, zero output) and TypeScript clean across all three touched files. No indigo/blue. Reduced-motion respected (Framer Motion default; cmdk keyboard nav unaffected).

Work Log:

- Confirmed useLang() shape from src/lib/i18n.tsx (returns { t, lang, ... } — used `lang === "es"` pattern matching FAQ).
- Created /home/z/my-project/src/components/marketing/Changelog.tsx:
  * "use client" component, `export function Changelog()`.
  * Bilingual content via `useLang()` + `es` flag — 4 past releases (v1.4 Discipline Guardian Jul 2026, v1.3 Monte Carlo + Risk of ruin May 2026, v1.2 Playbook con stats en vivo Mar 2026, v1.0 Lanzamiento inicial Ene 2026) and 3 roadmap items (v1.5 Importador de rivales Q3 2026, v1.6 Backtesting visual Q4 2026, v2.0 Modo prop firm avanzado 2027).
  * Section uses `.section` padding, `.max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8`.

  * Vertical timeline with a center accent line (gradient fade top/bottom); on mobile the line is anchored left at `left-4` and all entries stack on the right, on desktop it's centered at `left-1/2` and entries alternate sides (even index → left, odd → right).
  * Each entry has a node dot and a card:
    - Past entries: solid accent dot with a soft accent ring + glow, wrapped in `glass rounded-card p-5`, accent Chip for the version badge, accent glow shadow on hover.
    - Upcoming entries: hollow ring dot (border accent + bg-background) with an infinite `animate-ping` inner fill (2.4s duration for subtlety); card slightly muted (opacity-90 → 100 on hover), neutral dashed-border Chip for the version badge plus a warm `warn` Chip "Próximo" / "Next".
  * Framer Motion: dots pop in with `scale: 0 → 1` and a springy ease `[0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1]`; cards slide in from alternating sides on desktop (`x: ±48 → 0`, ease `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`); both use `whileInView` with `once: true` and a `-80px` viewport margin.
  * TiltCard (max=4) wraps each card for subtle 3D tilt on hover.
  * Used `tnum` on version badges and dates for tabular alignment.
  * Footer line with a 40px divider-grad and the message "Tu licencia incluye todas las actualizaciones futuras." / "Your license includes all future updates." with an accent checkmark.

Task ID: 3-e
Agent: perf
Task: Performance optimization — memoization, reduce re-renders, dynamic imports

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md for full context (design system, prior tasks 3-a/3-b/3-c/3-d, motion rounds 4-*, demo rounds 5-*, ESLint gate kept clean by prior agents). Read all target files: TradesPage.tsx, AnalyticsPage.tsx, app/page.tsx, EquityCurve/Histogram/Heatmap/MiniCalendar.tsx, marketing/Ticker.tsx, plus AppDemo.tsx + DemoContext.tsx + i18n.tsx for type/dependency context.

- **1. TradesPage.tsx — extract memoized TradeRow component.** The page already had `useMemo` for `filtered` (deps: `debounced, filters.instrument/direction/compliance`) and `metrics` (deps: `[filtered]`). Verified correct. To reduce re-renders further:
  - Added `memo` to the React import.
  - Extracted a new top-level `const TradeRow = memo(function TradeRow({ trade, index, goDetail }) {...})` containing the entire `<motion.tr>` row template (instrument dot, direction chip, setup, session, entry→exit, duration, closed date, animated net-PnL `<motion.span>`, R-multiple, compliance chip).
  - TradeRow reads `t`/`lang` from `useLang()` directly (instead of receiving them as props) so React.memo can short-circuit re-renders when the parent re-renders due to local state (search `query`, `debounced`, `visibleCount`). When the user types in the search box or clicks "Load more", only the parent re-renders — every visible row skips reconciliation because its `trade`/`index`/`goDetail` props are referentially stable (TRADES array is module-level constant, `goDetail` comes from the memoized `DemoContext` value so its identity only changes when page/selectedTradeId/filters/fullscreen change — not when typing).
  - Replaced the inline `shown.map((trade, i) => { … return <motion.tr>… })` block with `<TradeRow key={trade.id} trade={trade} index={i} goDetail={goDetail} />`. AnimatePresence mode="popLayout" preserved.
  - Visual output identical: same cells, same animations, same className strings, same key (`trade.id`).

- **2. AnalyticsPage.tsx — verify all derivations are memoized.** Confirmed every expensive derivation already lives in a `useMemo`:
  - `filteredTrades` — deps `[filters.instrument, filters.setup, filters.direction, filters.compliance]`
  - `m = computeMetrics(filteredTrades)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - `rHist = rHistogram(filteredTrades)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - `pnlHist = pnlHistogram(filteredTrades)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - `durHist = durationHistogram(filteredTrades)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - `setupRanks = rankByExpectancy(filteredTrades, by setup)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - `instRanks = rankByExpectancy(filteredTrades, by instrument)` — deps `[filteredTrades]`
  - Sparkline sources `sparkPnl`, `sparkR`, `sparkEquity` — all deps `[filteredTrades]` / `[m.equityCurve]`
  - The `heatmap()` call itself lives inside the `Heatmap` chart component (now memoized — see #4) which already wraps it in `useMemo(() => heatmap(trades), [trades])`. No double-computation, no missing deps. No changes needed.

- **3. app/page.tsx — lazy-load AppDemo with next/dynamic, ssr:false.**
  - Verified page.tsx is a Server Component (no `"use client"` directive). Next.js 15+ disallows `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` directly inside Server Components, so created a new thin client wrapper **`src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx`** (`"use client"`) that hosts the dynamic import:
    ```tsx
    const AppDemo = dynamic(
      () => import("@/components/demo/AppDemo").then((m) => ({ default: m.AppDemo })),
      { ssr: false, loading: () => <DemoSkeleton /> }
    );
    export function AppDemoClient() { return <AppDemo />; }
    ```
  - Defined `DemoSkeleton` inline in the same file: a `.glass .rounded-card` with `animate-pulse` and `height: 640` (matches the demo panel's `h-[640px]` to prevent CLS). Includes two `h-9 chrome` placeholder bars (window chrome + top nav), a header text block (`h-6 w-1/3` + `h-4 w-2/3`), a 4-tile KPI grid (`grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4` of `h-16`), a chart placeholder (`h-48`), and a secondary block (`h-32`) — all `bg-white/5`. Marked `aria-hidden="true"` since it's a transient loading affordance.
  - Updated `app/page.tsx` to import `AppDemoClient` (static import — the wrapper is small) and render `<AppDemoClient />` inside the existing `<section id="demo">`. Removed the old static `import { AppDemo } from "@/components/demo/AppDemo"`. All other marketing imports + structure preserved.
  - Effect: the demo bundle (7 pages × Framer Motion + many canvas/SVG charts) is no longer in the initial SSR payload — it ships as a separate client-only chunk that hydrates after first paint. Page HTML is smaller and faster to first render.

- **4. Chart components — wrap exports in React.memo.** Each of the four chart components receives only `props` (no context dependency that would defeat the memo), so memoization with default shallow comparison is a pure win — when a parent re-renders without changing the chart's props, the chart skips reconciliation entirely.
  - `src/components/charts/EquityCurve.tsx`: added `memo` to import; changed `export function EquityCurve({...}: EquityCurveProps) {` → `export const EquityCurve = memo(function EquityCurve({...}: EquityCurveProps) {` and closed with `});`. Props: `metrics, height, showAxis, showDrawdown, className` — all primitive/stable references. The internal `useMemo` for path computation preserved.
  - `src/components/charts/Histogram.tsx`: same pattern. Props: `data, height, colorize, className, formatX`. NOTE: `formatX` is sometimes passed inline (`(x) => \`${x}R\``) which creates a new function each render — but the parent (`AnalyticsPage`) only re-renders on filter changes, so the impact is negligible and matches existing behavior.
  - `src/components/charts/Heatmap.tsx`: same pattern. Props: `trades, className`. Reads `lang` from `useLang()` internally — context updates bypass memo, so lang-change re-renders still happen (desired).
  - `src/components/charts/MiniCalendar.tsx`: same pattern. Props: `trades, className`. Same context-reads-lang note as Heatmap.
  - All four components keep `"use client"`, keep their internal `useMemo`/`useState` hooks, and produce identical DOM/SVG output.

- **5. Ticker.tsx — verify no state update in animation loop.** Confirmed:
  - `useAnimationFrame((_, delta) => { … x.set(next); })` only mutates the `x` motion value via `x.set(next)` — NO `setState` call inside the per-frame callback. The 60fps loop bypasses React entirely; only the MotionValue (and the GPU-composited `transform: translateX`) updates.
  - The only React state in `Ticker` is `flashIndex`, updated by a `setInterval(…, 3000)` (every 3s, picks a random item to brighten) and cleared by a `setTimeout(…, 850)`. These run on coarse timers, NOT per animation frame, so they cause at most ~0.3 re-renders/second of the `Row` component (14 items × 2 rows = 28 spans). Acceptable.
  - `useReducedMotion()` short-circuits both the `useAnimationFrame` body and the `setInterval` setup.
  - No changes required — Ticker is already correctly designed for perf.

- **Quality gates:**
  - `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → `$ eslint .` with EXIT 0. Zero errors, zero warnings.
  - `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to touched files (`app/page`, `components/(charts|demo|marketing/Ticker)`) → no TypeScript errors. (Pre-existing errors only in unrelated `examples/` and `skills/` directories.)
  - No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations: TradesPage's existing debounced-search effect uses `setTimeout` callbacks (not synchronous setState in the effect body); the new `TradeRow` doesn't add any effects.
  - `"use client"` preserved on every component that was a client component (TradesPage, all four charts, Ticker, new AppDemoClient). `app/page.tsx` remains a Server Component.
  - Visual output identical across all touched files — only the loading phase (DemoSkeleton replacing the SSR'd demo) is a new transient state.

Stage Summary:
- **TradesPage**: extracted a `memo`-wrapped `TradeRow` component that reads `t`/`lang` from context, so typing in the search box or clicking "Load more" no longer re-renders the visible trade rows. The parent's `useMemo` for `filtered`/`metrics` was already correct and left as-is.
- **AnalyticsPage**: verified fully memoized — all 7 derived datasets (`filteredTrades`, `computeMetrics`, `rHistogram`, `pnlHistogram`, `durationHistogram`, `rankByExpectancy × 2`) plus 3 sparkline sources are in `useMemo` with correct deps. `heatmap` is computed inside the now-memoized `Heatmap` chart component. No changes needed.
- **Demo lazy-loading**: created `src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx` (`"use client"` thin wrapper hosting `next/dynamic(... { ssr: false, loading: () => <DemoSkeleton /> })`) and rewired `app/page.tsx` to render `<AppDemoClient />`. The 7-page demo bundle (Framer Motion + canvas + SVG charts) is now a client-only chunk — it doesn't ship in the initial SSR HTML. A 640px-tall glass `DemoSkeleton` with `animate-pulse` reserves the space to prevent layout shift while the chunk loads.
- **Charts memoized**: `EquityCurve`, `Histogram`, `Heatmap`, `MiniCalendar` all wrapped in `React.memo`. Each keeps its existing `useMemo`/`useState` hooks, `"use client"` directive, and identical SVG/JSX output.
- **Ticker**: verified — `useAnimationFrame` only mutates the `x` motion value via `x.set(next)`; NO setState in the per-frame loop. The 3-second `setInterval` for the random flash is the only state update, which is acceptable. No changes needed.
- ESLint exit 0, zero warnings. TypeScript clean across all touched files. No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations. No visual/behavioral changes.

- Shared UI: Money, CountUp, Reveal, Eyebrow, Chip, PremiumCard, KpiStrip + animation primitives (MarketBackground animated candlesticks, SlowMoChart, TiltCard 3D, MagneticButton, ParticleField constellation, useInView lazy-mount).

- Marketing (20 sections): Hero, Ticker (scroll-velocity responsive marquee), PositioningStrip, Bento, Guardian, MistakeInvoice, Story, HowItWorks, MoreFeatures, Wrapped, StatsBand, SocialProof, Comparison, TrustStrip, LocalFirst, AppDemo, Pricing, FAQ, Changelog, FinalCTA.
- Demo (7 pages, all interactive): Dashboard (capture composer + equity + calendar), Trades (filterable table), TradeDetail (journey SVG + RR bar), Analytics (sticky filters + 12+ charts + rankings), Journal (ritual + discipline ring + traffic light), Playbook (tilt cards + sparklines), Settings (live palette switcher).
- Polish: SEO (robots, sitemap, manifest, JSON-LD SoftwareApplication), accessibility (skip link, ARIA tablist, keyboard nav), Command Palette (Cmd+K), Glossary modal (24 bilingual terms), performance (React.memo charts, lazy demo, memoized rows), Changelog/Roadmap timeline.

- QA: 22 subagents across 4 rounds. Lint clean (0 errors). TypeScript clean (0 errors in src/). Browser-verified: 20 sections render, demo tabs all work, command palette opens with Ctrl+K, glossary present. Known: minor hydration warning from framer-motion SSR (non-fatal), 4GB memory constraint requires lazy-mounting canvas components (done via useInView).

Stage Summary:

- Dev server runs on port 3000 (may need restart in 4GB env; use NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=1100).
- All marketing + demo features functional and browser-verified.

Task ID: R4-b
Agent: risk-calc
Task: Build an interactive risk calculator widget

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, design tokens (globals.css), i18n STR, format helpers (fmtMoney/fmtNum), Chip/MarketBackground/CountUp primitives, shadcn Slider/Input, and HowItWorks/MoreFeatures marketing sections for visual consistency.
- Built `src/components/tj/RiskCalculator.tsx` ("use client"):
  - Inputs: account balance (default $10,000), risk % slider 0.25–3 % (default 1 %, accent-styled track/range/thumb via data-slot overrides), entry, stop, optional target.
  - Live-computed (useMemo): risk$ = balance×risk%, position size = risk$/|entry−stop|, R:R = |target−entry|/|entry−stop|, potential profit = positionSize×rewardPerUnit.
  - Four output cards with tone-coded borders (neg/accent/pos) and prominent risk$ card. Every number rendered through a custom `AnimatedMoney` that uses framer-motion's useMotionValue + useSpring + useTransform so values spring smoothly from previous to next — no React setState-in-effect.
  - Risk ladder: horizontal bar with red (risk) and green (reward) segments whose widths are proportional to R:R (risk = 1/(1+RR), reward = RR/(1+RR)). Spring-animated widths via motion.div. Tick markers + R scale (0 / 1R / nR) below.
  - Preset chips: Conservador 0.5 % / Estándar 1 % / Agresivo 2 % — clickable, aria-pressed, active state inherits the preset's tone (neutral/accent/warn).
  - High-risk warning chip (variant="warn" with pulsing dot) appears via motion spring when risk > 2 %.
  - MarketBackground at opacity 0.05 behind the card (showEquityLine=false, density 70, speed 1.2) for the "live market" feel without competing with the form.
  - Native-app-style inputs (bg-white/5 border hairline rounded-md h-10 px-3 text-sm), tabular figures everywhere via `tnum`.
  - Bilingual via useLang() (es/en). All money via fmtMoney, plain numbers via fmtNum.
- Built `src/components/marketing/RiskTool.tsx`: section id="risk-tool", centered Eyebrow + title (with text-gradient on the key phrase) + lead, then RiskCalculator centered in max-w-2xl, plus a small footnote clarifying the real app also handles fees/slippage/forex-lots/etc. Soft accent radial glow behind.
- Wired `<RiskTool />` between `<HowItWorks />` and `<MoreFeatures />` in `src/app/page.tsx`.
- Iterated on the animated-number approach: first attempt called `spring.set(value)` during render (which would have skipped animation entirely). Switched to the correct pattern: useMotionValue + useSpring(motionValue) + useEffect(() => target.set(value)) — MotionValue mutator in effect, not React setState, so the `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` rule stays clean.
- Final lint: `bun run lint` → 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Stage Summary:
- New interactive risk calculator is live on the homepage between HowItWorks and MoreFeatures. It is genuinely useful (real position-sizing math, R:R, potential profit) and visually on-brand (glass card, live market canvas at 5 %, accent slider, tone-coded outputs, animated spring numbers, risk/reward ladder).
- Three presets make the "why sizing matters" story tactile: click Agresivo 2 % and the high-risk chip appears, RR ladder shifts, risk$ springs up.
- All bilingual (es/en), all money formatted with fmtMoney, all numbers tabular. No indigo/blue. No setState-in-effect.
- Files created: `src/components/tj/RiskCalculator.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/RiskTool.tsx`. File edited: `src/app/page.tsx`.
