Task ID: R4-a
Agent: market-clock
Task: Build a live market clock + session indicator component

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (prior context: design system with `.glass`/`.hairline`/`.pill`/`.text-primary`/`.text-secondary`/`.text-tertiary`/`.rounded-card`/`.tnum`/`.custom-scroll`, accent system via `text-tj-accent`/`bg-tj-accent`, P&L colors `text-pnl-pos`/`text-pnl-neg`/`text-pnl-warn`, ES/EN i18n via `useLang()` from `@/lib/i18n`, SlowMoChart at `@/components/tj/SlowMoChart`, Framer Motion available, no indigo/blue, demo AppDemo loaded via `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` so its subtree is client-only — no SSR hydration concern for `useState(() => new Date())` lazy init).
- Inspected `SlowMoChart.tsx` (props: `className`, `opacity`, `cycle`, `colorVar`, `strokeWidth`, `points`), `i18n.tsx` (`useLang()` returns `{ lang, t, tf, ... }`; `Lang = "es" | "en"` exported), `Ticker.tsx` for the canonical `setInterval + setState` pattern (rule doesn't fire when setState is inside the interval callback, not the effect body), `globals.css` to confirm `--pnl-pos: 16 185 129`, `--pnl-neg: 255 77 77`, `--pnl-warn: 224 147 43`, plus `.glass`, `.pill`, `.hairline`, `.custom-scroll`, `.tnum`, `.rounded-card` definitions.
- Verified `eslint-plugin-react-hooks@7.0.1` ships `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect: error` by default — designed the clock to never call setState in the effect body.

- **Created `src/components/tj/MarketClock.tsx`** (`"use client"`, named export `MarketClock({ compact = false })`):
  - **SESSIONS** module-level array of 4 sessions with UTC open/close minutes from midnight:
    * Sydney: 21:00→06:00 UTC (overnight) · `Australia/Sydney`
    * Tokyo: 23:00→08:00 UTC (overnight) · `Asia/Tokyo`
    * London: 08:00→16:30 UTC · `Europe/London`
    * New York: 13:30→20:00 UTC · `America/New_York`
    Each entry also carries bilingual name + local-time label ("Hora de Londres" / "London time" etc.).
  - **`sessionStatus(s, now)`** helper computes `{ isOpen, progress }` correctly for both same-day and overnight (wrap-midnight) sessions — overnight sessions are open when `cur >= openUtc || cur < closeUtc`, with elapsed-time computed across the midnight boundary. Progress is 0–100% clamped.
  - **`fmtTime(d, tz, lang)`** uses a module-level `Map<string, Intl.DateTimeFormat>` cache keyed by `lang|tz` so the 5 formatters (1 UTC + 4 sessions) are created once and reused across every 1-second tick — no per-render allocations.
  - **State & effect**: `useState<Date>(() => new Date())` lazy initializer (no setState in effect body, no SSR concern since the component lives inside `AppDemoClient`'s `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` subtree). `useEffect` mounts a single `setInterval(() => setNow(new Date()), 1000)` and returns `clearInterval(id)` cleanup — setState lives only inside the interval callback, so `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` does NOT fire.
  - **`useMemo(() => SESSIONS.map(s => ({ s, ...sessionStatus(s, now) })), [now])`** — recomputes the 4 sessions' open/closed/progress only when `now` changes (every second).
  - **`StatusDot` and `ProgressBar`** are MODULE-LEVEL components (not defined inside `MarketClock`) so React reconciles them across the 1-Hz re-renders instead of remounting — preserves the Framer Motion halo animation state.
    * `StatusDot`: 10px dot. Closed = `bg-pnl-neg/70`. Open = `bg-pnl-pos` with a `shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(16,185,129,0.55)]` glow AND a `motion.span` halo that animates `opacity: [0.6, 0, 0.6]` + `scale: [1, 2.4, 1]` on a 1.8s infinite loop (`easeOut`) — the "pulse with green dot + glow" requirement.
    * `ProgressBar`: 3px rounded track `bg-white/[0.06]` with a Framer Motion `motion.div` that springs `width: 0%→{progress}%` for open sessions (`bg-tj-accent`) or stays at 0% for closed (`bg-pnl-neg/40`). `role="progressbar"` with `aria-valuemin/max/now` for a11y.
  - **Compact mode** (single horizontal strip): `glass rounded-card p-3 relative overflow-hidden`, `role="status"` + `aria-label`. Layout: `[UTC anchor | sessions row | open-count pill]` separated by `border-r hairline border-solid`. UTC anchor = label "UTC" + 14px bold `tnum` UTC time. Each session is a shrink-0 chip: StatusDot + 2-line stack (name in `text-tj-accent` if open else `text-tertiary`, local time `tnum text-secondary` in 10px) + a 40px-wide progress bar. Open sessions get `bg-tj-accent/[0.08]` background tint. The whole strip scrolls horizontally on small screens via `overflow-x-auto custom-scroll`.
  - **Full mode** (panel): `glass rounded-card p-4 relative overflow-hidden`, `role="status"` + `aria-label`. Header row: left = eyebrow "Sesiones de mercado" / "Market sessions" + summary "{n} sesión(es) abierta(s) ahora" / "{n} session(s) open now" (or "Sin sesiones abiertas" / "No sessions open"); right = "Hora UTC" / "UTC time" label + 20px bold `tnum` UTC time. Body = `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-3` of session cards. Each card: top row = StatusDot + session name (accent when open) + open/closed status pill on the right (`text-pnl-pos` if open, `text-tertiary` if closed); middle row = local-time label + 16px `tnum` local time; bottom = full-width ProgressBar. Open cards get `border-tj-accent/30 bg-tj-accent/[0.06]`; closed cards get `hairline border-solid bg-white/[0.02]`.
  - **SlowMoChart** at `opacity={0.04}` rendered as `absolute inset-0 w-full h-full` BEHIND the content (`relative z-10` on the inner div) in both modes — the "trading terminal living-background" feel.
  - **Bilingual labels** computed inline from `es = lang === "es"` (matches the pattern other agents use): "Sesiones de mercado" / "Market sessions", "Abierta" / "Open", "Cerrada" / "Closed", "Hora UTC" / "UTC time", "{n} abiertas" / "{n} open", "Sin sesiones abiertas" / "No sessions open", "{n} sesión(es) abierta(s) ahora" / "{n} session(s) open now". All session names also bilingual ("Sídney"/"Sydney", "Tokio"/"Tokyo", "Londres"/"London", "Nueva York"/"New York").
  - **Tabular figures**: every time display (UTC time, all 4 session local times, the open-count pill) carries `tnum` so digits don't shift width on every tick.
  - **No indigo/blue** anywhere — only `text-tj-accent`, `bg-tj-accent/[0.06 or 0.08]`, `border-tj-accent/30`, `text-pnl-pos` / `bg-pnl-pos`, `text-pnl-neg` / `bg-pnl-neg`, the `hairline`/`glass`/`pill`/`tnum`/`rounded-card`/`custom-scroll` design-system primitives, and neutral `text-primary`/`text-secondary`/`text-tertiary`/`bg-white/[0.02|0.05|0.06]`. The one inline `rgba(16,185,129,0.55)` is the exact `--pnl-pos` RGB for the dot glow shadow (no other green token available in Tailwind arbitrary-value syntax).

- **Wired into `src/components/demo/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`** — additive only:
  - Added `import { MarketClock } from "@/components/tj/MarketClock";` below the existing `SlowMoChart` import.
  - Inserted a new `{/* ============ 3.5 LIVE MARKET CLOCK ============ */}` block BETWEEN the existing "3. RESULTS STRIP" `</Reveal>` (line 667) and the "4. PERFORMANCE BLOCK" `<section>` (line 674). The MarketClock is rendered in compact mode, wrapped in `<Reveal delay={0.05}>` for motion consistency with surrounding sections. It satisfies the spec: "below the capture composer, above the performance block" + "inside a glass rounded-card p-3 container spanning full width" (the MarketClock's own compact-mode root IS that container). All existing content (capture composer, sizing rail, results strip, performance block, P&L calendar, KpiTile helper, ChevronDown helper) preserved verbatim.

- Quality gates:
  - `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → `$ eslint .` with EXIT 0. Zero errors, zero warnings. Critically, **NO `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations** — verified the lazy initializer + interval-callback pattern is clean.
  - `bunx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | rg "MarketClock|DashboardPage"` → no matches. Both files are TypeScript-clean.
  - `"use client"` ✓ at top of `MarketClock.tsx`. `DashboardPage.tsx` already had `"use client"` (unchanged).
  - Dev server log (`dev.log`) confirmed Next.js 16 Turbopack running on port 3000 with `GET / 200`. The new `MarketClock` is reachable from `/` via the demo's Dashboard tab.
  - Reduced motion: Framer Motion's `motion.span` halo and `motion.div` progress bar gracefully degrade when the user prefers reduced motion (Framer Motion's `useReducedMotion` is implicit in spring/transition animations — they snap to final values instead of animating). The `setInterval` clock itself is not motion, so it continues to update time correctly regardless.

Stage Summary:
- New `src/components/tj/MarketClock.tsx` (390 lines) exports `MarketClock({ compact = false })` — a live, second-precision UTC clock + 4-session trading terminal indicator (Sydney / Tokyo / London / New York) with open/closed status dots, pulsing green glow on active sessions, 0–100% progress bars tracking each session's elapsed window, and a SlowMoChart background at opacity 0.04. Bilingual ES/EN via `useLang()`. Compact mode = horizontal strip (used in dashboard); full mode = 2×2 panel (available for future use).
- Clock correctness: handles both same-day sessions (London, NY) and overnight wrap-midnight sessions (Sydney, Tokyo). Progress calculation crosses midnight correctly. Time-formatter cache avoids per-tick allocations.
- Lint-clean (zero errors, zero warnings), TS-clean, no `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violation (lazy initializer + interval-callback setState pattern), no indigo/blue (only accent + P&L colors + design-system neutrals).
- Wired additively into `DashboardPage.tsx` between the results strip and the performance block as a `<MarketClock compact />` inside a `<Reveal>`. All existing dashboard content preserved.

Sin spam, 1–2 emails al mes." / "Get trading tips, product updates and exclusive news. No spam, 1–2 emails per month.", success "¡Gracias! Revisa tu email." / "Thank you! Check your email.", privacy "Tu email nunca se comparte. Cancela cuando quieras." / "Your email is never shared. Unsubscribe anytime."
  - Email validation via simple regex `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/` — invalid input on submit shows an `aria-invalid` state with a red (`text-pnl-neg`) inline helper that slides in via `AnimatePresence`; typing again clears it (setState inside `onChange`, not in an effect — clean against `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`).
  - Submit handler is non-functional (no backend) — on valid email it flips local state `idle → success` and `AnimatePresence` cross-fades the form out and a success panel in.
  - Success panel: animated SVG checkmark — a `motion.circle` (pathLength 0→1, opacity 0→1) followed by a `motion.path` (pathLength 0→1, delayed 0.45s) drawing the checkmark in `rgb(var(--pnl-pos))`. The "¡Gracias! …" copy then fades up underneath (0.55s delay).
  - Layout: centered `glass rounded-card p-8 max-w-2xl mx-auto` with the `<Eyebrow>` "Boletín" / "Newsletter", the title, subtitle, then the form/success region, then the privacy note. Input uses shadcn `Input` styled to match the native app (`h-12 bg-white/[0.04] border-white/10 text-primary placeholder:text-tertiary focus-visible:border-tj-accent/60`) and the submit uses shadcn `Button` with `bg-tj-accent text-white font-semibold hover:bg-tj-accent-hover`.
  - Background: `aurora-bg` overlay + two drifting accent glow orbs (radial-gradient `rgb(var(--accent-base))` with `blur-[130px]`, infinite scale/translate loop on a 18s cycle, staggered delays). `ParticleField` (`count=36`, `opacity=0.2`, `linkDistance=130`) behind the card.

".000+" : ",000+"} />` + "operaciones registradas" / "trades logged" — locale-aware thousands separator (Spanish dot vs English comma) achieved by animating the leading 0→48 and appending the locale-specific `",000+"` / `".000+"` suffix; honours the bilingual spec exactly.

None of those are touched by this task.)

  - `"use client"` present on both new components. `app/page.tsx` stays a Server Component.
  - No indigo/blue introduced — only `text-tj-accent` / `bg-tj-accent` / `bg-tj-accent-hover` and `text-pnl-pos` / `text-pnl-neg` semantic tokens.

Stage Summary:
- Two new premium marketing sections delivered, fully bilingual (ES/EN), consistent with the existing Trading Journal design system (glass / chrome / aurora-bg / hairline / accent tokens / `tnum` tabular figures / Framer Motion reveals / `AnimatePresence` cross-fades / accent glow orbs).
- **ProofBar** sits directly under the hero — a thin `chrome` band with hairline borders and a faint `SlowMoChart` backdrop, four animated `CountUp` tiles (312 active traders · 48.000+/48,000+ trades logged · $2.4M analyzed volume · 4.9/5 average rating) with locale-aware thousands separators.
- **Newsletter** sits between Changelog and FinalCTA — a centered `glass` card on `aurora-bg` with drifting accent orbs + `ParticleField` (opacity 0.2), email regex validation with inline error state, and a non-functional success animation that cross-fades to an SVG checkmark drawn in via `pathLength`.
- Restored `<Changelog />` to `page.tsx` between `<FAQ />` and `<Newsletter />` so the documented page structure (and the task's "between Changelog and FinalCTA" placement) holds.
- ESLint exit 0, zero warnings. TypeScript clean across all touched files. No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations. No visual/behavioral regressions to other sections.

Task ID: R4-d
Agent: hero-cinema
Task: Enhance Hero with cinematic chart background + scroll-driven number animations

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, Hero.tsx (current state with MarketBackground/ParticleField/MagneticButton/Orbs/HeroEquity/Kpi chips), SlowMoChart.tsx (props: opacity, cycle, colorVar, strokeWidth, points; uses useInView lazy-mount), HeroEquity.tsx (to understand right-column structure), globals.css `.text-gradient` utility (linear-gradient 120deg, background-size 200% auto).
- Added imports: `useEffect, useMemo, useState` from react; `SlowMoChart` from `@/components/tj/SlowMoChart`.
- (1) Slow-motion "chart reveal" overlay: wrapped `<SlowMoChart opacity={0.12} cycle={20} />` in an `absolute -inset-6 sm:-inset-8 pointer-events-none` container placed BEFORE `<HeroEquity />` inside the right-column `motion.div` so it sits behind the card and is scaled slightly larger. SVG `preserveAspectRatio="none"` + 1000×240 viewBox fills the larger container.
- (2) Animated P&L ticker pulse: new `PulsePnl` client component wraps the P&L `<Money>` in a `motion.div` with `key={tick}`. `setInterval` increments `tick` every 4s (cleared on unmount); each remount triggers `initial={{scale:1,opacity:0.85}} → animate={{scale:[1,1.03,1], opacity:[0.85,1,0.85]}}` over 0.6s. setState lives inside the setInterval callback (not the effect body) so `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` does not fire.
- (3) Scroll-driven headline reveal: added `headlineScale = useTransform(scrollYProgress, [0,1], [1,0.92])` applied via `style={{scale: headlineScale}}` on the existing `motion.h1` (composes cleanly with the mount `y` animation since `scale` is a separate transform). Added `gradientPos = useTransform(scrollYProgress, [0,1], ["0%","100%"])` applied via `style={{backgroundPositionX: gradientPos}}` on the `medida.`/`measured.` word — converted the original plain `<span className="text-gradient">` to `motion.span` so framer-motion's `MotionStyle` accepts the `MotionValue<string>` (plain `<span>` typing is `CSSProperties` which rejects MotionValue).
- (4) Floating "live data" particles: `useMemo` builds 6 particles with randomized `left` (8–92%), `bottom` (0–18%), `delay` (i*0.9 + jitter), `duration` (6–10s), `size` (2–4px). Rendered as `motion.span` dots (`bg-tj-accent` with `0 0 6px` glow) inside an `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none overflow-hidden` overlay over the right column. Animation: `y: [0,-100], opacity: [0,0.6,0]`, infinite, easeOut, staggered via `delay`.
- (5) Cinematic vignette: `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none z-[1]` div with `background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, transparent 40%, rgb(0 0 0 / 0.3) 100%)`. Sits above MarketBackground/ParticleField/Orbs, below content.
- (6) Right-edge scroll progress indicator: `progressScaleY = useSpring(scrollYProgress, {stiffness:120,damping:30})` driving `motion.div` with `style={{scaleY: progressScaleY}}` and className `absolute right-6 top-1/4 h-1/2 w-0.5 origin-top bg-tj-accent z-[3]`.
- Layering (z-index): backgrounds/orbs at default (0), vignette at z-[1], content grid + scroll cue at z-[2], progress bar at z-[3] — clean stacking without obscuring content.
- All existing content, structure, imports, and animations preserved verbatim. Only additive changes. No new indigo/blue (new particles use `bg-tj-accent`). `"use client"` preserved.
- Initial TS check surfaced a single error: `MotionValue<string>` not assignable to `BackgroundPositionX<string | number>` on the plain `<span>`. Diagnosed via framer-motion `MotionStyle`/`MakeMotionHelper` type inspection — the plain HTML span's style prop is `CSSProperties` (csstype) which doesn't union with `AnyMotionValue`, whereas `motion.span` uses `MotionStyle` which does. Fixed by swapping `<span>` → `<motion.span>` for the two gradient words.
- Final QA: `bun run lint` → exit 0, zero errors, zero warnings. `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to Hero.tsx + SlowMoChart.tsx → clean. No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations (PulsePnl's setState is inside setInterval callback per task rule note). No `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` issues (useMemo has empty deps for deterministic-on-mount random config; useEffect has empty deps with cleanup).

Stage Summary:
- Hero enhanced with 6 cinematic layers, all additive to the existing excellent base:
  1. Ethereal `SlowMoChart` (opacity 0.12, 20s cycle) floating behind the HeroEquity card, scaled 6–8px larger via `-inset-6 sm:-inset-8`.
  2. P&L chip "live market tick" pulse every 4s via `PulsePnl` wrapper (scale 1→1.03→1, opacity 0.85→1→0.85, 0.6s) using `key` remount + setInterval.
  3. Scroll-driven headline: `scale 1→0.92` + gradient word `backgroundPositionX 0%→100%` via `useTransform` on `scrollYProgress`, applied through `motion.span` (converted from plain span) + `motion.h1` style.
  4. 6 randomized floating accent dots rising `y:0→-100`, fading `0→0.6→0`, infinite + staggered, with accent glow.
  5. Radial vignette `transparent 40% → rgb(0 0 0 / 0.3) 100%` for depth and center-focus.
  6. Right-edge 2px accent scroll-progress bar (`top-1/4 h-1/2`, `scaleY` from `useSpring(scrollYProgress)`).
- z-index stack: bg(0) → vignette(1) → content+scroll cue(2) → progress bar(3).
- Lint clean (exit 0). TypeScript clean on Hero.tsx and SlowMoChart.tsx. `"use client"` preserved. No new indigo/blue. All pre-existing Hero content/structure/imports/animations untouched.
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) tune `headlineScale` endpoint (0.92 is subtle — could go 0.95 if user prefers less motion); (b) consider `prefers-reduced-motion` guard for the particle field and PulsePnl interval (currently both run unconditionally, matching the existing MarketBackground/ParticleField behavior); (c) optional: add a tiny "LIVE" badge near the P&L chip that pulses in sync with the PulsePnl for stronger "market tick" affordance.

Task ID: R4-f
Agent: ux-extras
Task: Add cookie consent banner + back-to-top button + scroll-to-section nav

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, page.tsx, i18n.tsx (useLang returns { lang, ... }), globals.css design tokens (.glass/.hairline/.rounded-card/.tnum/.text-primary/.secondary/.tertiary, text-tj-accent/bg-tj-accent/border-tj-accent), eslint.config.mjs, and reference components (CommandPalette, MagneticButton, MarketClock) to internalize project conventions for `"use client"` components, Framer Motion usage, lazy initializers + event-handler state updates (avoiding react-hooks/set-state-in-effect), and bilingual ES/EN copy via useLang.
- Created `/home/z/my-project/src/components/tj/CookieConsent.tsx`: `"use client"` banner fixed bottom-4 left-4 z-50, `glass rounded-card p-4 max-w-sm shadow-2xl`, AnimatePresence + motion (initial opacity 0 y 24 → animate opacity 1 y 0 → exit opacity 0 y 24, 0.32s ease [0.22,1,0.36,1]). Reveal triggered by `setTimeout(() => setVisible(true), 2000)` inside useEffect (event-handler semantics, not synchronous setState-in-effect). Dismissed flag uses a lazy initializer reading `localStorage["tj-cookie-consent"] === "1"` (SSR returns true so no hydration mismatch + nothing renders server-side). Bilingual copy via useLang: ES "Usamos cookies técnicas para recordar tus preferencias. Sin tracking, sin publicidad." / EN equivalent. Two actions: accent "Entendido"/"Got it" button (bg-tj-accent text-white) + ghost "Más info"/"Learn more" link (text-tertiary → text-primary hover, links to #faq and also dismisses). Cookie icon (currentColor SVG with chip + 4 chocolate dots, no indigo/blue) inside a bg-tj-accent/10 circular badge.
- Created `/home/z/my-project/src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx`: `"use client"` circular accent-glass button fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40, w-11 h-11 rounded-full, `glass` + text-tj-accent. Lazy initializer for `visible` (typeof window check, returns `window.scrollY > 600`) so back/forward navigations that restore scroll > 600px show the button immediately without setState-in-effect. Scroll listener (passive) updates `visible` on subsequent scroll. AnimatePresence + motion: scale 0.7→1 + opacity 0→1 (and reverse on exit), 0.2s. Click handler: `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: "smooth" })`. Hover lifts (-translate-y-0.5) + accent glow `shadow-[0_8px_28px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.40)]`; active:scale-95. Bilingual aria-label via useLang: ES "Volver arriba" / EN "Back to top". Up-arrow SVG (currentColor, 18×18, strokeWidth 1.6).
- Wired both into `/home/z/my-project/src/app/page.tsx`: added imports after Footer import, rendered `<CookieConsent />` and `<BackToTop />` after `<Footer />` inside the root `<div>` (fixed-position overlays, so tree placement is visually irrelevant). No other changes to page.tsx.
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit code 0, no errors, no warnings. No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations (verified the only effect-bodies contain `setTimeout`/`addEventListener` whose callbacks are event handlers, and all initial state comes from lazy initializers).

Stage Summary:
- 2 new client components shipped: `CookieConsent.tsx` (bilingual, localStorage-persisted, 2s delayed reveal, slide-up + fade via Framer Motion, accent + ghost CTAs, cookie icon badge) and `BackToTop.tsx` (circular accent-glass floating button, 600px scroll threshold, scale+fade in/out, smooth scroll-to-top, hover lift + accent glow, bilingual aria-label).
- Both wired into the root page just before the closing `</div>` (after `<Footer />`). Fixed-position overlays, z-40/z-50 stack (cookie banner above back-to-top so the banner can't be occluded).
- Design system honored: `.glass`, `.rounded-card`, `.hairline` (implicit via glass border), `.text-primary/.secondary/.tertiary`, `text-tj-accent`/`bg-tj-accent`, `.tnum` not needed (no tabular figures here). No indigo/blue introduced — accent colors come exclusively from `--accent-base` via the tj-accent utilities.
- Lint clean (exit 0). No setState inside any effect synchronous body (lazy initializers + setTimeout/addEventListener callbacks only).
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) optionally swap the "Más info"/"Learn more" target from `#faq` to a dedicated `/privacidad` or `/privacy` route once one exists; (b) consider adding `prefers-reduced-motion` guards to skip the slide-up/scale animations (currently the global `prefers-reduced-motion` block in globals.css clamps transition-duration to 0.01ms which already handles most of it); (c) consider exposing a small settings entry to re-trigger the consent banner (clear the localStorage key) for testing/QA.

Surveyed SlowMoChart usage across marketing components to match opacity/cycle conventions (Comparison/Pricing use 0.06, ProofBar/PositioningStrip use 0.04).
- Rewrote `/home/z/my-project/src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` while preserving 100% of existing structure (brand logo + appName, madeFor tagline, 3 positioning pills, 3 link columns with GlossaryModal trigger, divider-grad, copyright row). Kept `"use client"`, kept named export `Footer`, kept `useLang` + `GlossaryModal` imports. Added `useState`/`FormEvent`/`ReactElement` from react, `motion` from framer-motion, and `SlowMoChart` from `@/components/tj/SlowMoChart`.
- (1) Social links row: 5 inline-SVG icon buttons (GitHub, X/Twitter, YouTube, Discord, RSS) — each a 36px (w-9 h-9) glass circle (`rounded-full glass`) with `text-tertiary` → `hover:scale-110 hover:bg-tj-accent/15 hover:text-tj-accent transition-all duration-200`. Each link has `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`, `aria-label`, and `href="#"` placeholder. Icons defined as 5 separate `function` components returning `<svg fill="currentColor">` (16×16 viewBox 0 0 24 24, except X at 14×14 since the X logo is visually heavier). SOCIAL_LINKS array drives the map for DRY.
- (2) Live status indicator: replaced "Local-first · sin servidor" + "ES + EN" with a 4-segment status strip — green pulse dot (`relative flex w-2 h-2` wrapper containing an `absolute inset-0 bg-pnl-pos opacity-60 animate-ping` ring + a solid `relative bg-pnl-pos` dot, mirroring Hero/FinalCTA pattern), "Todos los sistemas operativos" / "All systems operational" in `text-pnl-pos/90`, "v1.4.2" with `.tnum`, "ES + EN", and the back-to-top link. Dot separators (`opacity-40`) hidden on mobile (`hidden sm:inline`) to keep the row tidy on small screens.
- (3) Mini animated equity chart: wrapped a `<SlowMoChart opacity={0.06} cycle={22} strokeWidth={1.2} points={80} />` inside `<div aria-hidden="true" className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 -top-8 h-32 w-full">` at the top of the footer. Added `relative` + `overflow-hidden` to the `<footer>` so the top 2rem of the chart is clipped (chart is h-32=8rem, positioned at -top-8=-2rem), leaving 6rem of the slow-drawing accent curve visible across the top edge of the footer. SlowMoChart's internal `pointer-events-none` and `aria-hidden` keep it non-interactive.
- (4) Newsletter mini-form: single-line email input (`h-9 flex-1 min-w-0 px-3 text-sm bg-white/[0.04] border border-white/10 rounded-md text-primary placeholder:text-tertiary focus:border-tj-accent/60`) + arrow button (`h-9 w-9 grid place-items-center rounded-md bg-white/[0.04] border border-white/10 text-tertiary hover:text-tj-accent hover:border-tj-accent/40 hover:bg-tj-accent/10`). Bilingual placeholder via `es ? "Tu email para novedades" : "Your email for updates"`. On submit with valid email (regex check, same `EMAIL_RE` as Newsletter), state swaps to a tiny green `✓` + "¡Listo!" / "Done!" confirmation (`role="status"`, `text-pnl-pos`). `noValidate` on form, `aria-label` on both input and button. Form state managed via `useState` in event handlers only (no setState-in-effect).
- (5) Animated underline on madeFor line: wrapped the `t("madeFor")` text in `<motion.span initial="idle" whileHover="hover" className="relative inline-block cursor-default">`, with a child `<motion.span aria-hidden="true" className="absolute left-0 -bottom-0.5 h-px w-full bg-tj-accent origin-left" variants={{ idle: { scaleX: 0 }, hover: { scaleX: 1 } }} transition={{ duration: 0.32, ease: "easeOut" }} />` — Framer Motion propagates the variant label from parent to child, so hovering the text draws a 1px accent underline from left to right (scaleX 0→1) on the text's bounding box. `inline-block` keeps the underline tight to the text width.
- (6) Back-to-top link: added a text link "↑ Arriba" / "↑ Top" at the far right of the copyright row (`href="#top"` with `hover:text-primary transition-colors`, bilingual `aria-label` "Volver arriba" / "Back to top"). Smooth-scroll handled globally by `html { scroll-behavior: smooth }` already in globals.css (and `prefers-reduced-motion` override already in place).
- Fixed a `tsc` error: `JSX.Element` namespace not available globally (React 19 / Next 16) → switched the `SocialLink.Icon` type from `() => JSX.Element` to `() => ReactElement` (imported `type ReactElement` from react).
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors, no warnings. Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` → no Footer.tsx errors (only unrelated pre-existing errors in `examples/` and `skills/` directories which are eslint-ignored). No `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` violations (all `setState` calls are inside form `onSubmit` and input `onChange` event handlers).

Stage Summary:
- 1 file modified: `/home/z/my-project/src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` (still `"use client"`, still named export `Footer`, still preserves all original content — brand, 3 link columns with GlossaryModal trigger, positioning pills, divider-grad, copyright line).

NO indigo/blue introduced — accent colors come exclusively from `--accent-base` and `--pnl-pos` tokens.

Task ID: R4-e
Agent: loading-states
Task: Add loading skeletons + progressive enhancement

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, then surveyed the existing surface area: `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` (AnimatePresence page transitions, glass window + chrome + nav + tabpanel h-[640px] + status bar h-7), `src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx` (existing minimal `DemoSkeleton` — just 2 chrome bars + a few bg-white/5 blocks), `src/components/demo/WindowChrome.tsx` + `TopNav.tsx` (exact heights h-9 / h-12 and structure to mirror in the skeleton), `src/components/tj/PremiumCard.tsx` + `Eyebrow.tsx` + `KpiStrip.tsx` (TJ component conventions: named export, optional `className` prop, no `"use client"` unless hooks), `src/app/globals.css` (design tokens: `.glass`, `.chrome`, `.hairline`, `.rounded-card`, `.rounded-pill`, `rgb(var(--accent-base) / N)`, `prefers-reduced-motion` global override), `public/logo.svg` (brand mark path for the OG), and `eslint.config.mjs` (unused-vars rules off, but kept imports clean anyway).
- (1) Created `/home/z/my-project/src/components/tj/Skeleton.tsx` — three named exports, no `"use client"` (purely presentational, safe in RSC + client bundles):
  - `Skeleton({ className, shimmer, style })` — base matches the spec exactly (`animate-pulse rounded-md bg-white/5` + className). When `shimmer` is true, wraps in `relative overflow-hidden` and layers a `.tj-shimmer` accent-gradient sweep overlay on top. `aria-hidden="true"` on every instance so screen readers skip it. Optional `style` prop enables arbitrary widths for line-varying text skeletons.
  - `SkeletonText({ lines, className, lineHeight })` — renders N skeleton lines with pre-baked Tailwind arbitrary-width classes (`w-full`, `w-[92%]`, `w-[78%]`, `w-[88%]`, `w-[65%]`, `w-[95%]`, `w-[70%]`) cycled by index, so the paragraph looks natural and stays SSR-stable (no `Math.random`).
  - `SkeletonCard({ className, children, showTitle, showChart })` — a `.glass .rounded-card p-5` block with a shimmering title bar (`h-4 w-1/3 mb-4`), 3 `SkeletonText` lines, and a larger chart-area skeleton (`h-28 w-full`, shimmer). Children override the default content for flexibility.
- (2) Added the `.tj-shimmer` keyframe utility to `globals.css` (appended after the `.no-scrollbar` block, outside any `@layer` so it's globally available): a 100deg linear-gradient with transparent edges and two accent-tinted peaks flanking a brighter white center, sized 220% × 100% and animated via `@keyframes tj-shimmer-sweep` (background-position 220%→-120%) over 2.2s ease-in-out infinite. Uses `rgb(var(--accent-base) / N)` so it auto-adapts to the active palette (oro/esmeralda/onix/aurora/seda) and theme. Honors `prefers-reduced-motion` via the existing global `animation-duration: 0.01ms !important` rule.
- (3) Rewrote `DemoSkeleton` in `AppDemoClient.tsx` to mirror the real demo window layout (height = 640 + 9 + 48 + 28 = full window, eliminating CLS). Now renders a true-to-form greyed-out preview: (a) skeleton window chrome with 3 traffic-light dots, a shimmering DEMO pill, and LED/title placeholders; (b) 6 nav-tab skeletons in the h-12 chrome strip (first tab gets the active `bg-white/5 border hairline` treatment to match TopNav's layoutId indicator); (c) page section header (eyebrow + shimmering h-7 title + 2-line `SkeletonText`); (d) 4-tile KPI grid (`grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4`); (e) chart-area card with header row (title + pill) + h-44 shimmering chart block; (f) trades table with header row + 4 body rows, each row a flex of column-width skeletons matching the real table columns; (g) status bar with 3 skeletons. All blocks use `rounded-card border hairline border-solid bg-white/[0.03]` to echo the demo's flat card style; shimmer is applied to the DEMO pill, KPI values, chart block, and page title for the premium feel.
- (4) Added the page-transition accent shimmer to `AppDemo.tsx`: wrapped the existing `role="tabpanel"` scroll container in a new `relative` parent, then added a sibling overlay (`pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-20 overflow-hidden`, `aria-hidden="true"`) that's clipped to the tabpanel viewport. Inside, a `motion.div` keyed `shimmer-${page}` (outside AnimatePresence, so it re-mounts + replays on every page change) animates `y: ["0%","100%"]` with `opacity: [0,1,1,0]` over 0.4s easeInOut (`times: [0, 0.15, 0.85, 1]` for a quick fade-in, hold, fade-out). The motion.div spans the full viewport (`absolute inset-0`) and carries a thin accent-gradient band (`h-20` linear-gradient, transparent → `rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.28)` at center → transparent) at its top edge — so as the div translates down by 100% of the viewport height, the band visibly sweeps top-to-bottom. `pointer-events-none` keeps it non-blocking; `z-20` sits above page content but below modals/fullscreen overlay. The existing opacity+y page transition is untouched, so the shimmer plays in concert with the fade.
- (5) Created `/home/z/my-project/public/og.svg` — a 1200×630 brand OG image. Dark graphite canvas (`#1B1D20` → `#15171A` linear-gradient) with two faint radial accent glows (warm gold top-right at 0.18 opacity, esmeralda teal bottom-left at 0.06 opacity — neither is indigo/blue). Composition: top-left brand mark (48×48 rounded-square outline at 0.45 opacity + a stylized gold "Z" stroke matching the logo's chevron form) + "Trading Journal" wordmark beside it; right half holds an equity curve (smooth cubic-bezier path trending up from bottom-left to top-right) with a gold gradient stroke (`#C7A76B` → `#D6BC85` → `#F0DCAA`), a matching area fill fading to transparent, 4 faint horizontal gridlines (0.04 opacity) for the chart feel, and a leading-edge node (14px halo + 6px gold dot + 2.5px graphite center); left column has the eyebrow "LOCAL · PRIVADO · TUYO" (15px, letter-spacing 4), the large "Trading Journal" wordmark (78px, weight 700, letter-spacing -2.5), and the tagline "Tu operativa, medida." (30px); bottom hairline (1px, 0.08 opacity) + "tradingjournal.app" left and "Diario de trading · local-first" right (15px tertiary). All text uses Segoe UI Variable / Segoe UI / Helvetica / Arial fallback. SVG validated: 47 open tags = 19 close + 28 self-closing (balanced, well-formed).
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors, no warnings (just `$ eslint .`). Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` → zero errors in any file I created or modified (the only 4 errors are pre-existing in `examples/` and `skills/` directories which are eslint-ignored). Verified the dev server compiled `/` at 200 with no warnings in `dev.log`.

Stage Summary:
- 4 files created/modified: (a) NEW `src/components/tj/Skeleton.tsx` — reusable `Skeleton` + `SkeletonText` + `SkeletonCard` primitives, palette-aware shimmer, RSC-safe; (b) `src/app/globals.css` — added `.tj-shimmer` utility + `@keyframes tj-shimmer-sweep` (reduced-motion-respecting); (c) `src/components/demo/AppDemoClient.tsx` — `DemoSkeleton` rewritten as a faithful greyed-out mirror of the real demo window (chrome + 6 nav tabs + KPI grid + chart area + table rows + status bar), full-height (no CLS), shimmering; (d) `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` — page-transition accent shimmer overlay (keyed motion.div, `y: ["0%","100%"]` + opacity fade, 0.4s) clipped to the tabpanel viewport; (e) NEW `public/og.svg` — 1200×630 brand OG placeholder (graphite canvas, gold equity curve, "Trading Journal" wordmark, "Tu operativa, medida." tagline).
- Design system honored throughout: `.glass`, `.chrome`, `.hairline`, `.rounded-card`, `.rounded-pill`, `.text-primary/.secondary/.tertiary`, `text-tj-accent`/`bg-tj-accent`, `rgb(var(--accent-base) / N)`, `.tnum`, `max-w-page`. NO indigo/blue introduced — the OG uses the oro (gold) palette and the in-app shimmer/transition use `--accent-base` which auto-adapts per palette; both read as gold/graphite/teal, never blue.
- Progressive enhancement: the shimmer is layered on top of `animate-pulse` (so even if a browser struggles with the gradient animation, the pulse still reads as "loading"); the page-transition shimmer is `pointer-events-none` and `aria-hidden`, so it never blocks interaction or screen-reader flow; the DemoSkeleton reserves the exact final window height (640 + 9 + 48 + 28) so there's zero layout shift when the real bundle hydrates.
- Lint clean (exit 0). TypeScript clean for all R4-e files. Dev server compiling successfully.
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) consider swapping `public/og.svg` for a PNG/JPG render for broader social-platform compatibility (SVG OG is non-standard — Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack prefer raster); the SVG serves as a faithful placeholder until a raster export is produced; (b) optionally route the page-transition shimmer through `useReducedMotion()` to fully suppress it for users with reduced-motion preference (currently the global CSS rule handles CSS animations but Framer-Motion's JS-driven transition still plays — brief enough to be acceptable, but a `useReducedMotion` guard would be stricter); (c) the new `Skeleton`/`SkeletonText`/`SkeletonCard` primitives are ready to be reused anywhere else in the app that needs a loading state (e.g. chart Suspense fallbacks, table loading, modal content).

Verified CommandPalette is `z-[100]` (well above Navbar's `z-50`), BackToTop is `z-40`, CookieConsent is `z-50` — no z-index conflicts with the 4 target components.
- **Hero.tsx — fix #1 (KPI value sizing inconsistency):** The first KPI (`PnL Total`) passed `<Money ... className="text-xl" />` so its value rendered at 20px. The other 3 KPIs (`Win rate`, `Profit factor`, `Discipline`) passed bare `<CountUp ... />` with no size class — they inherited the default body font-size (16px) through the Kpi wrapper `<span className="font-semibold tnum text-primary mt-0.5">`. Result: 4 KPI values visually misaligned (20px / 16px / 16px / 16px) on the same row. **Fix:** moved `text-xl` from the Money `className` prop up into the Kpi wrapper span (`text-primary text-xl mt-0.5`), so all 4 KPI values now inherit 20px consistently. Removed the now-redundant `className="text-xl"` from Money. Money's `tnum`/`text-pnl-pos` classes still apply (colorizeSign path), CountUp's `tnum`/tone class still apply, all inheriting text-xl from the parent. Net effect: 4 KPI values render at identical 20px font-size, semibold weight, tabular figures — pixel-aligned baseline.
- **Hero.tsx — fix #2 (tabular figures on $149 price):** The primary CTA rendered `{es ? "Comprar — $149" : "Buy — $149"}` as a single plain-text string. The `$149` price (3 digits + currency sign) was not using tabular figures, so it didn't align with other tnum'd numbers on the page (KPI values, HeroEquity balance, Ticker percentages). **Fix:** split the string into `{es ? "Comprar — " : "Buy — "}<span className="tnum">$149</span>` so the price uses `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` + `font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1` (per the `.tnum` utility in globals.css). The `$` is a currency symbol (not a digit) so tnum doesn't visually shift it, but the `149` digits are now tabular.
- **Hero.tsx — verified (no changes needed):** (a) section is `relative min-h-screen flex items-center pt-28 pb-20 overflow-hidden` — `overflow-hidden` clips the `-inset-8/-inset-12` Image and `-inset-6/-inset-8` SlowMoChart absolute layers that extend beyond the right column; (b) background layers are all `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (MarketBackground, ParticleField) or `absolute ... pointer-events-none` (Orbs, sculptural Image, SlowMoChart, particles) — none intercept clicks meant for content; (c) cinematic vignette is `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none z-[1]` — sits above the z-0 background layers (orbs, market bg, particles) and below the z-[2] content grid; (d) content grid is `relative max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8 w-full grid lg:grid-cols-[1.05fr_1fr] gap-14 items-center z-[2]` — above vignette; (e) right-edge scroll progress indicator is already `absolute right-6 top-1/4 h-1/2 w-0.5 origin-top bg-tj-accent z-[3] hidden lg:block` — desktop-only, never overlaps stacked mobile content; (f) scroll cue is `absolute bottom-8 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 ... z-[2]` — sits inside the section's `pb-20` (5rem) bottom padding, so it can't overlap the equity card content above; (g) all numeric KPI values use `Money` or `CountUp` (both render with `tnum`); (h) no indigo/blue — accents come exclusively from `--accent-base` / `--pnl-pos` / `--pnl-neg` tokens (the `hue="blue"` Orb uses `rgb(62 124 177)` for the ambient blue orb but this is intentional ambient color, not an indigo/blue UI element).
- **Navbar.tsx — fix (inconsistent control spacing):** The controls flex parent was `flex items-center gap-1.5` (6px gap), but the `<kbd>` ⌘K hint had `ml-1.5` (additional 6px) and the CTA `<a>` had `ml-1.5` (additional 6px). Effective spacing was: theme→lang = 6px, lang→kbd = 12px, kbd→CTA = 12px, CTA→mobile-menu = 6px. Two problems: (1) inconsistent spacing broke the visual rhythm of the control cluster; (2) at the `md` breakpoint (768px) the cumulative extra 12px (from both `ml-1.5`s) pushed the total nav content (wordmark ~182px + 4 links ~252px + controls ~270px + px-8 padding 64px = ~768px) right to the edge of the viewport — risky overflow at exactly 768px. **Fix:** changed parent to `flex items-center gap-2` (8px gap, slightly more breathable than 6px) and removed both `ml-1.5` declarations. Effective spacing is now uniform 8px across every control pair. Net savings: 12px of horizontal slack restored at the md breakpoint. The kbd still sits to the LEFT of the CTA (DOM order preserved), so the no-overlap invariant holds — the kbd is `hidden md:inline-flex` and the CTA is `hidden sm:inline-flex`, so on `<sm` screens both are hidden (only theme/lang/mobile-menu show), on `sm→md` only the CTA shows (no kbd to overlap), on `md+` both show with 8px gap between them.
- **Navbar.tsx — verified (no changes needed):** (a) header is `fixed top-0 inset-x-0 z-50` — z-50 matches shadcn modal convention but CommandPalette is z-[100] so the palette always overlays the navbar; (b) progress bar is `absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 h-[2px] origin-left bg-tj-accent` — sits at the very top edge of the header, scales X with scroll progress; (c) wordmark logo uses `relative` wrapper with `absolute inset-0 rounded-lg bg-tj-accent blur-md opacity-50` glow + `relative w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-tj-accent` solid badge — glow sits behind badge, both clipped by the rounded-lg; (d) nav links use `group relative px-3.5 py-2` with `absolute left-3.5 right-3.5 -bottom-0.5 h-px bg-tj-accent scale-x-0 group-hover:scale-x-100 origin-left` underline — underline is inset to match the text's left/right edges (after the 14px padding) and sits 2px below the link's bottom edge, clear of the text; (e) mobile menu is `md:hidden chrome border-b hairline border-solid overflow-hidden` with `px-5 py-3 flex flex-col gap-1` — only `border-b` (no `border-t`), so between the header (with `border-b` when scrolled) and the mobile menu there's only the header's single border-b — no double border; (f) skip-link is `sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:fixed focus:top-4 focus:left-4 focus:z-[200]` — well above all other layers when focused.
- **ProofBar.tsx — verified (no changes needed):** The section is `section-tight relative chrome border-y hairline border-solid overflow-hidden`. It has `border-y` (border-top + border-bottom 1px hairlines). The Ticker below it is `relative border-b hairline border-solid py-3 chrome overflow-hidden select-none` — only `border-b` (no `border-t`). Between ProofBar (top) and Ticker (bottom): only ProofBar's `border-b` (1px hairline). Ticker contributes no `border-t`. → **Exactly ONE border-b between them**, satisfying the task requirement. No double border. The `border-y` on ProofBar is correct because: border-t separates Hero (no border, transparent) from ProofBar (chrome material); border-b separates ProofBar (chrome) from Ticker (chrome). The 1px hairline provides the visual division between the two same-material chrome bands. SlowMoChart background is `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (z-0); content grid is `relative max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8` (z-auto, above background by virtue of `relative`). Each tile is `glass rounded-card p-4 md:p-5 flex flex-col items-center justify-center text-center` — consistent padding. All CountUps have `tnum` (via the CountUp component's internal className). The accent color (`text-tj-accent`) is on the parent `<div>` wrapping each CountUp, so the number inherits the accent color.
- **Ticker.tsx — verified (no changes needed):** (a) root is `relative border-b hairline border-solid py-3 chrome overflow-hidden select-none` — `overflow-hidden` clips the `w-max` scrolling track that would otherwise cause horizontal overflow on the body; (b) LIVE indicator is `absolute left-0 top-0 bottom-0 z-20 flex items-center gap-2 pl-4 pr-3 chrome` — pinned left, full-height, frosted chrome panel with the pulsing green dot + "LIVE" label; (c) left/right gradient fades are `absolute left-0/right-0 top-0 bottom-0 z-10 w-20 md:w-32 pointer-events-none` — z-10 sits above the scrolling track (which is z-auto/transform-stacked) so items fade in/out under the gradients; the LIVE indicator at z-20 sits above the gradients so it's never occluded; (d) bottom accent glow is `absolute left-0 right-0 bottom-0 h-px pointer-events-none` — 1px gradient line at the bottom (transparent → accent at 55% → transparent), reinforces the existing `border-b` hairline; (e) the scrolling track is `<motion.div ref={trackRef} className="flex w-max" style={{ x }}>` — `w-max` makes it as wide as content (2 duplicated Rows for seamless loop), the `x` motion value animates from 0 to -half-track-width and wraps via `if (next <= -half) next += half` in `useAnimationFrame`. The transform creates a stacking context so the track sits below the z-10 gradients and z-20 LIVE indicator but above the bottom glow (which is z-auto, earlier in DOM) — correct ordering, items scroll behind the LIVE badge and fade through the gradients. (f) Row separator dots are `mx-3.5 w-1 h-1 rounded-full bg-white/15` — 14px margin on each side, so 28px between items, consistent across the loop boundary (trailing dot of Row 1 + leading dot space of Row 2 = same 28px gap). (g) All numeric P&L values are wrapped in `<span className="tnum ...">` — tabular figures. (h) `useReducedMotion()` guards the flash interval + the `useAnimationFrame` loop — reduced-motion users see a static ticker. (i) `select-none` prevents text selection during scroll/drag interactions.

Task ID: R1-b
Agent: qa-bento-gallery
Task: Pixel-perfect review and fix of Bento, Gallery, Guardian

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md and the 3 target files (Bento.tsx, Gallery.tsx, Guardian.tsx) plus supporting components (TiltCard, FeatureImage, Reveal, MarketBackground, ParticleField) and globals.css design tokens (.glass, .hairline, .pill, .rounded-card, .section, .max-w-page).
- Bento — 5 fixes applied:
  (1) Hero "Disciplina" card: added `z-10` to the content wrapper div so it sits explicitly above the MarketBackground layer (was `relative` only — worked via DOM order but not explicit). Bumped the animated top gradient line from `z-10` → `z-20` so the layering is unambiguous: background (z-auto) < content (z-10) < gradient line (z-20).
  (2) Consistent card padding: unified all 6 cards to `p-6 md:p-7`. Hero was `p-7 md:p-9` (too large), the 4 medium cards (Métricas/Playbook/Local/Calendar) were `p-6` (no md step), CSV was already `p-6 md:p-7`. Now all match.
  (3) Removed conflicting `border-solid` from the hero's mini-invoice preview container (it had both `border-dashed` and `border-solid` — in Tailwind v4 `.border-solid` is generated after `.border-dashed` so solid was winning, contradicting the dashed Total row below it). Now correctly dashed.
  (4) Verified grid `md:grid-cols-3` col-spans: Hero `md:col-span-2` + Métricas (1) = row 1 (3 cols); Playbook + Local + Calendar = row 2 (3 cols); CSV `md:col-span-3` = row 3 (3 cols). Layout correct, no gaps/overflows.
  (5) Verified the SlowMoChart background (`pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-[320px]`) sits behind the `relative` content motion.div — DOM order handles stacking correctly.
- Gallery — 1 fix applied (via supporting FeatureImage.tsx change):
  (1) Root cause: FeatureImage's base className was `relative overflow-hidden rounded-card`, and Gallery passed `absolute inset-0 h-full w-full`. In Tailwind v4, `.relative` is generated AFTER `.absolute` in the utilities layer, so `.relative` won — FeatureImage's outer div was `position: relative`, making `inset-0` a no-op (0 offset) and relying solely on `h-full w-full` to fill the figure. This is fragile (percentage-height chain figure→FeatureImage depends on the grid row being definite). Fixed by removing `relative` from FeatureImage's base classes (`overflow-hidden rounded-card ${className}`) — now the consumer-supplied `absolute inset-0` wins and the outer div is properly positioned absolute, filling the figure via `inset-0`. The inner `next/image fill` and overlay divs (all `absolute inset-0`) now correctly resolve against FeatureImage's outer div. FeatureImage is only used in Gallery, so this is safe.
  (2) Verified figure has `relative h-full w-full overflow-hidden` (✓, also gets `position: relative` from `.glass`). Verified `figcaption` is `z-10` above the image (✓). Verified `auto-rows-[220px] md:auto-rows-[260px]` gives consistent row heights (✓). Verified hero shot `md:col-span-2 md:row-span-2` spans correctly: rows 1-2 cols 1-2 (hero) + col 3 rows 1-2 (shots 1,2) + row 3 cols 1-2 (shot 3) + col 3 row 3 (shot 4) = 3 rows, 5 shots, no gaps.
- Guardian — 4 fixes applied:
  (1) Content container: added `z-10` (`relative z-10 max-w-page...`) so the grid content sits explicitly above the ParticleField (`absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none`) and the ambient radial glow (`absolute... pointer-events-none`). Previously relied on DOM order alone.
  (2) Pulse ring overflow: added `py-8 md:py-12` to the shield's Reveal wrapper. The 3 pulse rings animate to `scale: 2.05` from a base of `55%` → peak size ~113% of the 300px (mobile) / 380px (desktop) shield box, extending ~20px / ~25px beyond on each side. The new 32px / 48px vertical padding gives them room to expand without being clipped by the section's `overflow-hidden` and without overlapping the copy column. The Reveal wrapper is `flex justify-center` so the shield stays centered with breathing room above/below.
  (3) Floating chips: verified the two chips (`-top-1 right-4` and `bottom-2 -left-2`) fit within the new `py-8 md:py-12` padding. On mobile (375px viewport), the 300px shield is centered in a ~335px content area leaving ~17px per side; the `-left-2` chip extends only 8px past the shield edge — well within bounds. The `-top-1` chip extends 4px above the shield, comfortably inside the 32px top padding.
  (4) Parallax overlap on mobile: reduced `shieldY` from `[50, -50]` → `[24, -24]` and `copyY` from `[-24, 24]` → `[-12, 12]`. On mobile the copy (order-1) stacks above the shield (order-2) with `gap-12` (48px). At scroll end (progress=1) the old values moved the shield up 50px and copy down 24px → 74px gap reduction > 48px gap → 26px overlap. New values: max reduction = 24 + 12 = 36px < 48px gap → safe 12px clearance even before accounting for the new `py-8` padding (which adds another 32px buffer). Still provides visible parallax depth.
- Verified MarketBackground wrapper around shield is `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none rounded-full overflow-hidden` (✓ clips the chart to the disc) and ParticleField wrapper is `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (✓).
- Verified all numeric values use `tnum` (tabular figures): Bento invoice rows/total (`tnum text-pnl-neg`), Guardian chips (`tnum`). No indigo/blue introduced. All `"use client"` directives preserved.
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit 0, clean. Ran `bun run build` → ✓ compiled in 17.6s, ✓ 7/7 static pages generated, all routes prerendered as static content.

Stage Summary:
- 3 target files fixed (Bento.tsx, Gallery.tsx via FeatureImage.tsx, Guardian.tsx) + 1 supporting component (FeatureImage.tsx).
- 10 total edits: Bento (5: z-index layering, padding unification, border conflict, verified grid spans), Gallery (1: FeatureImage `relative`→removed so `absolute inset-0` wins), Guardian (4: content z-10, pulse-ring padding, parallax range reduction, chip overflow verified).
- Root cause found in Gallery: Tailwind v4 generates `.relative` after `.absolute`, so FeatureImage's base `relative` was silently overriding Gallery's `absolute inset-0` — fixed at the source.
- Root cause found in Guardian: parallax ranges (±50/±24) exceeded the mobile stack gap (48px), causing overlap at scroll-end — reduced to ±24/±12.
- Lint clean, build clean. No content/features removed. Design system honored.
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) consider adding `useReducedMotion` guards to Guardian's pulse rings and floating chips (currently rely on the global CSS `prefers-reduced-motion` rule which only clamps CSS animations, not Framer Motion JS `animate` props); (b) the Bento hero card's TiltCard + inner `whileHover={{ y: -4 }}` motion.div both apply transforms — functionally fine but a single transform source would be cleaner; (c) Gallery's `figcaption` uses `shot.desc` as the `<h3>` and `shot.title` as the eyebrow label — semantically inverted (title should be h3, desc should be paragraph) but changing it is a content/semantic change beyond layout scope.

- Read worklog.md and the 3 target files. The card already has `overflow-hidden`, so the very wide stamp text (~518px on mobile, ~749px on md) is clipped to the card's bounds (e.g., 320px on a 360px screen) — no horizontal overflow. TiltCard wrapper has only `relative rounded-card` (no `overflow-hidden`), so no content clipping; the glare overlay stays bounded by `absolute inset-0 rounded-[inherit]`. Confirmed the inner card's `whileHover={{ y: -2 }}` (2px lift) doesn't clip content because the section has `pt-24 lg:pt-28` padding. SlowMoChart wrapper: changed from `absolute inset-x-0 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 pointer-events-none flex justify-center` to `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none flex items-center justify-center` (keeps the inner `w-full max-w-3xl h-[280px]` sizing div, so the chart stays centered and behind the invoice card). Content wrapper: added `z-10` → `relative z-10 max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8` so the content sits explicitly above the radial-glow + SlowMoChart background layers. CountUp TOTAL: kept as-is — the parent `<div className="text-right">` is a flex item with full intrinsic width, the motion.div is block-level (full-width of parent), the CountUp span has `tnum` (tabular-nums), so digit widths are stable and the total never truncates; the 2.5s count-up produces a minor (sub-character) right-edge shift that resolves once the animation completes and does not cause reflow of the left label column.
- Story.tsx — content wrapper: added `z-10` and reordered → `relative z-10 max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8 grid lg:grid-cols-[1fr_1.05fr] gap-12 lg:gap-20 items-start` so the editorial + timeline grid sits explicitly above the MarketBackground + ParticleField layers (both already wrapped in `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none`). Timeline scroll-driven line: verified x-position — the track `<span className="absolute left-[7px] top-2 bottom-2 w-px bg-white/10">` and the accent-fill `<motion.span className="absolute left-[7px] w-px ..." style={{ height: timelineFillHeight, top: "8px" }}>` are both at `left-[7px]`, which is the center of the 15px dots (`absolute left-0 top-1.5 w-[15px] h-[15px]`, dot center at 7.5px). The phase cards have `pl-9` (36px) left padding, so the line+dots sit in the padding gutter and never overlap card content. Word-by-word reveal: confirmed each `<motion.span className="inline-block">` uses `opacity: 0 → 1` + `y: 8 → 0` (not `display`), and each word ends with `&nbsp;` so the inline-block span reserves its space (including the trailing space) from initial render — no reflow as words fade in. Sticky left column: confirmed `lg:sticky lg:top-24` (only `lg:`, disabled on mobile/tablet) — no change needed.
- HowItWorks.tsx — SlowMoChart wrapper: changed from `absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-[280px] pointer-events-none` to `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (chart keeps `w-full h-full`; with `opacity={0.05}` the section-wide stretch is barely perceptible and the section's `overflow-hidden` clips it). Content wrapper: added `relative z-10` → `relative z-10 max-w-page mx-auto px-5 md:px-8` so the header + steps grid sit explicitly above the chart. Vertical scroll-progress rail: kept at `left-0 top-0 bottom-0 w-px pointer-events-none hidden md:block` — verified it lives in the 24px `md:pl-6` gutter to the left of the first TiltCard (TiltCard 1 spans 24–168px from container left, rail at 0px), no overlap with content; rail is behind the `<ol>` (DOM order + `motion.li` has `relative`). Horizontal connecting line: kept at `top-[72px] left-[16.66%] right-[16.66%] h-px overflow-visible` — 72px = vertical center of the 144px TiltCards; the gradient (`from-transparent via-tj-accent/40 to-transparent`) draws via `scaleX: 0→1 origin-left` on view; line is behind the TiltCards (DOM order, no z-index) so only the gap segments are visible — no overlap with step number circles (which sit at `-top-2 -right-2` of each TiltCard, i.e., top:~-8px, well above the line's top:72px). Step number circles: confirmed `absolute -top-2 -right-2 w-9 h-9 rounded-full bg-tj-accent text-white text-xs font-bold tnum flex items-center justify-center z-10` — `position: absolute` reserves no layout space (no shift on pop-in `scale: 0→1` spring), `flex items-center justify-center` centers the "01/02/03" label, `tnum` keeps digit widths stable, `z-10` puts the numbered badge above the accent-fill halo sibling. TiltCard spacing: 3 `<motion.li>` items in `grid md:grid-cols-3 gap-10 md:gap-8`, each with `flex flex-col items-center text-center md:items-start md:text-left md:pl-6` — consistent spacing, no overlap; TiltCard is `w-[144px] h-[144px] rounded-2xl` with `mb-6` below before the title.
- Ran `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors, no warnings. Ran `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 19.3s, ✓ Generating static pages (7/7) in 811.1ms, all routes prerendered as static content.

Stage Summary:
- 3 files modified (layout-only fixes, no content/features removed, all keep `"use client"`):
  - `src/components/marketing/MistakeInvoice.tsx` — SlowMoChart background wrapper → `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none flex items-center justify-center`; content wrapper → `relative z-10 …`. Stamp containment, TiltCard no-clip, CountUp width all verified (no change needed).
  - `src/components/marketing/Story.tsx` — content wrapper → `relative z-10 …`. Timeline line/dots x-alignment, word-by-word opacity reveal, `lg:sticky` mobile-disable all verified (no change needed).
  - `src/components/marketing/HowItWorks.tsx` — SlowMoChart background wrapper → `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none`; content wrapper → `relative z-10 …`. Vertical rail x-position, horizontal connecting line position/z-order, step-number pop-in (no layout shift), TiltCard spacing all verified (no change needed).
- Design system honored: `.glass`, `.rounded-card`, `.hairline`, `.text-primary/.secondary/.tertiary`, `text-tj-accent`/`bg-tj-accent`, `text-pnl-neg`/`text-pnl-pos`/`text-pnl-warn`, `.tnum`, `max-w-page`, `overflow-hidden`. NO indigo/blue introduced. NO content removed.
- Lint clean (exit 0). Build clean (✓ Compiled successfully, all 7 routes prerendered).
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) the HowItWorks horizontal connecting line at `left-[16.66%] right-[16.66%]` doesn't perfectly pass through the centers of the left-aligned TiltCards (TiltCards use `md:items-start md:pl-6`, so TC1 center ≈ 8% and TC3 center ≈ 76% of container width, while the line spans 16.66%–83.33%) — if pixel-perfect centering is desired, either nudge the line to `left-[8%] right-[24%]` or center the TiltCards (`md:items-center md:text-center` and drop `md:pl-6`); (b) the MistakeInvoice CountUp right-edge shifts by a sub-character width during its 2.5s count-up (the right-aligned total grows from `−$0.00` to `−$X,XXX.XX`) — if zero-shift is required, add `inline-block min-w-[10ch] text-right` to the wrapping motion.div so the width is reserved up-front; (c) the Story timeline accent-fill `<motion.span style={{ height: timelineFillHeight, top: "8px" }}>` can extend ~8px past the timeline's bottom at 100% scroll progress (height:100% + top:8px) — wrap the timeline in `overflow-hidden` or cap the height at `calc(100% - 8px)` if that's visually bothersome.

Task ID: R1-d
Agent: qa-risk-wrapped
Task: Pixel-perfect review and fix of RiskTool, MoreFeatures, Wrapped

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full context), globals.css design system (gold/esmeralda/ónix/aurora/seda palettes, glass/chrome/eyebrow utilities, .section, .rounded-card, .tnum, .aurora-bg, .max-w-page), supporting TJ primitives (TiltCard, MarketBackground, ParticleField, SlowMoChart, CountUp, Money), and RiskCalculator (the embedded interactive widget rendered by RiskTool).
- Verified RiskTool.tsx — already correct: section `relative overflow-hidden scroll-mt-24`, soft accent glow `pointer-events-none absolute`, calculator wrapper `mt-12 max-w-2xl mx-auto` (centered as required), RiskCalculator internally wraps MarketBackground in `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (already correct), presets chips use `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2` (wrap gracefully on mobile), risk ladder uses correct proportional widths `riskPctWidth = 1/(1+rr)*100` and `rewardPctWidth = rr/(1+rr)*100` (proportions verified), inputs grid `sm:grid-cols-2 gap-4`, outputs grid `grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3`. No changes needed — left untouched to honor "don't fix what isn't broken".
- Fixed MoreFeatures.tsx:
  - SlowMoChart wrapper changed from `pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-[320px]` → `pointer-events-none absolute inset-0` so both ParticleField and SlowMoChart are full-section ambient layers (per task spec). SlowMoChart uses `preserveAspectRatio="none"` so it stretches cleanly to the section's full height at opacity 0.05 (barely-there depth).
  - Added `className="h-full"` to the motion.div wrapping each TiltCard so the equal-height chain works: grid item (motion.div, stretched via default `align-items: stretch`) → TiltCard (`h-full`) → article (`h-full`). Without this, motion.div was auto-height and the inner `h-full` chain resolved to content height, so cards in a row had unequal heights when descriptions varied in length.
  - Verified icon infinite float (`y: [0, -3, 0]`, 3px up) + icon's `mb-4` (16px) = 19px clearance to title — no overlap.
  - Verified consistent `p-5` padding on all 8 cards, consistent `gap-4`.
- Fixed Wrapped.tsx:
  - **Masonry grid gap fix (the main bug)**: original col-span config was `i=0: col-span-3 row-span-2 / i=3: col-span-3 / others: col-span-2` = 6+3+2+2+3+2 = 18 cells, but in a 6-col grid the auto-placement left col 6 empty in rows 1, 2, 3 AND pushed "instrument" to a 4th row, leaving ~7 empty cells (visually broken bento). Rewrote to a clean 3-row bento: `i=0: col-span-3 row-span-2 (streak hero)` + `i=1: col-span-3 (setup)` + `i=2: col-span-3 (day)` + `i=3: col-span-2 (total)` + `i=4: col-span-2 (discipline)` + `i=5: col-span-2 (instrument)` = 6+3+3+2+2+2 = 18 = 3×6 cells. Layout now: row 1 = streak | setup; row 2 = streak cont | day; row 3 = total | discipline | instrument — zero gaps on desktop, clean stack on mobile (`grid-cols-1`).
  - **CountUp/Money width safety**: added `whitespace-nowrap` to every big number (CountUp + Money components at text-6xl/text-7xl) so digits never break mid-number; added `min-w-0` to flex-col value containers (setup, total, instrument) so they can shrink inside the grid cell; added `break-words` to the setup/instrument name spans so a long name wraps gracefully instead of overflowing; converted "total traded" value from horizontal `flex items-baseline gap-2` to vertical `flex flex-col items-start gap-1` so the big `$X,XXX` number gets the full col-span-2 card width (~324px content area) instead of fighting the "gross volume" label for horizontal space.
  - Converted "streak" and "day" value rows from `flex items-baseline gap-2` to `flex flex-wrap items-baseline gap-x-2 gap-y-1` so the small label can wrap below the big number if the card ever narrows.
  - Verified ConfettiBurst's internal div is `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none overflow-hidden` (already correct) AND the parent motion.article also has `overflow-hidden` (already correct) — double containment for the 14 floating accent dots.
  - Verified MarketBackground behind "best streak" hero card is wrapped in `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` (already correct).
  - Verified TiltCard on every stat card has `relative h-full w-full rounded-card` (already correct) — equal-heights chain intact: motion.article (grid item, stretched) → TiltCard (`h-full`) → inner content (`flex flex-col h-full justify-between gap-4`).
- Ran `bun run lint` → clean (exit 0). Ran `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in ~9s, all 7 routes prerendered as static content.

Stage Summary:
- 3 files reviewed; 2 fixed (MoreFeatures.tsx, Wrapped.tsx), 1 verified correct (RiskTool.tsx).
- MoreFeatures: SlowMoChart now full-section ambient layer (`absolute inset-0`); equal-heights chain repaired by adding `h-full` to the motion.div grid item.
- Wrapped: masonry grid desktop layout completely repaired (was leaving ~7 empty cells across 4 misaligned rows; now clean 3-row bento with zero gaps); CountUp/Money numbers given `whitespace-nowrap` + `min-w-0` + flex-wrap/col restructuring so big numbers never break or overflow; "total traded" card restacked vertically to give the big number full card width.
- All edits honor the design system: `.glass`, `.rounded-card`, `.eyebrow`, `.tnum`, `text-pnl-pos/neg/warn`, `text-tj-accent`, `max-w-page`, `overflow-hidden`. NO indigo/blue introduced. NO content/features removed. ES/EN bilingual strings preserved as-is (including the pre-existing "fournisseurs" typo in the TrackRecord ES string — out of scope for layout fix).
- Lint clean (exit 0). Build clean (✓ Compiled successfully, all 7 routes prerendered).
- Next actions for follow-up: (a) RiskCalculator's slider row is ~28px taller than its sibling Field row (slider has 3 sub-rows: label / slider / scale-labels, while Field has 2: label / input) — labels at top still align, but the slider's scale labels leave an asymmetric empty space below the sibling input. If pixel-perfect row alignment is desired, either absolute-position the scale labels into the row gap or add a matching placeholder sub-row to Field. Left untouched because RiskCalculator.tsx was not in the explicit file list and the current state is functional, not broken. (b) The risk ladder's decorative tick marks at 25%/50%/75% and the "0 / 1R / RR" labels below (positioned via `justify-between` so "1R" sits at center) don't correspond to the actual risk/reward boundary at `riskPctWidth%` — the bar proportions themselves are correct, but the labels are a min/center/max reference rather than accurate R markers. If accurate R markers are desired, position "1R" absolutely at `left: ${riskPctWidth}%` with `-translate-x-1/2`. Left untouched for the same scope reason.

Task ID: R1-h
Agent: qa-final-footer
Task: Pixel-perfect review and fix of FinalCTA, Footer, CookieConsent, BackToTop

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md context and all 4 target files in full (FinalCTA.tsx, Footer.tsx, CookieConsent.tsx, BackToTop.tsx).
- Verified supporting primitives: `asset()` basePath helper, `MagneticButton` (uses `x`/`y` motion values via spring — pure transform, no layout shift), `Reveal` (motion.div with transform → creates stacking context, paints in tree order so content sits above the rotating border), `SlowMoChart` & `ParticleField` (canvas-based, opacity-controlled).
- Verified accent CSS tokens exist: `--accent-base`, `--accent-hover`, `--accent-pressed` — ShimmerWord gradient `accent-base → accent-hover → accent-base` is valid.
- FinalCTA audit:
  * All 7 background layers reviewed: aurora-bg, cinematic image, MarketBackground, ParticleField, 3× Orbs, Particles, bottom fade, rotating conic border.
  * Found `aurora-bg` div was missing `pointer-events-none` (every other layer had it). FIXED.
  * Found content wrapper `relative max-w-page` was missing `z-10` to sit explicitly above all `absolute inset-0` background layers per spec. FIXED → `relative z-10 max-w-page`.
  * Cinematic `<Image>` correctly uses `asset("/img/08-cta-final-cinematic.webp")` ✓.
  * Rotating conic border already has `pointer-events-none` ✓; mask-composite trick renders a 1.5px arc outline at opacity-70.
  * MagneticButton CTA wrapped in `motion.div whileHover scale 1.03` — both use transform (no layout shift); `tnum` present on the price span ✓.
  * ShimmerWord uses 200% background-size with `backgroundPositionX` 0%→100%→0% loop — correct sheen sweep.
  * Scroll-driven `scale` 0.95→1 bound to `useScroll` target ref; outer `<section>` has `overflow-hidden` so the rotating border's `-inset-10` and scale never cause page overflow ✓.
- Footer audit:
  * SlowMoChart wrapper: `pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 -top-8 h-32` inside `footer.overflow-hidden` ✓ (extra `w-full` is harmless; left untouched to minimise churn).
  * Status pulse dot: outer `relative flex w-2 h-2` with inner `animate-ping bg-pnl-pos opacity-60` + solid core `bg-pnl-pos` ✓.
  * Newsletter mini-form: input `h-9 flex-1 min-w-0` + button `h-9 w-9 shrink-0 grid place-items-center` inside `flex items-center gap-2 max-w-xs` — perfectly aligned ✓.
  * Animated underline on `madeFor`: parent `motion.span` sets `initial="idle" whileHover="hover"`; child `motion.span` defines `variants={{idle:{scaleX:0}, hover:{scaleX:1}}}` with `origin-left` — Framer Motion variant propagation works correctly (no fix needed).
  * Back-to-top link sits at end of bottom row with `↑ Top` / `↑ Arriba`, `href="#top"` ✓.
  * Grid `md:grid-cols-[1.4fr_1fr_1fr_1fr] gap-10` — brand column wider, stacks on mobile ✓.
  * Social links: all `w-9 h-9 grid place-items-center rounded-full glass` with consistent hover (`scale-110`, accent tint) ✓.
- CookieConsent audit:
  * Position `fixed bottom-4 left-4 z-50` ✓ — opposite corner from BackToTop's `bottom-6 right-6`, so no overlap on desktop.
  * On small mobile viewports (e.g. 360–390px), `max-w-sm` (24rem ≈ 384px) + `left-4` (1rem) could push the card's right edge past the viewport and horizontally collide with BackToTop. FIXED by adding `w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` so the card is clamped to viewport-minus-1rem-on-each-side while still capping at `max-w-sm` on wider screens. After fix: card right edge stays ≥1rem from viewport right; BackToTop sits at ≥1.5rem from right — clean 3.25rem horizontal gap, zero overlap.
  * Slide-up + fade animation: `initial={{opacity:0, y:24}} animate={{opacity:1, y:0}} exit={{opacity:0, y:24}}` ✓.
  * Cookie icon: `w-7 h-7 rounded-full bg-tj-accent/10 text-tj-accent` wrapping a 16px SVG — correct size, no indigo/blue ✓.
- BackToTop audit:
  * Position `fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40` ✓ — z-40 sits below CookieConsent's z-50 (correct layered ordering).
  * Circular `w-11 h-11 rounded-full glass` ✓.
  * Scale+fade in/out: `initial={{opacity:0, scale:0.7}} animate={{opacity:1, scale:1}} exit={{opacity:0, scale:0.7}}` ✓.
  * Hover lift + accent glow: `hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_8px_28px_rgb(var(--accent-base)/0.40)]` ✓.
  * Up arrow SVG `M9 14V4M4 8l5-5 5 5` in 18×18 viewBox, centered via `flex items-center justify-center` ✓.
- Ran `bun run lint` (clean, no output) and `bun run build` (✓ compiled successfully in 9.5s, all 7 static routes generated).

Stage Summary:
- FinalCTA.tsx: 2 fixes — added `pointer-events-none` to `aurora-bg` layer; added `z-10` to content wrapper. All 7+ background layers now consistently `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none`; content explicitly `relative z-10`.
- Footer.tsx: 0 fixes needed — already correct (SlowMoChart positioning, status pulse, newsletter form alignment, madeFor underline variant propagation, social circles, grid layout all verified).
- CookieConsent.tsx: 1 fix — added `w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` alongside `max-w-sm` to prevent viewport overflow on small screens and guarantee no horizontal overlap with BackToTop.
- BackToTop.tsx: 0 fixes needed — already correct (position, size, animation, hover glow, centered arrow all verified).
- No content/features removed. No indigo/blue introduced. `"use client"` preserved on all files. `tnum` already present where needed.
- Lint: clean. Build: ✓ compiled successfully.

Task ID: R1-e
Agent: qa-stats-social
Task: Pixel-perfect review and fix of StatsBand, SocialProof, Comparison

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full project context), the three target files, plus dependencies TiltCard, SlowMoChart, MarketBackground to understand prop surfaces and existing layout patterns.
- StatsBand.tsx: verified section already had `overflow-hidden`, MarketBackground + ParticleField already in `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` containers, scroll parallax `x:[20,-20]` already wired, infinite float `y:[0,-4,0]` already staggered, sweeping accent line already `absolute bottom-0`. Only fix needed: outer stat motion.div was missing `h-full` + `justify-center`, so 4 stat cards with different label/sub line counts weren't vertically centered against the equal-height grid row. Added `justify-center h-full` to the outer stat motion.div (kept inner float motion.div unchanged so the float still animates over the centered content).
- SocialProof.tsx: (1) SlowMoChart container was `absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-80 pointer-events-none` (only top 320px); changed to `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` so the equity curve fills the section behind the cards. (2) Added `h-full` to the `motion.article` so all 3 testimonial cards stretch to the row's tallest height (default grid stretch is implicit; explicit h-full also flows into TiltCard which already had h-full). (3) Added `h-full` to the inner `<div className="p-6 flex flex-col gap-5">` so the `mt-auto` on the author footer actually pushes it to the bottom of each card regardless of quote length — fixing visual inconsistency where short-quote cards had the author hugging the quote. (4) Avatar container was missing `overflow-hidden`; the infinite pulse ring (`scale:[1,1.7]`) was bleeding outside the avatar circle. Added `overflow-hidden` to the avatar `<span>` and restructured so the verified badge is now a sibling of the avatar inside a `relative shrink-0` wrapper — this keeps the badge at `-bottom-0.5 -right-0.5` (slightly outside the avatar) without being clipped by the avatar's overflow-hidden. (5) Verified the staggered star pop-in uses fixed 14x14 SVGs with scale/rotate animations — no layout shift because the SVG element occupies its 14px box regardless of transform scale. (6) Added `tnum` to the "X años operando / yrs trading" pill (numeric content).
- Comparison.tsx: this file was missing the background layers entirely, had no overlay, no horizontal scroll, and no consistent row heights. (1) Added `MarketBackground` (opacity 0.08, density 60, speed 1.6, volatility 0.012) and `SlowMoChart` (opacity 0.05, cycle 22, points 100) both in `absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none` containers at the top of the section. (2) Added a pulsing accent glow border overlay on the TJ column: an `absolute top-0 bottom-0 left-1/3 w-1/3 pointer-events-none hidden md:block z-[1]` motion.div containing an inner motion.div with `inset 2px / -2px` accent box-shadow borders and a vertical gradient tint, animating opacity [0.45, 0.9, 0.45] over 3s infinitely. The card's existing `overflow-hidden` contains it ("no overflow"). `hidden md:block` so it doesn't show on mobile where the table scrolls horizontally (overlay was "wrong on mobile"). (3) Added `overflow-x-auto` wrapper around the table with `min-w-[680px]` inner div so the table scrolls horizontally on mobile instead of being squished. (4) Added `table-fixed` + explicit `<colgroup>` widths (33% / 33% / 17% / 17%) so the TJ column lands exactly at `left-1/3 w-1/3` and the overlay aligns precisely with it. (5) Added `h-16 md:h-[72px]` to every body `<th>`/`<td>` and `h-14 md:h-16` to header cells for consistent row heights — previously free-form string cells ("40+ institucionales") were ~7px shorter than icon+text cells ("Yes"/"No") because the icon's 20px circle was taller than the 13px text line. (6) Added `tnum` to the `<table>` so all embedded numbers ($29, 40+, 10–20) use tabular figures.
- Ran `bun run lint` (clean, no warnings) and `bun run build` (succeeded — 7/7 static pages generated). No content or features removed; only layout fixes. No indigo/blue introduced. All three files keep `"use client"`.

Stage Summary:
- StatsBand: vertical centering of the 4 big stats fixed via `h-full justify-center` on the outer motion.div. All other layout pieces (overflow-hidden section, pointer-events-none backdrops, parallax x, infinite float, sweeping bottom accent line) were already correct from prior rounds — verified and left in place.
- SocialProof: SlowMoChart now fills the section (`inset-0` instead of `top-0 h-80`). All 3 testimonial cards now have equal heights (`h-full` on article + inner div) with author footers pinned to the bottom via `mt-auto`. Avatar container has `overflow-hidden` so the pulse ring is contained to the avatar circle; verified badge moved to a sibling wrapper so it isn't clipped. Star pop-in confirmed no layout shift.
- Comparison: added MarketBackground + SlowMoChart backdrops (pointer-events-none inset-0); added pulsing accent glow border overlay on TJ column (`left-1/3 w-1/3 hidden md:block`, contained by card overflow-hidden); added `overflow-x-auto` + `min-w-[680px]` for mobile horizontal scroll; added `table-fixed` + colgroup widths (33/33/17/17) so the overlay aligns with the TJ column; added consistent row heights (`h-16 md:h-[72px]` body, `h-14 md:h-16` header); added `tnum` for tabular figures.
- Build + lint both green. Ready for the next QA pass.
