Task ID: R3-f
Agent: faq-glossary
Task: Add FAQ search + glossary improvements + new glossary terms

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, FAQ.tsx, glossary.ts, GlossaryModal.tsx, Chip.tsx, accordion.tsx, dialog.tsx, input.tsx, theme.tsx, globals.css, and Footer.tsx to understand existing patterns (frozen-glossary philosophy, glass/hairline/tnum utilities, lucide-react icons, controlled vs uncontrolled Radix Dialog).
- Added 6 new bilingual glossary terms to `/home/z/my-project/src/lib/trading/glossary.ts`, bringing the total from 24 → 30 (verified, no duplicate `term` strings):
    · Liquidity (basics), Volatility (basics)
    · Spread (execution), Slippage (execution)
    · Risk-reward ratio (risk), Kelly criterion (risk)
  Each term keeps the frozen-English `term` field and adds ES/EN 1–2 sentence definitions consistent with the existing voice. Spread/Slippage reference each other; Risk-reward/Kelly cross-reference win rate & payoff semantics already present.
- Refactored `/home/z/my-project/src/components/tj/GlossaryModal.tsx` to support **controlled mode** (optional `open` + `onOpenChange` props) so FAQ's "no results" link can drive the dialog. Uncontrolled mode (trigger-only) preserved for Footer + default usage.
- GlossaryModal enhancements:
    1. **Category dropdown** — native `<select>` (`appearance-none`, `bg-white/5 border hairline rounded-md h-9 px-3 text-sm`, custom `ChevronDown` icon) sitting next to the existing chips; both control the same `activeCat` state. Chips remain for mouse users, dropdown for keyboard/power users.
    2. **Live count** — `<p className="mt-2.5 text-xs text-tertiary tnum">` shows `{n} términos`/`{n} terms` (singular/plural aware) plus a " · filter applied" hint when query or category is active.
    3. **Keyboard navigation** — list has `role="listbox"` + `tabIndex={0}`; `onKeyDown` handles ArrowUp/ArrowDown (wraps), Enter/Space (toggles expand), Home/End (jump). Active card gets `aria-selected`, a focus ring (`ring-1 ring-tj-accent/40`) and auto-`scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })`.
    4. **Recently viewed** — new section at the top with `Clock` icon + "Vistos recientemente"/"Recently viewed" eyebrow; shows the last 3 expanded terms as accent Chips. Persisted in `localStorage` under `tj-glossary-recent` (SSR-safe `readRecent`/`writeRecent` helpers, JSON.parse try/catch, falls back to in-memory if storage unavailable). Clicking a recent chip resets filters, sets `activeIndex` to that term, scrolls it into view, and expands it.
    5. **Collapsed-by-default cards** — each term card now collapses to a single-line preview (term + category chip + truncated definition) and expands on click OR Enter to reveal the full definition. A `ChevronDown` rotates 180° when expanded.
    6. **Search broadened** — query now matches against both `term` and the active-language definition (previously term-only), so e.g. searching "pérdida" surfaces "Drawdown".
    7. Added a keyboard-hint footer: "Usa ↑ ↓ para navegar y Enter para expandir." / "Use ↑ ↓ to navigate and Enter to expand."
- Edited `/home/z/my-project/src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx`:
    · Added `"use client"` (already had it) and `useState`/`useMemo` imports via `* as React`.
    · Added a search input above the accordion, styled exactly per spec: `bg-white/5 border hairline rounded-md h-10 pl-10 pr-3 text-sm` with a `Search` lucide icon absolutely positioned at left-3. Placeholder is ES/EN aware.
    · `useMemo` filters `items` by question + answer text (case-insensitive, active language).
    · When `filtered.length === 0`, the accordion is replaced by a "No se encontraron resultados" / "No results found" panel containing a button that calls `setGlossaryOpen(true)` to open the controlled GlossaryModal.
    · The same controlled GlossaryModal instance powers BOTH the bottom "Browse the glossary →" link AND the "no results → open glossary" link, so only one dialog instance exists.
    · Used a `key={hasQuery ? \`search-${query.trim()}\` : "default"}` on the Accordion to force a fresh mount on each keystroke so the first match opens by default and stale accordion state from the previous filter doesn't persist. `defaultValue="item-0"` always valid since the no-results branch handles the empty case separately.
- Verified rules: `"use client"` on FAQ.tsx ✓ and GlossaryModal.tsx ✓; NO indigo/blue introduced (only `--accent-base` / `--tj-accent` / `--txt-*` / `--divider` tokens) ✓; `tnum` applied to the glossary count and FAQ search (no numeric spans in FAQ besides the count which uses tnum) ✓; ES/EN bilingual on every visible string ✓; SSR-safe localStorage (lazy `useEffect` read, `typeof window === "undefined"` guards) ✓.
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings). Ran `bun run build 2>&1 | tail -10` (after clearing a stale `.next/lock` from a prior interrupted build) → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.6s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content.

Stage Summary:
- Glossary now ships 30 terms across 5 categories (basics 8, risk 6, psychology 2, metrics 9, execution 5) — all bilingual, all English-term-frozen.
- GlossaryModal is now a small power tool: dual filter entry points (chips + dropdown), live result count, full keyboard nav with roving-style active row + auto-scroll, collapsed-by-default expandable cards, and a persisted "recently viewed" quick-access row. Controlled-mode support means it can be driven by any parent (FAQ's no-results link today, future marketing CTIs tomorrow).
- FAQ now has real-time search with graceful "no results → open glossary" handoff that reinforces the frozen-glossary philosophy: when a visitor can't find their answer, they're one click away from the canonical vocabulary rather than a dead end.
- Lint: clean (exit 0). Build: ✓ Compiled successfully, 7/7 static routes.
- Next actions: (a) The GlossaryModal `aria-selected` semantics treat the arrow-key-tracked row as "selected" — for stricter ARIA listbox compliance one could split into `aria-activedescendant` + a separate multiselect state, but for a single-expand-at-a-time browse UX the current shape is fine. (b) The FAQ search uses a remount-on-keystroke `key` (cheap for 8 items); if FAQ grows past ~30 items, consider switching to a controlled Radix `value`/`onValueChange` on the Accordion to avoid remounts. (c) The "recently viewed" localStorage key is `tj-glossary-recent` (array of term strings, max 3) — if a future settings panel exposes "clear history", it should also clear this key alongside `tj-theme` / `tj-palette` / `tj-cookie-consent`. (d) The collapsed-card preview uses `truncate` on the definition; if a future term has a very long first line, the truncation may hide the most informative part — consider switching to a 1-line `line-clamp-1` + fade mask for visual consistency.

  - Imported `useCallback`. Added `TOUR_KEY = "tj-demo-tour-seen"` constant and an SSR-safe lazy initializer `readTourActive()` (returns `false` on server → no hydration mismatch, `true` for first-time visitors on client).
  - Added `tourActive` state and a stable `dismissTour` callback that flips the state and persists the flag to `localStorage` (wrapped in try/catch for private-mode resilience). The localStorage write happens inside the `setTourActive` updater guarded by `if (!prev) return prev;` so it only fires once per session.
  - Hooked `dismissTour()` into the existing `setPage`, `goDetail`, and `goBack` context methods (guarded by `if (tourActive)`) so ANY in-app navigation — tab clicks, trade-row clicks, the back button — dismisses the tour. Added `tourActive` + `dismissTour` to the `DemoCtx` interface, the memoized value, and the dep array.

  - Imported `useEffect` from `react`.
  - Added a global `keydown` listener (mounted once) that parses digit keys 1–6 and calls `setPage(NAV_ITEMS[num-1].key)`. Guarded against hijacking typing: ignores events whose target is an `INPUT`/`TEXTAREA`/`SELECT` or `isContentEditable`. Calls `e.preventDefault()` only when a digit is matched.
  - Added a tiny `<kbd>` element inside each tab button after the label span: `hidden lg:inline-flex items-center justify-center w-4 h-4 rounded text-[9px] font-mono bg-white/8 text-tertiary tnum`, displaying `index + 1` (1–6). `aria-hidden="true"` since it's a visual hint, not a label. Mobile shows icon-only, sm shows icon+label, lg+ shows icon+label+digit.
- Updated `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx`:
  - Imported `useEffect`. Added an Escape keydown listener inside `AppDemoInner` that fires only while `fullscreen` is active and calls `setFullscreen(false)`. The setState call lives in the event handler, not the effect body, so the `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` rule is respected.
  - Added `relative` to the `glass` container div so absolutely-positioned children anchor to the window itself.
  - Added a new `<TourHint />` component placed inside the page-content `relative` wrapper, above the shimmer overlay (z-30 vs shimmer's z-20). TourHint:
    - Reads `tourActive` + `dismissTour` from `useDemo()` and `t` from `useLang()`.
    - Auto-dismisses via `setTimeout(dismissTour, 8000)` cleared on unmount / when `tourActive` flips.
    - Renders an `AnimatePresence`-wrapped `motion.div` with slide-down + fade-in (`y: -10 → 0`, opacity 0 → 1) and slide-up + fade-out exit, 0.35s easing `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`.
    - Strip: `pointer-events-none absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-30`, with a centered info icon + bilingual hint text. Background `bg-tj-accent/10 backdrop-blur-sm`, border-bottom `border-tj-accent/20`, text `[11px] text-secondary`.

    - Added a small inline `InfoIcon` SVG (currentColor accent circle + i-dot + stem, no indigo/blue).
  - Added a fullscreen-only vignette overlay as the last child of the `glass` container: `pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-40 rounded-card` with `boxShadow: "inset 0 0 100px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.38), inset 0 0 28px 0 rgb(var(--accent-base) / 0.08)"` — a soft dark vignette plus a whisper of accent tint around all four edges, drawn on top of all chrome/content/status-bar but transparent to clicks.
- Verified: `bun run lint` → exit 0 (clean). `bun run build` → first two attempts failed with transient filesystem errors (`ENOENT .next/build-manifest.json`, then a SIGSEGV during page-data collection) unrelated to my changes (these are memory/manifest-phase issues in the sandbox, not code issues — disk: 8.0G free, mem: ~1.2G used / ~2.8G available); third attempt after `rm -rf .next` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.2s, 7/7 static routes generated. Also ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` → only pre-existing errors in `examples/` and `skills/` (eslint-ignored) — zero TypeScript errors in any file I touched.
- Sanity-checked rules: `"use client"` retained on both edited components. No indigo/blue introduced (TourHint and vignette use `tj-accent` / `--accent-base` only, info icon uses `text-tj-accent`). `tnum` applied to the kbd digit. No `setState` calls inside any `useEffect` body — all setState calls are inside event-handler / setTimeout callbacks. Existing content (WindowChrome, status bar, shimmer, all 7 pages, arrow-key/home/end tablist roving) preserved verbatim.

Stage Summary:
- Three new premium micro-interactions land in the demo, all SSR-safe and accessible:
  1. **Tour hint**: first-visit-only thin accent strip at the top of the demo content area, auto-dismisses after 8s / on any navigation / via the X, slides down/up with fade, `pointer-events-none` except the dismiss button. Persistence key `tj-demo-tour-seen`.
  2. **Keyboard shortcuts**: pressing 1–6 (when not typing in a form field) switches to the corresponding tab; tiny `tnum`-styled `<kbd>` hints appear next to each tab label on `lg+` screens.

(c) The vignette is intentionally very subtle (`rgba(0,0,0,0.38)` over a 100px blur); if user testing wants a stronger "cinema" feel, bump the alpha to ~0.55 and the spread to ~14px, but the current values match "subtle gradient vignette" from the brief.

Task ID: R3-g
Agent: theme-transitions
Task: Add smooth theme/palette transitions + palette preview animation

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, globals.css (design tokens, `body { transition: ... 0.4s ease }`, `data-theme`/`data-palette` attribute cascade), lib/theme.tsx (ThemeProvider writes `data-theme`/`data-palette` on `<html>` and persists to localStorage), app/layout.tsx (inline FOUC-prevention script that also writes both attributes pre-paint), app/page.tsx (composition root), marketing/Navbar.tsx (theme toggle button with `aria-label="Toggle theme"` at top-right), demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx (existing live preview card with MiniSparkline, palette swatches with `boxShadow: 0 0 0 2px` active ring, 3-tile KPI strip).
- Created `src/components/tj/ThemeTransition.tsx` (`"use client"`): premium iOS-style circular reveal overlay. A `MutationObserver` on `document.documentElement` watches the `data-theme` and `data-palette` attributes. On change it reads the navbar theme-toggle button's bounding rect (via `[data-theme-toggle]` selector with `[aria-label="Toggle theme"]` fallback, top-right viewport fallback if missing) to compute the reveal origin, then renders a single `motion.div` (`pointer-events-none fixed inset-0 z-[60]`, background `rgb(var(--accent-base))`) that animates `clipPath` from `circle(0px at Xpx Ypx)` to `circle(maxRadius px at Xpx Ypx)` (maxRadius = distance to the farthest viewport corner from the origin) while opacity fades 0.3 → 0 over 0.6s with the project's `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease. Re-triggering during an in-flight animation remounts the overlay via React `key={activeKey}` (counter incremented on each mutation), so rapid theme/palette cycling feels snappy. `useReducedMotion()` short-circuits to `null` so reduced-motion users see only the underlying CSS color transition. Skips the very first observer callback when values haven't actually changed (snapshot `lastTheme`/`lastPalette`).
- Edited `src/app/page.tsx`: imported `ThemeTransition` and rendered `<ThemeTransition />` immediately after `<ReadingProgress />` (before `<main>`), so the overlay sits at the top of the React tree but only becomes visible during a theme/palette switch. Stays out of layout flow because it's `position: fixed`.
- Edited `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`: added `data-theme-toggle` attribute to the theme toggle button so the overlay can locate it robustly (the `aria-label` selector is kept as a fallback for resilience).
- Enhanced `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx` palette swatches:
  • Active ring `boxShadow` changed from `0 0 0 2px` to `0 0 0 3px` (more prominent ring for the active palette, per spec).
  • Added a hover tooltip span above each swatch (`-top-9`, centered) that shows the palette name (e.g. "Oro", "Esmeralda") in a small rounded pill colored with the palette's own `dark` accent — so hovering doubles as a tiny color preview. Tooltip uses CSS `group-hover:opacity-100` for a 200ms fade-in, includes a small rotated-square pointer below it in the same color, and is `aria-hidden="true"` (the visible label below the swatch already conveys the same text to assistive tech).
- Enhanced the existing live preview card in the same file:
  • Restructured the body from a single flex row into a `flex flex-col sm:flex-row` layout: left column = big P&L number + a new "sample KPI tile" showing pos/neg samples (Best +$642.00 in `text-pnl-pos`, Worst −$218.00 in `text-pnl-neg`); right column = the mini equity curve.
  • Added explicit `transition-colors duration-[400ms]` to every colored element in the preview (P&L number, sub-info text, accent dots, KPI values, labels, the new sample tile, the bottom KPI strip) so they recolor in lockstep with the body's 0.4s CSS transition when palette/theme changes.
  • Updated `MiniSparkline`: enlarged from `w-32 md:w-36 h-11` to `w-32 md:w-40 h-12`, and added inline `style={{ transition: "stroke 0.4s ease" }}` on the line path, `style={{ transition: "fill 0.4s ease" }}` on the end dot, and `style={{ transition: "stop-color 0.4s ease" }}` on both gradient stops so the equity curve recolors smoothly with the palette (SVG attributes that map to CSS properties don't transition by default; these declarations opt them in). The framer-motion `pathLength`/`opacity`/`scale` animations are on different properties and continue to work unchanged.
- Ran `bun run lint` — clean (no errors, no warnings). Ran `bun run build` — `✓ Compiled successfully in 9.8s`, all 7 routes prerendered. Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the four touched files (`ThemeTransition.tsx`, `page.tsx`, `Navbar.tsx`, `SettingsPage.tsx`) — zero TypeScript errors (the remaining tsc errors are all in `examples/` and `skills/` directories outside the project source tree, pre-existing).

Stage Summary:
- New file: `src/components/tj/ThemeTransition.tsx` — MutationObserver-driven circular reveal overlay (`pointer-events-none fixed inset-0 z-[60]`), accent-colored, opacity 0.3→0 over 0.6s, expands from the navbar theme-toggle button position. Honors `prefers-reduced-motion`. Mounted in `src/app/page.tsx` right after `<ReadingProgress />`.
- `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`: theme toggle button got a `data-theme-toggle` attribute (selector hook for the overlay).
- `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx` palette swatches: active ring upgraded from 2px → 3px accent; new hover tooltip shows palette name in a pill colored with the palette's own dark accent (aria-hidden, group-hover fade).
- `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx` live preview card: restructured to a 2-column flex layout (P&L + new sample KPI tile on the left, mini equity curve on the right); added explicit `transition-colors duration-[400ms]` on every colored element so palette/theme switches recolor the whole card smoothly; `MiniSparkline` enlarged and given CSS transitions on `stroke`/`fill`/`stop-color` so it recolors in lockstep. All numbers use `tnum`. NO indigo/blue anywhere — only accent tokens and P&L pos/neg vars.
- Lint clean. Build green. TypeScript clean on touched files.

Task ID: R4-a
Agent: cursor-glow
Task: Add cursor glow follower + enhance magnetic effects

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history), then read `src/components/tj/MagneticButton.tsx`, `src/app/page.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`, `src/components/tj/ThemeTransition.tsx` (for framer-motion + reduced-motion + MutationObserver patterns), and grepped `globals.css` to confirm `bg-tj-accent` / `--accent-base` token wiring (line 333: `.bg-tj-accent { background-color: rgb(var(--accent-base)); }`). Also confirmed existing MagneticButton call sites in Hero.tsx, Pricing.tsx, TradeDetailPage.tsx, FinalCTA.tsx to ensure backwards compatibility when extending the props.
- Created `src/components/tj/CursorGlow.tsx` (`"use client"`): a premium accent halo that trails the cursor with a spring lag.
    · `useMotionValue` x/y are fed by a passive `pointermove` listener on `window`; two `useSpring`s (stiffness 80, damping 20, mass 0.6) produce the soft trailing lag.
    · The halo is a single `motion.div`: `pointer-events-none fixed z-[5] hidden md:block w-96 h-96 rounded-full bg-tj-accent blur-3xl mix-blend-screen`. Opacity animates between 0 and 0.06 over 0.25s ease-out so it fades in on first move and out when the cursor leaves the window.
    · Centered on the cursor by subtracting half the element size (192px = w-96/2) when setting the motion values — avoids `translateX/Y` which would conflict with framer-motion's `style.x`/`style.y` transform mapping.
    · Reduced-motion short-circuit via `useReducedMotion()` returns `null` early so no listeners are attached and no DOM is rendered. SSR-safe: initial render returns `null` because `enabled` defaults to `false`; a `matchMedia("(min-width: 768px)")` check inside `useEffect` flips `enabled` true only on md+ clients, with a `change` listener so resizing across the breakpoint re-activates correctly.
    · Visibility tracking: `pointerout` on `document` only hides when `e.relatedTarget === null` (true window exit) so moving between child elements doesn't flicker the halo; `pointerover` re-shows it.
    · Initial motion values are -1000/-1000 so the halo sits off-screen until the first `pointermove` — combined with the spring it slides in from the cursor's first position rather than fading in at the viewport corner.
- Wired `CursorGlow` into `src/app/page.tsx`: imported the component and rendered `<CursorGlow />` immediately after `<ThemeTransition />` (and before `<main>`), so it sits above page chrome (z-[5] < navbar z-50 < reading-progress z-40 < command palette z-50) but below interactive content. Stays out of layout flow because it's `position: fixed`.
- Extended `src/components/tj/MagneticButton.tsx` with optional `target`, `rel`, `ariaLabel`, and `type` props so external links (Footer social icons, target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer") and accessible-name-only buttons can use the magnetic effect without losing accessibility/security attrs. Anchor mode forwards `target`/`rel`/`aria-label`; button mode defaults `type="button"` (prevents accidental form submits) and forwards `aria-label`. All four new props are optional → existing call sites in Hero/Pricing/TradeDetailPage/FinalCTA remain 100% compatible (verified by lint+build passing unchanged).
- Edited `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`: the primary "Comprar"/"Buy" CTA was ALREADY wrapped in `<MagneticButton strength={0.25}>` (R0 work) so it stayed untouched. Replaced the secondary "Probar la demo"/"Try the demo" `<motion.a>` with the same pattern used by the primary CTA: an outer `<motion.div whileHover={{ scale: 1.03 }} whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }} className="inline-block">` wrapper around `<MagneticButton href="#demo" strength={0.15} className="...">`. Swapped the secondary's `hover:-translate-y-0.5` (CSS transform that would fight MagneticButton's `style.x`/`style.y`) for `hover:bg-white/8` and `transition-colors` (instead of `transition-all`) so the only transform on the element is the magnetic one.
- Edited `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`: imported `MagneticButton`; replaced each of the 5 social icon `<a>` elements (GitHub, X/Twitter, YouTube, Discord, RSS) with `<MagneticButton href target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" ariaLabel={label} strength={0.3} className="...">`. Removed the previous `hover:scale-110` (would conflict with the magnetic transform) and `transition-all` → `transition-colors`. The `target`/`rel`/`aria-label` accessibility + security semantics are fully preserved via the new MagneticButton props.
- Verified rules: `"use client"` on CursorGlow.tsx ✓ (already on FinalCTA.tsx + Footer.tsx); NO indigo/blue introduced — CursorGlow uses `bg-tj-accent` only, FinalCTA/Footer edits preserve existing accent tokens; `tnum` not relevant to these edits (no new numeric spans); ES/EN bilingual strings unchanged on the two CTAs; SSR-safe (matchMedia + initial null render + passive listeners cleaned up on unmount).
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings). Ran `bun run build 2>&1 | tail -10` (after clearing a stale `.next/lock` and one transient ENOENT on `_buildManifest.js.tmp` — both unrelated Next.js sandbox flakiness, resolved by `rm -rf .next`) → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.7s, all 7/7 routes prerendered as static content. Also ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the 5 touched files → zero TypeScript errors.

Stage Summary:
- New file: `src/components/tj/CursorGlow.tsx` — accent halo trailing the cursor (`bg-tj-accent` @ 0.06 opacity, `blur-3xl`, `w-96 h-96`, `mix-blend-screen`, `fixed z-[5] hidden md:block`), `useMotionValue`+`useSpring(80/20)` lag, fades out on window exit, hidden on mobile + reduced-motion. Mounted in `page.tsx` right after `<ThemeTransition />`.
- `src/components/tj/MagneticButton.tsx`: extended with optional `target`/`rel`/`ariaLabel`/`type` props (backwards-compatible).
- `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`: secondary "Probar la demo"/"Try the demo" CTA now wrapped in `<MagneticButton strength={0.15}>` inside the same `motion.div whileHover/whileTap` scale wrapper pattern as the primary CTA. Primary "Comprar" already had `strength={0.25}` from R0 — unchanged.
- `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`: all 5 social icons now use `<MagneticButton strength={0.3}>` with `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" ariaLabel={label}` preserved. `hover:scale-110` removed to avoid transform conflicts.
- Lint clean. Build green. TypeScript clean on touched files. No existing features removed; all accessibility + security semantics preserved.
- Next actions: (a) The CursorGlow halo uses `mix-blend-screen` which is invisible on pure-black backgrounds — in the "Ónix" dark palette the glow will be subtle; if it feels too faint, bump opacity from 0.06 → 0.08-0.10, but the spec asked for 0.06 so it stays. (b) The `pointerout`/`pointerover` listeners on `document` (vs `window`) are correct because pointer events bubble to document but `relatedTarget` semantics work best there; if a future Safari quirk shows the halo "sticking" after the cursor leaves, swap to `mouseleave` on `document.documentElement`. (c) MagneticButton's `ariaLabel` prop is camelCase (matches React convention) while the native attribute is `aria-label`; consumers must use `ariaLabel={...}` rather than `aria-label={...}` — documented in the JSDoc but could be made friendlier with a `...props` spread if more attrs are needed later. (d) The CursorGlow z-index is `z-[5]`, below the navbar (`z-50`) — intended, so the glow passes *under* the sticky navbar rather than washing it out; if a future design wants the glow above the navbar, bump to `z-[55]`.

Task ID: R4-d
Agent: stats-enhance
Task: Enhance StatsBand with animated counters + add new feature highlights

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (1755 lines), the existing `StatsBand.tsx` (4-stat CountUp-based band with MarketBackground + ParticleField + parallax + accent gradient line), `AnimatedNumber.tsx` (rAF easeOutExpo count-up triggered by `useInView`, formats via `fmtInt`/`fmtPct`/`fmtMoney`/`fmtNum`, `tnum` always applied), `CountUp.tsx` (legacy — kept for the 11 other consumers), `PositioningStrip.tsx`, `Bento.tsx`, `Eyebrow.tsx`, `Reveal.tsx`, `SlowMoChart.tsx`, `page.tsx`, and `lib/trading/format.ts` (confirmed `fmtPct(v, lang, decimals)` = `${fmtNum(v*100, lang, decimals)}%`, so `value=1, decimals=0` → "100%"). Confirmed `CountUp` is still used in 11 other files — only `StatsBand.tsx` switches to `AnimatedNumber`.
- **Edited `src/components/marketing/StatsBand.tsx`** — replaced the 4-stat array with a 6-stat discriminated-union array and switched the per-stat number renderer from `CountUp` to `AnimatedNumber`:
  · Stat 1: `{kind:"num", value:40, format:"int", suffix:"+"}` → "40+" / "Métricas calculadas" / "Calculated metrics" (+ sub "institucionales"/"institutional-grade").
  · Stat 2: `{kind:"num", value:72, format:"int"}` → "72" / "Operaciones en demo" / "Trades in demo" (+ sub "datos de muestra"/"sample data").
  · Stat 3: `{kind:"num", value:1, format:"percent", decimals:0}` → "100%" / "Local y privado" / "Local & private" (+ sub "sin servidor, sin nube"/"no server, no cloud"). The `value=1` works because `fmtPct` multiplies by 100, and `decimals=0` strips the default `.0` so it renders exactly "100%".
  · Stat 4: `{kind:"num", value:5, format:"int"}` → "5" / "Paletas de acento" / "Accent palettes" (+ sub listing the 5 palettes).
  · Stat 5: `{kind:"num", value:30, format:"int"}` → "30" / "Días de garantía" / "Day guarantee" (+ sub "devolución sin preguntas"/"no-questions refund").
  · Stat 6: `{kind:"text", text:"ES+EN"}` → renders a plain `<span className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold tnum ...">ES+EN</span>` (no animation, smaller font size since 5 chars vs 1–4 for the numbers).
  · Grid changed from `grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-y-10 gap-x-6` to the spec-exact `grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-6 gap-4`.
  · Number font size reduced from `text-5xl md:text-6xl` to `text-4xl md:text-5xl` so the 6-col layout breathes (each cell is ~187px at `max-w-page` lg); label `text-xs md:text-sm`, sub `text-[11px] md:text-xs`.
  · Preserved every premium layer: MarketBackground (`opacity={0.10} density={70} speed={1.4} volatility={0.014}`), ParticleField (`opacity={0.3} count={42} linkDistance={140}`), `aurora-bg` + accent gradient wash, scroll-driven `x: +20 → -20` parallax on the grid wrapper, per-stat staggered entrance (`delay: i * 0.08`), per-stat infinite float (`duration: 4 + i*0.6`, `delay: i * 0.35`), and the bottom animated accent gradient line (`scaleX` draw on view, 1.4s ease).
  · Per-stat entrance stagger tightened from `i * 0.1` to `i * 0.08` to keep the 6-stat reveal under ~0.6s.
  · Removed the unused `CountUp` import; added `AnimatedNumber` import.
- **Created `src/components/marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx`** — `"use client"` cross-fading carousel of 5 feature highlights:
  · Highlights (all bilingual ES/EN):
    1. Privacy — "100 % local, sin nube" / "100 % local, no cloud".
    2. Institutional metrics — "Métricas institucionales" / "Institutional metrics".
    3. CSV import — "Importación CSV universal" / "Universal CSV import".
    4. Discipline — "Disciplina que cuesta dinero" / "Discipline that costs money".
    5. Bilingual — "Bilingüe ES + EN nativo" / "Native ES + EN bilingual".
  · 5 bespoke inline SVG icons (currentColor, strokeWidth 1.3–1.6) — `PrivacyIcon` (server/drive with slot dots + content lines), `MetricsIcon` (4-bar chart), `ImportIcon` (file with download arrow into tray), `DisciplineIcon` (shield + check), `BilingualIcon` (globe with meridians). NO indigo/blue — all use `text-tj-accent` / `bg-tj-accent/15`.
  · Auto-advance: `setInterval` every `ADVANCE_MS = 4000ms`, `setIdx((i) => (i+1) % count)`. Pauses on hover (`onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave`) and on focus capture (`onFocusCapture`/`onBlurCapture`) for keyboard users — interval re-creates via the `useEffect` dep array when `paused` flips.
  · Cross-fade: `AnimatePresence` with default `mode="sync"` (NOT `mode="wait"`) so leaving and entering panels briefly overlap for a true cross-fade. Each panel is `absolute inset-0` inside a `relative glass rounded-card` container with `min-h-[184px] md:min-h-[168px]`. Entrance: `{opacity:0, y:10} → {opacity:1, y:0}`; exit: `{opacity:0, y:-10}`; transition `duration:0.55, ease:[0.22,1,0.36,1]`.
  · Premium extras: an animated accent gradient line draws across the top of the card on view (`scaleX 0→1`, 1.1s); a thin progress bar at the bottom of the card fills from `scaleX:0 → 1` over exactly 4s in lockstep with the auto-advance interval, keyed by `progress-${idx}-${paused}` so it remounts (and restarts) on every slide change and collapses to 0 instantly when paused (so resuming restarts the 4s cleanly).
  · Each panel layout: `flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center justify-center text-center md:text-left gap-3 md:gap-5` — icon chip stacks above the text on mobile, sits to the left on md+. Icon chip `w-12 h-12 md:w-14 md:h-14 rounded-md bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent` with its own subtle `scale:0.85→1 + opacity:0→1` entrance (`delay:0.08` after the panel starts).
  · Dots: row below the card, one per highlight. Active dot animates `width: 26px` and full accent color; inactive dots `width: 6px` and `accent/28`. Each dot is a real `<button>` with descriptive bilingual `aria-label` (`Ir a la característica N de 5: <title>` / `Go to feature N of 5: <title>`) and `aria-current={active ? "true" : undefined}`. `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-tj-accent/60` for keyboard nav. Hover state: inactive dots get a subtle `bg-tj-accent/15` ring behind them.
  · Background: `SlowMoChart opacity={0.05} cycle={22}` drifting behind the glass card + `aurora-bg` wash. Section padding: `section-tight relative overflow-hidden` (matches PositioningStrip/StatsBand rhythm).
  · `role="region" aria-roledescription="carousel"` on the card for AT semantics; section-level `aria-label` for the band.
- **Edited `src/app/page.tsx`**: imported `FeatureRotator` from `@/components/marketing/FeatureRotator` (alphabetically positioned between `PositioningStrip` and `Bento` in the import block) and inserted `<FeatureRotator />` between `<PositioningStrip />` and `<Bento />` in the main composition — exactly where the spec asked.
- Verified rules:
  · `"use client"` present on both `StatsBand.tsx` (retained) and `FeatureRotator.tsx` (added). ✓
  · NO indigo/blue anywhere — only `tj-accent`, `accent-base`, `text-primary/secondary/tertiary`, `aurora-bg`, `glass`, `hairline` tokens. ✓
  · `tnum` applied to every numeric span: `AnimatedNumber` applies it internally for the 5 animated stats, plus an explicit `tnum` on the static "ES+EN" span. ✓
  · ES/EN bilingual on every visible string (labels, sub-labels, highlight titles+descs, aria-labels, section aria-label). ✓
  · `"use client"` correctly hoisted as the very first line of `FeatureRotator.tsx`. ✓
  · No `setState` calls inside `useEffect` bodies — `setIdx` lives in the `setInterval` callback, `setPaused` lives in the event handlers. ✓
- **Verification runs**:
  · `bun run lint` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings).
  · `bun run build` → first three attempts hit transient sandbox issues (an `ENOENT _buildManifest.js.tmp` filesystem race, then two `SIGKILL` OOM kills during Turbopack page-data collection — `free -h` showed 1.7Gi free / 3.3Gi available, so it was a brief memory spike, not a code issue). Fourth attempt after `rm -rf .next && sleep 5` → ✓ Compiled successfully, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content.
  · `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the three touched files (`StatsBand.tsx`, `FeatureRotator.tsx`, `page.tsx`) → zero TypeScript errors (the remaining pre-existing tsc errors live in `examples/` and `skills/` directories outside the project source tree).

Stage Summary:
- `StatsBand.tsx`: now 6 stats (was 4), AnimatedNumber-driven (was CountUp), 6-col responsive grid (was 4-col). New stats: 40+ calculated metrics, 72 trades in demo, 100% local & private (count-up from 0%→100%), 5 accent palettes, 30-day guarantee, ES+EN native bilingual (plain text, no animation). Every premium background/parallax/float/gradient-line layer preserved verbatim.
- `FeatureRotator.tsx` (new file): 5-highlight cross-fading carousel with auto-advance (4s, pause on hover/focus), AnimatePresence sync-mode cross-fade (true overlap), animated progress bar in lockstep with the interval, animated top accent line on view, accessible dot navigation with bilingual aria-labels, glass card on SlowMoChart + aurora backdrop, bilingual content throughout.
- `page.tsx`: `<FeatureRotator />` wired between `<PositioningStrip />` and `<Bento />` per spec; import added alphabetically in the marketing block.
- Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (7/7 static routes). TypeScript clean on all touched files. No indigo/blue. `tnum` on every numeric span. `"use client"` where hooks used.
- Next actions: (a) The progress bar uses a key-based remount on `idx` change to restart the 4s fill — if a future redesign wants a smoother "scrub-style" pause (freeze mid-fill instead of reset-to-0), switch to a manually-tick-driven `useEffect` that updates `scaleX` via `requestAnimationFrame` and pauses the rAF loop. (b) The FeatureRotator currently has no keyboard arrow-key navigation between highlights (only the dot buttons are keyboard-reachable); for stricter carousel a11y one could add `onKeyDown` left/right handlers on the card that call `goTo(idx ± 1)`. (c) The StatsBand `text-3xl md:text-4xl` size for "ES+EN" is deliberately smaller than the numeric `text-4xl md:text-5xl` to keep visual weight balanced — if a future palette swap makes "ES+EN" feel cramped at lg, bump to `text-4xl md:text-5xl` to match the others. (d) Both components use the `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease consistently with the rest of the site; if a future design refresh changes the global ease, update both files in lockstep.

Task ID: R4-b
Agent: demo-export
Task: Add demo share/export button + print styles + PDF-friendly layout

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history) and the three target files: `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx`, `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx`, `src/app/globals.css`. Also read `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (translation dictionary), `src/lib/trading/data.ts` (TRADES source + Trade interface), `src/app/page.tsx` (page composition to verify which components use `.fixed`/`<header>`/`<footer>`/`<canvas>` for print-style targeting), and grepped CursorGlow / ThemeTransition / CookieConsent / BackToTop / ReadingProgress / CommandPalette / MarketBackground / ParticleField / SlowMoChart / Navbar / Footer for their root-element classes to design robust print selectors.
- Added 4 new i18n keys to `src/lib/i18n.tsx`: `demoShare` (es "Compartir" / en "Share"), `demoShareCopied` (es "✓ Copiado" / en "✓ Copied"), `downloadData` (es "Descargar datos" / en "Download data"), `dataDownloaded` (es "✓ Descargado" / en "✓ Downloaded"). Inserted `demoShare`/`demoShareCopied` right after `demoTourDismiss` in the demo block, and `downloadData`/`dataDownloaded` right after `loadSample` in the settings block, so the dictionary stays logically grouped.
- Edited `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx`:
    · Added `useState` to the React import (was `useEffect` only).
    · Added a new `ShareButton` component (placed in its own `/* Share button (status bar) */` section, before the TourHint section). It uses `navigator.share({ title: "Trading Journal — Demo", url: window.location.href })` when available, and falls back to `navigator.clipboard.writeText(url)` with a 2s `copied` confirmation. All `navigator`/`window` access is guarded by `typeof … !== "undefined"` for SSR safety. The share icon is an inline SVG (three nodes connected by two lines — standard "share" glyph, 11×11 viewBox 16×16, `stroke="currentColor"`), aria-hidden, NO indigo/blue (uses `currentColor` → inherits `text-tertiary`/`hover:text-primary`).
    · Restructured the demo status bar's right cluster: the fullscreen button was previously the lone right-side element; both Share and Fullscreen are now wrapped in `<span className="flex items-center gap-3">` so the `justify-between` layout still works (left = autoSaved pill, middle = DEMO account on sm+, right = Share + Fullscreen). Share button label is `hidden sm:inline` (matches Fullscreen's pattern) so the status bar doesn't overflow on mobile.
- Edited `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx`:
    · Added `import { TRADES } from "@/lib/trading/data";` (the in-memory sample dataset — 60 deterministic trades).
    · Added a new `DownloadIcon` inline SVG component (download arrow into a tray, 24×24 viewBox, `stroke="currentColor"`, accent-friendly, no indigo/blue) next to the existing `RefreshIcon`.
    · Added `downloadState` state (`"idle" | "loaded"`) alongside the existing `sampleState`.
    · Added `handleDownloadData()`: builds a CSV string with the 10 spec-mandated columns (id, instrument, setup, direction, entry, exit, qty, netPnl, rMultiple, closedAt) from `TRADES`. Strings are CSV-escaped (double quotes for values containing commas/quotes/newlines, `""` for embedded quotes). Numbers emitted raw (no locale grouping) so the file imports cleanly into Excel/Sheets/Numbers. Dates serialized as ISO via `tr.closedAt.toISOString()` (guarded against invalid Dates). The CSV is wrapped in a `Blob({ type: "text/csv;charset=utf-8;" })`, an object URL is created, an `<a download="trading-journal-sample.csv">` is appended to `document.body`, `.click()`-ed, removed, and the URL is revoked on the next tick. The `downloadState` flips to `"loaded"` for 2 s to show the `✓ Descargado`/`✓ Downloaded` confirmation. The whole thing is wrapped in try/catch so environments without Blob/URL.createObjectURL fail silently.
    · Restructured the "Sample data" SettingSection's right-side action area: the existing "Cargar datos de ejemplo" `motion.button` is now wrapped together with a new "Descargar datos" `motion.button` in `<div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-3 shrink-0">`. Both buttons share the same visual language (h-11, min-w-[200px], AnimatePresence with idle/loaded states, `text-pnl-pos` check confirmation). The Download button is given a slightly more accent-forward treatment (`border-tj-accent/40 bg-tj-accent/10 hover:bg-tj-accent/15`) to distinguish it visually from the neutral Load button, while staying within the accent-token palette (no indigo/blue). All numbers in the surrounding metadata strip ("60 trades · 12 instruments · 3 months · Deterministic") already use `tnum` and are unchanged.
- Edited `src/app/globals.css`: appended a comprehensive `@media print { … }` block (~125 lines, 9 numbered sub-sections) after the `@keyframes tj-shimmer-sweep` rule. The block:
    1. Forces `html, body { background: #ffffff !important; color: #000000 !important; }` and disables the `body::before` SVG-noise overlay; also overrides `:root[data-theme="dark"]`/`[data-theme="light"]` body backgrounds so the no-FOUC theme script doesn't bleed through.
    2. Hides chrome & overlays via a single tight selector list: `body > div > header` (Navbar — scoped to the page wrapper so `<header>`s nested inside marketing sections like Hero stay visible), `body > div > footer` (Footer — same scoping; Story.tsx's in-card `<footer>` stays visible), `.fixed` (catches Navbar header, CookieConsent, BackToTop, ReadingProgress, CommandPalette modal, ThemeTransition reveal overlay, CursorGlow halo, and the Sonner toaster in one shot), `canvas` (hides MarketBackground, ParticleField, and SlowMoChart — all three render a `<canvas>`), and an explicit `.tj-no-print` opt-out hook for future use.
    3. Resets `main, section, article, header, footer` to `overflow: visible !important` (several sections use `overflow: hidden` for decorative effect — that would clip content on paper) and forces their background to white.
    4. Strips `.glass, .chrome` surfaces: `background: #fff`, `border: none`, `box-shadow: none`, `backdrop-filter: none` (and the `-webkit-` prefix).
    5. Universal `*` reset: `color: #000`, `background-color: transparent`, `border-color: transparent`, no `box-shadow`/`text-shadow`/`backdrop-filter`. This forces P&L colors (red/green) to legible solid black on paper — they would print poorly otherwise.
    6. `.text-gradient` and its descendants: gradient fills rely on `background-clip: text` which doesn't print, so the rule forces solid black (`-webkit-text-fill-color: #000`, `background: none`, `background-clip: initial`) so gradient headings stay readable.
    7. Page-break rules: `main > section { break-inside: avoid; page-break-inside: avoid; }` keeps each marketing section together (page breaks fall *between* sections, not through them); `h1/h2/h3 { break-after: avoid; page-break-after: avoid; }` prevents orphaned headings at the bottom of a page.
    8. Resets the page wrapper backgrounds (`.min-h-screen`, `.flex-1`) one more time.
    9. Resets `html.dark` / `html[class*="dark"]` background (the no-FOUC script adds `.dark` to `<html>` before paint) so the dark class doesn't leak into print.
- Verified rules: `"use client"` already present on AppDemo.tsx + SettingsPage.tsx (no hooks added to globals.css — it's a stylesheet, not a component); NO indigo/blue introduced (ShareButton icon uses `currentColor`, DownloadIcon uses `currentColor`, Download button uses `tj-accent` tokens only); `tnum` already present on the SettingsPage metadata strip — no new numeric spans added (the CSV uses raw numbers but that's data, not UI); ES/EN bilingual via `useLang()` + the 4 new i18n keys; SSR-safe (all `navigator`/`window`/`document` access in the ShareButton and handleDownloadData is guarded).
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings). Ran `bun run build 2>&1 | tail -10` — first two attempts hit a transient Next.js Turbopack manifest-cache race (`ENOENT … _buildManifest.js.tmp.XXX` + `Cannot find module … /icon.svg/route.js`), both unrelated to my changes (a known sandbox flakiness — R4-a hit the same issue and resolved it with `rm -rf .next`); cleared `.next/` and re-ran → ✓ Compiled successfully in 13.3s, all 7/7 routes prerendered as static content. Also ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the touched files (`AppDemo`, `SettingsPage`, `globals`, `i18n`) → zero TypeScript errors.

Stage Summary:
- `src/lib/i18n.tsx`: +4 keys (`demoShare`, `demoShareCopied`, `downloadData`, `dataDownloaded`) — bilingual ES/EN.
- `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx`: +`useState` import; new `ShareButton` component (Web Share API → clipboard fallback → 2s `✓ Copiado`/`✓ Copied` confirmation, share-icon SVG, bilingual via `useLang()`); status bar right cluster restructured to `<span className="flex items-center gap-3">` holding Share + Fullscreen buttons (layout unchanged on mobile, both labels `hidden sm:inline`).
- `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx`: +`import { TRADES }`; new `DownloadIcon` SVG; new `downloadState` + `handleDownloadData()` (CSV builder with the 10 spec columns, Blob download as `trading-journal-sample.csv`, 2s `✓ Descargado`/`✓ Downloaded` confirmation, try/catch for restricted environments); "Sample data" section's action area restructured to a 2-button flex row (Load + Download), Download button visually distinguished with `tj-accent` border/bg tint (no indigo/blue).
- `src/app/globals.css`: +~125-line `@media print { … }` block (9 numbered sub-sections) that hides navbar/footer/cookie-consent/back-to-top/cursor-glow/MarketBackground/ParticleField/SlowMoChart/command-palette/reading-progress/theme-transition/sonner-toaster (via `body > div > header` + `body > div > footer` + `.fixed` + `canvas`), strips glass/chrome/shadows/borders, forces black-on-white via universal `*` reset, neutralizes `.text-gradient` to solid black (gradients don't print), forces `overflow: visible` on all main/section/article/header/footer, and sets `break-inside: avoid` + `break-after: avoid` so page breaks fall between major sections and headings never orphan.
- Lint clean. Build green (7/7 routes prerendered). TypeScript clean on touched files.
- Next actions: (a) The Share button only shows its text label on `sm+` (`hidden sm:inline`) — on mobile it's icon-only, matching the Fullscreen button's pattern; if a fuller mobile label is desired, drop the `hidden sm:inline` but the status bar (`h-7 text-[11px]`) is tight so the icon-only mobile treatment is intentional. (b) The Download CSV uses raw numbers (no `$`/`%` formatting) for clean spreadsheet import; if a human-readable variant is wanted later, add a second button or a format toggle, but raw is the correct default for data export. (c) The print stylesheet's universal `* { color: #000 !important; }` forces P&L red/green to solid black on paper — this is intentional (colored text prints poorly and burns toner) but if a future "print in color" mode is wanted, scope the reset to `:not(.print-color)`. (d) The `body > div > header` / `body > div > footer` selectors assume the page wrapper stays a single `<div>` child of `<body>` — if `Providers` ever wraps the page in another div, bump to `body > div > div > header` or switch to a `data-tj-navbar`/`data-tj-footer` attribute hook on the components themselves (more robust, but a bigger change).

Task ID: R4-f
Agent: error-pages
Task: Add custom 404 page + error boundary + loading states

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, layout.tsx, page.tsx, i18n.tsx, MarketBackground/ParticleField/Hero for visual consistency (acrylic body backdrop, `.glass`/`.chrome`/`.text-gradient`/`.tnum` utilities, accent button pattern from Hero, `useLang()` bilingual pattern).
- Verified `bg-tj-accent` / `bg-tj-accent-hover` utilities exist in globals.css (@theme inline maps `--color-tj-accent`/`--color-tj-accent-hover` from `--accent-base`/`--accent-hover`).
- Confirmed `Providers` (ThemeProvider + LanguageProvider) wraps everything inside the root layout, so `useLang()` is available to `not-found.tsx`, `error.tsx`, and `loading.tsx`.
- Created `src/app/not-found.tsx` (client): full-screen centered 404 over the body's acrylic backdrop + a `MarketBackground` layer at opacity 0.06 (equity line off) + `ParticleField` at opacity 0.2 + a soft accent orb. Large `404` in `.text-gradient` with `clamp(6rem, 18vw, 12rem)`, `tnum` on the digits, Framer Motion staggered entrance. ES/EN heading + subcopy, accent "Volver al inicio / Back to home" button linking to `href="/"` (Next.js applies `basePath` automatically).
- Created `src/app/error.tsx` (client, required for error boundaries): full-screen centered over acrylic backdrop with two soft orbs (accent + pnl-neg tint). Pulsing red status pill ("Error en tiempo de ejecución / Runtime error"), "Algo salió mal / Something went wrong" heading, brief message, discrete `error.digest` reference line (tnum), accent "Reintentar / Try again" button calling `reset()`, glass "Volver al inicio / Back to home" link. `useEffect` logs the caught error for observability.
- Created `src/app/loading.tsx` (client): lightweight root loading state — three accent dots in a row with staggered pulse (delay 0/0.18/0.36s, opacity+scale keyframes, accent glow shadow), "Cargando… / Loading…" label below. No canvas components on purpose (fast shell).
- Lint: first pass surfaced one warning (unused `eslint-disable no-console` directive in error.tsx — the rule isn't enabled). Removed the directive; `bun run lint` now passes with 0 problems.
- Build: `bun run build` compiles successfully (Next.js 16.1.3 Turbopack) and the `/_not-found` route is now registered as a static page alongside `/`.

Stage Summary:
- Three new files in `src/app/`: `not-found.tsx`, `error.tsx`, `loading.tsx` — all bilingual (ES/EN) via `useLang()`, all client components (required for framer-motion + the error boundary contract), all matching the premium acrylic + glass + accent design system with NO indigo/blue.
- 404 uses `text-gradient` + `tnum` on the "404", subtle `MarketBackground` (opacity 0.06) + `ParticleField` (opacity 0.2) per spec, accent home button.
- Error boundary receives `{ error, reset }`, calls `reset()` on retry, offers a glass home link, surfaces `error.digest` when present.
- Loading state is intentionally lightweight (CSS/Framer only, no canvas) with 3 staggered pulsing accent dots.
- All home links use `href="/"` (Next.js applies the configured `basePath="/JournalTradingWeb"` automatically).
- `bun run lint` → 0 problems. `bun run build` → ✓ compiled successfully, `/_not-found` registered as static route.

Task ID: R4-e
Agent: keyboard-help
Task: Add keyboard shortcuts overlay + enhance accessibility

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history) plus the four anchor files: `src/components/tj/CommandPalette.tsx` (for glass modal + Framer Motion + Kbd styling + Escape-capture pattern + `[cmdk-root]` detection), `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx` (for the existing ⌘K chip layout I needed to mirror), `src/app/page.tsx` (mount point), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` + `src/lib/theme.tsx` (for the `useLang`/`useTheme` APIs and the `toggle()`/`toggleTheme()` semantics). Also grepped for existing `keydown` listeners to avoid conflicts — found the demo's `TopNav.tsx` 1–6 tab switcher (already input-guarded, no conflict with T/L/?).
- Created `src/components/tj/ShortcutsHelp.tsx` (`"use client"`): a glass modal overlay that opens via a window CustomEvent `tj:open-shortcuts-help` (dispatched by `GlobalShortcuts` on `?` and by the Navbar `?` button on click). Closes on Escape (capture-phase listener, beats any other Escape handler), backdrop click, or X button. Framer Motion fade + scale + lift (`[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease, matching CommandPalette). Bilingual ES/EN via `useLang()`. Lists all six shortcuts in a `<ul>` with `<kbd>` chips: `⌘/Ctrl K` (Abrir command palette / Open command palette), `?` (Mostrar esta ayuda / Show this help), `1–6` (Cambiar pestaña de demo / Switch demo tab), `Esc` (Cerrar / Close), `T` (Cambiar tema / Toggle theme), `L` (Cambiar idioma / Toggle language). Header has title + subtitle + X button; footer has an `esc cerrar/close` hint + "Pulsa ? cuando quieras / Press ? anytime" reminder. While open, sets `document.body.dataset.shortcutsHelpOpen = "true"` so `GlobalShortcuts` can suppress T/L firing behind the modal. Exports an `openShortcutsHelp()` helper (single source of truth for the event name) used by both `GlobalShortcuts` and the Navbar.
- Created `src/components/tj/GlobalShortcuts.tsx` (`"use client"`): invisible side-effect component (returns `null`). Listens on `window` for `keydown` and routes three shortcuts:
  · `T` (case-insensitive, so Shift+T also works) → `toggleTheme()`.
  · `L` (case-insensitive) → `toggle()` (language).
  · `?` (Shift+/) → `openShortcutsHelp()`.
  All three skip when: target is INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT/contentEditable; `[cmdk-root]` exists in the DOM (CommandPalette open — cmdk renders that attribute on its root); `body[data-shortcuts-help-open="true"]` (ShortcutsHelp open, manages its own keys); or any meta/ctrl/alt modifier is held (never hijack Cmd+T, Ctrl+L, etc. — Shift is allowed since `?` requires it). `?` detection is layout-tolerant: `e.key === "?"` OR (`e.shiftKey && (e.key === "/" || e.code === "Slash")`) so it works on both US (Shift+/ = `?`) and ES keyboards (where `?` is on a different physical key but the Slash `e.code` is layout-independent).
- Edited `src/app/page.tsx`: imported `ShortcutsHelp` + `GlobalShortcuts` and rendered them right after `<CommandPalette />` (so all three top-level keyboard-driven overlays live together at the top of the React tree, before `<Navbar />`).
- Edited `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`: imported `openShortcutsHelp` and added a small `?` button immediately after the `⌘K` kbd chip. Style exactly per spec: `hidden md:inline-flex items-center justify-center w-7 h-7 rounded-md border hairline border-solid text-[11px] text-tertiary hover:text-primary hover:bg-white/8` (plus `transition-colors` for smooth hover, matching the other controls). Uses `t("navShortcuts")` for both `aria-label` and `title` so the tooltip is bilingual.
- Edited `src/lib/i18n.tsx`: added the new `navShortcuts` STR key (`{ es: "Atajos de teclado (?)", en: "Keyboard shortcuts (?)" }`) right after the existing `navFaq` key — keeps the nav cluster of keys together and gives the Navbar `?` button a localized label/title.
- Verified rules: `"use client"` on all three component files (ShortcutsHelp, GlobalShortcuts, Navbar already had it); NO indigo/blue introduced — only `bg-tj-accent`, `text-primary/secondary/tertiary`, `bg-white/8`, `bg-white/[0.03]`, `bg-black/50`; `tnum` on the `<kbd>` chip (the `1–6` chip benefits from tabular figures for visual consistency with CommandPalette's kbd); ES/EN bilingual throughout via `useLang()`.
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings). Ran `bun run build 2>&1 | tail -10` — the first 4 attempts hit a transient Next.js 16 + Turbopack + `output: export` race on `.next/static/<hash>/_buildManifest.js.tmp` (same ENOENT flakiness previously documented by R4-a/R4-c in this worklog, unrelated to code changes — the Webpack build path also confirmed "✓ Compiled successfully in 17.9s" so the TypeScript/JSX compiles cleanly); 5th attempt with `rm -rf .next` succeeded: all 6 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`), `out/` directory generated. Also ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` filtered to the touched files (`ShortcutsHelp.tsx`, `GlobalShortcuts.tsx`, `Navbar.tsx`, `page.tsx`, `i18n.tsx`) → zero TypeScript errors.

Stage Summary:
- New file: `src/components/tj/ShortcutsHelp.tsx` — bilingual glass modal (`max-w-md`, `z-[100]`) listing all 6 keyboard shortcuts with `<kbd>` chips; opens via `tj:open-shortcuts-help` window CustomEvent; closes on Esc/backdrop/X; Framer Motion scale+fade. Sets `body[data-shortcuts-help-open]` while mounted. Exports `openShortcutsHelp()` helper.
- New file: `src/components/tj/GlobalShortcuts.tsx` — invisible window keydown router. `T` → toggle theme, `L` → toggle language, `?` → open shortcuts help. Guards against form fields, `[cmdk-root]` (CommandPalette open), `body[data-shortcuts-help-open]`, and meta/ctrl/alt modifiers. Layout-tolerant `?` detection (e.key + e.code fallback for ES keyboards).
- `src/app/page.tsx`: mounted `<ShortcutsHelp />` and `<GlobalShortcuts />` immediately after `<CommandPalette />` (top of React tree, before `<Navbar />`).
- `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`: added a `?` button (spec-exact class list + `transition-colors` + `aria-label`/`title` from `t("navShortcuts")`) immediately after the existing `⌘K` kbd chip — the two hint chips now read as a visual pair (`⌘K ?`).
- `src/lib/i18n.tsx`: added `navShortcuts` STR key (`es: "Atajos de teclado (?)"`, `en: "Keyboard shortcuts (?)"`).
- Lint clean. Build green (6/6 routes prerendered, `out/` generated). TypeScript clean on touched files. No existing features removed; all accessibility semantics preserved (button has `aria-label` + `title`, modal has `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-label`, X button has its own `aria-label`).
- Next actions: (a) The `1–6` shortcut listed in the help overlay is documented as "only when demo is focused" but the actual demo listener (`src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx` line 87) is a global window listener that fires whenever not in a form field — if a future refinement wants the "only when demo focused" behavior, gate the TopNav listener on `document.activeElement` being inside `#demo-tablist` or the demo container. (b) The `?` button in the Navbar uses `t("navShortcuts")` for both `aria-label` and `title` — if a future audit wants them distinct (e.g. a shorter `aria-label="Atajos"` for screen readers), add a separate STR key. (c) `GlobalShortcuts` does not preventDefault on `T`/`L` when a modifier is held — this is intentional so browser-owned shortcuts like Cmd+T (new tab) keep working, but it also means Alt+T won't toggle theme; if a future spec wants Alt+T to work, drop `e.altKey` from the skip list. (d) The `body[data-shortcuts-help-open]` flag is set/unset in a useEffect cleanup — if a future refactor unmounts `ShortcutsHelp` while open (e.g. route change), the flag is still cleaned up correctly because the effect's cleanup runs on unmount.

Task ID: R4-g
Agent: social-trust
Task: Add animated trust badges + enhance social proof with live activity

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history through R4-f) and the four target-relevant files: `src/app/page.tsx` (page composition order — `<Footer />` → `<CookieConsent />` → `<BackToTop />`), `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` (brand column structure: logo → madeFor → pills → newsletter form → social links), `src/components/tj/CookieConsent.tsx` (z-50 / `bottom-4 left-4` / 2 s reveal — confirmed `bottom-20 z-30` would sit above it visually without stacking conflict), `src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx` (z-40 / `bottom-6 right-6` — confirmed no overlap on the left side), `src/components/marketing/SocialProof.tsx` (avatar hue pattern `rgb(var(--accent-base))` / `rgb(var(--pnl-pos))` / `rgb(var(--pnl-warn))` reused for the LiveActivity avatars), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (verified `posLocal` / `posOnce` keys exist for badge labels — reused instead of adding new keys; confirmed `useLang()` exposes `lang` + `t`), `src/app/globals.css` (confirmed `.glass`, `.rounded-card`, `.rounded-pill`, `.tnum`, `text-primary/secondary/tertiary`, `bg-tj-accent`, `text-tj-accent`, `bg-pnl-pos`, `text-pnl-pos/90` utilities all exist).
- Created `src/components/marketing/LiveActivity.tsx` (`"use client"`): a fixed bottom-left rotating "live activity" feed.
    · 8 deterministic activities (Marc R., Aina F., Daniel S., Pol V., Júlia M., Roger C., Núria P., Toni B.) — each with SVG-initials avatar, bilingual action ("acaba de registrar una operación" / "just logged a trade", etc.), bilingual relative timestamp ("hace 2 min" / "2 min ago"), and a hue token (`accent-base` / `pnl-pos` / `pnl-warn`) rotating for visual variety. NO Math.random in render — pure deterministic list, cycled by `idx` state.
    · Cycle: single `setInterval` of 5000 ms increments `idx = (idx + 1) % 8`. AnimatePresence `mode="wait"` with `initial={false}` ensures the first paint doesn't animate (avoids hydration flash). Each notification: 0.4 s slide-in from `x: -28`, ~4.2 s visible, 0.4 s slide-out — matches the spec's "hold 4 s, then slide out, then next slides in" cadence.
    · Outer container: `hidden sm:block fixed bottom-20 left-4 z-30 max-w-[280px]` — sits visually above CookieConsent (`bottom-4`), below BackToTop in z-order (`z-30 < z-40 < z-50`), hidden on mobile (too cramped). `aria-live="polite"` + `role="status"` + bilingual `aria-label` for screen readers.
    · Inner card: `glass rounded-card p-3 shadow-2xl` per spec. Top: "● LIVE" indicator — a green pulse dot (`animate-ping` ring + solid `bg-pnl-pos` core, 1.5×1.5 px) + "LIVE" wordmark in `text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.14em] font-semibold text-pnl-pos/90`. "LIVE" is kept universal (not translated) — it's a status indicator like "REC" or "ON-AIR" that reads identically in any language on trading terminals worldwide.
    · Body: avatar (28×28 circle, initials in `text-[10px] font-bold text-white`, hue from the Activity) + name (`font-semibold text-primary`) + action (`text-secondary`) + timestamp (`text-[10px] text-tertiary tnum` — `tnum` because the timestamp contains a numeric "X min").
    · State follows the project rule: `setIdx` is only called inside the `setInterval` callback (event-handler semantics), never synchronously inside the `useEffect` body. Cleanup clears the interval.
    · Reduced-motion: relies on the global `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule in `globals.css` which zeroes out animation/transition durations — Framer Motion will then snap between notifications without smooth motion, but the badge still rotates through the list. The `animate-ping` pulse dot also collapses to a single frame.
- Edited `src/app/page.tsx`: added `import { LiveActivity } from "@/components/marketing/LiveActivity";` (alphabetically after the existing `BackToTop` import on line 35) and rendered `<LiveActivity />` immediately after `<BackToTop />` inside the page wrapper `<div>`. Per spec.
- Edited `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`: added a row of 4 trust badges below the social links in the brand column.
    · New `TrustBadge` helper component (`icon: ReactElement` + `label: string` → `<span className="inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 glass rounded-pill px-3 py-1.5 text-tertiary text-xs transition-colors duration-200 hover:text-tj-accent hover:bg-tj-accent/10">`). Label wrapped in `<span className="tnum">` so any numeric glyphs ("100%", "ES + EN") render with tabular figures; `tnum` is a no-op on purely alphabetic labels ("Sin tracking") so it's harmless to apply uniformly.
    · Four badges wired per spec: (1) `LockIcon` + `t("posLocal")` ("100 % local" / "100 % local"), (2) `EyeOffIcon` + inline bilingual "Sin tracking" / "No tracking" (no existing i18n key for this — added inline `es ? … : …` ternary, matching the Footer's existing inline-bilingual pattern at the bottom status row), (3) `GlobeIcon` + literal "ES + EN" (universal, not translated), (4) `DollarIcon` + `t("posOnce")` ("Pago único" / "One-time payment").
    · Four new inline SVG icon components added after the existing `RSSIcon`: `LockIcon` (padlock: rect + U-shape shackle, 12×12 viewBox 0 0 16 16, `stroke="currentColor"`), `EyeOffIcon` (eye outline + pupil + diagonal slash, 12×12 viewBox 0 0 16 16, `stroke="currentColor"`), `GlobeIcon` (circle + horizontal equator + curved meridians, 12×12 viewBox 0 0 16 16, `stroke="currentColor"`), `DollarIcon` (vertical stem + S-curve, 12×12 viewBox 0 0 16 16, `stroke="currentColor"`). All use `currentColor` so they inherit `text-tertiary` and shift to `text-tj-accent` on hover — NO indigo/blue anywhere.
    · `ReactElement` type was already imported in Footer.tsx (line 3: `import { useState, type FormEvent, type ReactElement } from "react";`), so no new imports needed.
- Verified rules: `"use client"` on LiveActivity.tsx (uses `useState`/`useEffect`); Footer.tsx already `"use client"`; page.tsx is a server component (no hooks) — no change needed. NO indigo/blue introduced — all icons use `currentColor`, all tints use `tj-accent` / `pnl-pos` tokens. `tnum` applied to every numeric-containing span (the timestamp in LiveActivity; the badge labels in Footer — harmless on non-numeric labels, ensures tabular alignment for "100%" and "ES + EN"). Deterministic data (8-entry fixed list, no Math.random). Bilingual via `useLang()` + inline ES/EN ternaries + reused `posLocal`/`posOnce` i18n keys.
- Ran `cd /home/z/my-project && bun run lint 2>&1 | tail -15` → exit 0, `eslint .` ran clean (no errors, no warnings). 
- Ran `bun run build 2>&1 | tail -25` — first attempt hit a transient prerender error (`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'es')` at the `t()` function during prerender of "/") that appears to have been from a parallel agent's intermediate write state (R4-c/d/e were editing files concurrently — `navShortcuts` key was being added to i18n.tsx while Navbar.tsx was being updated to use it). Subsequent clean builds with `rm -rf .next` + `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=1536` (the sandbox has only 4 GB RAM with no swap — Turbopack needs the lower heap limit to avoid SIGKILL OOM) → ✓ Compiled successfully in 16.8 s / 19.4 s, all 7/7 routes (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`) prerendered as static content. Also confirmed the intermittent Turbopack manifest-cache race (`ENOENT … _buildManifest.js.tmp.XXX`) documented by R4-a — retrying with a clean `.next/` resolves it. No TypeScript errors on the touched files.

Stage Summary:
- `src/components/marketing/LiveActivity.tsx` (new file, ~180 lines): `"use client"` rotating live-activity feed. Fixed `bottom-20 left-4 z-30 max-w-[280px]` glass card, `hidden sm:block`. 8 deterministic activities (name + SVG-initials avatar + bilingual action + bilingual timestamp + hue token), cycling every 5 s via `setInterval`. AnimatePresence `mode="wait"` slides each card in from `x: -28` (0.4 s, `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease), holds ~4.2 s, slides out, then the next card enters. Top-of-card "● LIVE" indicator (green `animate-ping` pulse dot + universal "LIVE" wordmark, not translated). `aria-live="polite"` + `role="status"` + bilingual `aria-label` for SR users. Reduced-motion handled by the global `prefers-reduced-motion` rule.
- `src/app/page.tsx`: +`LiveActivity` import (line 38, alphabetically after `BackToTop`); `<LiveActivity />` rendered on line 90, immediately after `<BackToTop />` per spec.
- `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx`: +4 trust badges in the brand column, below the social-links row. New `TrustBadge` helper (`glass rounded-pill px-3 py-1.5 text-tertiary text-xs hover:text-tj-accent hover:bg-tj-accent/10`) + 4 inline SVG icons (`LockIcon`, `EyeOffIcon`, `GlobeIcon`, `DollarIcon` — all 12×12, `stroke="currentColor"`, no indigo/blue). Badges: "100 % local" (lock), "Sin tracking"/"No tracking" (eye-off), "ES + EN" (globe), "Pago único"/"One-time payment" (dollar). Labels wrapped in `tnum` for tabular-figure alignment on numeric glyphs.
- Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (7/7 routes prerendered as static content).
- Next actions: (a) The "LIVE" wordmark is intentionally universal (not translated) — it's a 4-letter status indicator that reads identically in any language on trading terminals; if a future localization push wants "EN DIRECTO" for ES, swap the hardcoded "LIVE" for `es ? "EN DIRECTO" : "LIVE"` (but note the 9-char ES variant will widen the header — adjust `gap-1.5` to `gap-1` or shorten to "DIRECTO" to keep the badge compact at `max-w-[280px]`). (b) The 8-activity list is hardcoded — if a future iteration wants more variety or real-feeling randomness, generate the list server-side with a seeded PRNG (e.g. mulberry32 with a fixed seed) so it stays deterministic across SSR/CSR but cycles through more than 8 entries. (c) The Footer trust badges reuse `posLocal` / `posOnce` i18n keys (same as the existing pills at the top of the brand column) — this is intentional reinforcement, not duplication, but if a future redesign wants distinct badge copy (e.g. "Datos locales" vs "100 % local"), add new i18n keys rather than repurposing. (d) The `bottom-20 left-4` placement puts LiveActivity 80 px from the bottom — exactly above where CookieConsent sits (16 px + ~120 px card height ≈ 136 px). If CookieConsent's content ever grows taller (e.g. longer copy or extra buttons), bump LiveActivity to `bottom-24` or `bottom-28` to maintain visual separation. (e) The build hit a transient i18n prerender error on the first attempt (parallel-agent write race during `navShortcuts` key addition) — this is not a regression from my changes; subsequent clean builds passed. If future builds hit the same `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'es')` at `t()`, check `src/lib/i18n.tsx` for any half-added keys (a `t("foo")` call site whose `foo:` key was never added to `STR`).

Task ID: R5-d
Agent: seo-enhance
Task: SEO enhancements — Open Graph, Twitter cards, structured data, meta tags

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full history) and the four target files: `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/app/sitemap.ts`, `src/app/robots.ts`, plus `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx` (to mirror its ES question/answer copy verbatim into the FAQPage JSON-LD). Verified `public/og.svg` exists (so OG_IMAGE swapped from `/og.png` → `/og.svg` per spec). Confirmed `public/img/08-cta-final-cinematic.webp` is the alternate OG candidate but stayed with `/og.svg` for brand consistency with the icon set.
- Audited existing `metadata` in `layout.tsx`: most fields were already correct (metadataBase, openGraph.url, openGraph.locale, twitter.card, SoftwareApplication.url, alternates.canonical all pointed at `https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb`). The deltas vs spec: (a) `OG_IMAGE` was `/og.png` (file doesn't exist; 404 on social scrapers); (b) `openGraph.alternateLocale` was a string `"en_US"` — Next.js typings accept `string | Array<string>`, spec wants the array form `["en_US"]`; (c) `twitter.images` was `[OG_IMAGE]` so it inherited the bad `/og.png` path.
- `src/app/layout.tsx` edits:
    · `OG_IMAGE` constant: `"/og.png"` → `"/og.svg"`. This propagates to both `openGraph.images[0].url` and `twitter.images[0]` (twitter.images already references `OG_IMAGE`).
    · `openGraph.alternateLocale`: `"en_US"` → `["en_US"]` (array form, matches spec and Next.js typings).
    · Added `faqSchema` constant: `FAQPage` schema with `mainEntity` array of 11 `Question` objects mirroring the ES copy from `FAQ.tsx` verbatim (pago único, datos seguros, Mac/Linux, importación, licencia perdida, actualizaciones, métodos de pago, reembolso, prueba, Core vs Pro, descuento estudiantes). Each `Question` has `name` + `acceptedAnswer.text`. Enables Google FAQ rich snippets.
    · Added `breadcrumbSchema` constant: `BreadcrumbList` with 2 `ListItem` entries — position 1 "Inicio" (SITE_URL) and position 2 "Trading Journal" (SITE_URL) — implementing the spec's "Home > (current page)" pattern for the single-page marketing site. Enables Google breadcrumb rich results.
    · Injected two new `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks in `<body>`, immediately after the existing SoftwareApplication JSON-LD, each using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(...) }}` (same pattern as the existing block). Order: SoftwareApplication → FAQPage → BreadcrumbList.
- `src/app/sitemap.ts` edits: added `SITE_URL` constant, swapped the entry URL from `https://tradingjournal.app` → `https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb`. `lastModified: new Date()` was already present (kept). `changeFrequency: "weekly"` + `priority: 1` retained.
- `src/app/robots.ts` edits: added `SITE_URL` constant, swapped sitemap URL from `https://tradingjournal.app/sitemap.xml` → `${SITE_URL}/sitemap.xml` (resolves to `https://mmortexx.github.io/JournalTradingWeb/sitemap.xml`). `rules: { userAgent: "*", allow: "/" }` retained. `force-static` export retained so the route prerenders.
- Verified with `bun run lint` (exit 0, no errors/warnings) and `bun run build` (7/7 routes prerendered, including `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml` as static). Build output confirmed both metadata routes generated cleanly.

Stage Summary:
- SEO metadata is now fully aligned with the GitHub Pages deployment URL across all four files (`layout.tsx`, `sitemap.ts`, `robots.ts`, plus the implicit `metadataBase` resolution for any relative OG image paths).
- Three JSON-LD blocks ship on every page: (1) `SoftwareApplication` with two `Offer`s (Core $29, Pro $49), `aggregateRating` (4.9 / 312 reviews), 9-item `featureList`, ES/EN `inLanguage`; (2) `FAQPage` with 11 Q&A pairs matching the visible FAQ component verbatim; (3) `BreadcrumbList` Home > Trading Journal. All three are eligible for Google rich results (software app, FAQ, breadcrumb).
- Open Graph: locale `es_ES` + alternate `en_US` (array form), `summary_large_image` Twitter card, OG image `/og.svg`, canonical = SITE_URL. The previous `/og.png` 404 is fixed.
- `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt` now point at the canonical GitHub Pages URL — Google Search Console will resolve the sitemap without redirects.
- Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (7/7 routes prerendered as static content, including `/robots.txt` + `/sitemap.xml`).
- Next actions: (a) Consider generating a 1200×630 raster OG image (PNG/JPG/WebP) — Facebook/LinkedIn/Twitter scrapers have inconsistent SVG support; `/og.svg` will work for some crawlers but a raster fallback at `/img/08-cta-final-cinematic.webp` (already exists) is the safer choice for max social-share compatibility. Swap `OG_IMAGE` to `/img/08-cta-final-cinematic.webp` if social previews render blank. (b) When the FAQ copy in `FAQ.tsx` changes, remember to mirror the change in `faqSchema` — they're intentionally duplicated (server-side JSON-LD can't import a client component's runtime array). A future refactor could extract the ES FAQ items into `src/lib/faq-items.ts` (shared by both the client component and the server JSON-LD) to eliminate the drift risk. (c) The `BreadcrumbList` has both items pointing to `SITE_URL` because this is a single-page marketing site — if multi-page routes (e.g. `/pricing`, `/demo`) are added later, generate the breadcrumb dynamically per-route via `generateMetadata` or a per-page `<script>` instead of hardcoding it in the root layout. (d) The `SoftwareApplication.aggregateRating` (4.9 / 312 reviews) is currently a static marketing claim — if real reviews are ever collected, wire this to a real review source to comply with Google's structured-data guidelines on self-serving reviews.
