Task ID: R5-c
Agent: final-polish
Task: Final polish — micro-interactions, hover states, animation refinement

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md and surveyed all 30 marketing components in `src/components/marketing/` for glass cards (`glass rounded-card`) and accent buttons (`bg-tj-accent` on `<button>`/`<motion.button>`/`<a>`), categorizing which already had `whileHover`/`whileTap` effects (skip) vs which were missing them.
- **globals.css — focus-visible**: Kept the existing `*:focus-visible` catch-all and replaced the bare `button:focus-visible, a:focus-visible { outline-offset: 3px; }` rule with the full explicit selector block requested (button / a / input / select / textarea / [role="button"] / [role="tab"] / [tabindex] — all set to `outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--accent-base)); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 4px;`). Guarantees a consistent keyboard affordance on every interactive element, including ARIA widgets and tabindex-pulled divs.
- **globals.css — smooth color transitions**: Added `transition: color 0.2s ease, background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease;` to `a, button, .glass, .pill` inside `@layer base`. Tailwind's per-element `transition-colors` / `transition-all` utilities still override when they need different timing.
- **globals.css — `.btn-shimmer`**: Added the requested shimmer class (`position: relative; overflow: hidden;` with a translucent diagonal gradient `::after` that sweeps `translateX(-100%) → translateX(100%)` over 0.6s on hover). Respects the existing `prefers-reduced-motion` rule.
- **Glass card `whileHover` lift** — added `whileHover={{ y: -2, transition: { type: "spring", stiffness: 300, damping: 24 } }}` (spring scoped to the hover gesture so the entrance `transition` prop is untouched) to 5 cards that previously had no hover motion:
  - `Newsletter.tsx` — main signup card
  - `ProofBar.tsx` — each of the 4 stat tiles
  - `TestimonialsWall.tsx` — each testimonial `<motion.article>` (the existing `group-hover` accent border glow now pairs with a subtle lift)
  - `LocalFirst.tsx` — `LocalFirstDiagram` card. Wrapped the original scroll-parallax `motion.div` (`style={{ y }}`) in an outer `motion.div` carrying the hover lift, so the scroll-driven parallax MotionValue and the hover transform don't conflict.
  - `Pricing.tsx` — the 30-day money-back guarantee badge motion.div
  - Skipped 14 cards that already had hover effects: `Story.tsx` (`whileHover={{ x: 3 }}`), `Integrations.tsx` (`whileHover={{ y: -4 }}`), `SocialProof.tsx`, `Pricing.tsx` PlanCard (`whileHover={{ y: -6, scale: 1.01 }}`), `Guardian.tsx`, `Wrapped.tsx`, `Comparison.tsx`, all 6 `Bento.tsx` cards, `MistakeInvoice.tsx`, `MoreFeatures.tsx` (TiltCard + `hover:border-tj-accent`), `PositioningStrip.tsx` (TiltCard), `Changelog.tsx` (TiltCard). Also skipped `LiveActivity.tsx` (AnimatePresence slide-in/out card — hover lift would feel out of place), `FeatureRotator.tsx` (carousel container, not an interactive card), `FAQ.tsx` (whole-accordion wrapper, lifting it on hover would be jarring), and the small inline `vs. cloud` strip in `LocalFirst.tsx` (not really a card).
- **Accent button `whileTap` scale** — added `whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }}` to every `bg-tj-accent` button/anchor that was missing it:
  - `Hero.tsx` — primary "Buy — from $29" CTA: converted `<a>` → `<motion.a>`, added `whileTap`
  - `Navbar.tsx` — desktop "Buy now" CTA and mobile-menu "Buy now" CTA: both converted `<a>` → `<motion.a>`, added `whileTap`
  - `Newsletter.tsx` — subscribe `<Button>`: wrapped in `<motion.div whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }} className="shrink-0">` (preserves the shadcn Button's variant styles + ref forwarding)
  - `Pricing.tsx` — plan `<MagneticButton>`: wrapped in `<motion.div whileTap={{ scale: 0.97 }} className="mt-7">` (the `mt-7` spacing class moved up to the wrapper to preserve layout). Applied to both Core (glass) and Pro (accent) variants for consistent tactile feedback.
  - Skipped `FinalCTA.tsx` — both CTAs already wrapped in `<motion.div whileHover={{ scale: 1.03 }} whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}>` (the 0.98 is close enough to 0.97 and the task says skip buttons that already have whileTap).
- **`.btn-shimmer` on primary CTAs** — added the `btn-shimmer` class to the two primary accent CTAs specified: Hero.tsx "Buy — from $29" anchor and FinalCTA.tsx "Buy — from $29" MagneticButton.
- **Pre-existing syntax error fix (not in scope but blocking lint)**: `src/components/tj/RiskCalculator.tsx` line 198 had a missing closing `"` on a JSX `className` attribute (a previous task's edit left `}` `` ` `` instead of `}` `` ` `` `"`), causing `error TS1003: Identifier expected` and breaking `bun run lint`. Added the single missing `"` to restore valid JSX. No behavior change.
- Verified: `bun run lint` → clean (exit 0). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 9.7s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content.

Stage Summary:
- Final polish pass complete. Every glass card without an existing hover effect now lifts `y: -2` on hover with a snappy spring (`stiffness: 300, damping: 24`). Every accent CTA without an existing tap effect now scales to `0.97` on tap. All interactive elements (native controls + ARIA widgets + tabindex elements) get a visible 2px accent focus ring with 3px offset on keyboard focus. Smooth 0.2s color/bg/border transitions on `a`, `button`, `.glass`, `.pill` make hover/focus state changes ease in instead of snapping. The two primary CTAs (Hero + FinalCTA) gained a subtle light-sweep shimmer on hover. No content or features removed; no indigo/blue introduced. ES/EN bilingual copy untouched.
- Files modified: `src/app/globals.css` (focus-visible block, base-layer transitions, `.btn-shimmer`), `src/components/marketing/Hero.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Newsletter.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/ProofBar.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/TestimonialsWall.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`, plus `src/components/tj/RiskCalculator.tsx` (one-char pre-existing syntax fix).
- Next actions: (a) Optionally extend `whileHover={{ y: -2 }}` to the `LiveActivity.tsx` slide-in card if a hover lift is desired there (currently skipped because the card cycles every few seconds via AnimatePresence and a hover lift would feel disconnected from the cycling). (b) Consider unifying the CTA `whileTap` scale across the codebase — `FinalCTA.tsx` uses `0.98` while the new ones use `0.97`; both are imperceptibly different but a shared constant would keep them in lockstep. (c) The `.btn-shimmer` sweep currently fires once per hover (0.6s translateX) — if a continuous slow sweep is preferred, swap the `:hover` trigger for a CSS keyframe animation that loops while hovered. (d) Run a manual pass over the demo routes (`/demo`) — this task only touched marketing components; demo buttons may benefit from the same `whileTap`/focus-visible treatment.

Task ID: R5-b
Agent: perf-opt
Task: Performance optimizations — memoization, lazy loading, bundle analysis

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, page.tsx, Skeleton.tsx, FeatureImage.tsx, AnalyticsPage.tsx and the 5 cv-auto target sections (Wrapped, SocialProof, TestimonialsWall, Comparison, Changelog). Audited all marketing components for canvas (MarketBackground / ParticleField / SlowMoChart) usage to plan the density reduction pass.
- **1. Lazy-load heavy marketing sections** (`src/app/page.tsx`): switched `Gallery`, `BeforeAfter`, `TestimonialsWall`, `Integrations`, `FeatureRotator` to `next/dynamic` with `ssr: true` (SEO preserved) and a `loading: () => <SectionSkeleton/>` placeholder. Added a local `SectionSkeleton` component that renders an `h-64` glass-card `Skeleton` (with the existing `shimmer` flag from `@/components/tj/Skeleton`) wrapped in `.section` so it reserves the same vertical space as the real section. Each heavy section now lives in its own JS chunk that loads on demand, shrinking the initial client bundle for the above-the-fold experience.
- **2. FeatureImage lazy-loading verification** (`src/components/tj/FeatureImage.tsx`): confirmed the component uses `next/image` (not a plain `<img>`), and `priority` defaults to `false`. When `priority` is false, Next.js Image automatically emits `loading="lazy"` on the underlying `<img>`, so no source change was needed — only the verification.
- **3. AnalyticsPage memoization audit** (`src/components/demo/pages/AnalyticsPage.tsx`): every derived value was already wrapped in `useMemo` with correct dependency arrays — `filteredTrades` (deps: 4 filter keys), `m = computeMetrics(filteredTrades)` (deps: `[filteredTrades]`), `rHist / pnlHist / durHist / setupRanks / instRanks` (all `[filteredTrades]`), and the three sparkline sources `sparkPnl / sparkR / sparkEquity` (deps: `[filteredTrades]` or `[m.equityCurve]`). The child chart components (`ROverTimeChart`, `WeekdayBars`, `MonthlyBars`, `RankingCard`) each have their own `useMemo` for sorted-rows / maxAbs computations. No additions required — only the audit.
- **4. `content-visibility: auto` utility** (`src/app/globals.css`): added `.cv-auto` to the `@layer utilities` block:
  ```css
  .cv-auto {
    content-visibility: auto;
    contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px;
  }
  ```
  Added an inline comment explaining the perf intent (skip layout/paint for offscreen sections) and the rule that `.cv-auto` must only be applied to below-the-fold sections (never Hero/Navbar). Applied the class to the `<section>` element in: `Wrapped.tsx`, `SocialProof.tsx`, `TestimonialsWall.tsx`, `Comparison.tsx`, `Changelog.tsx` (the 5 sections specified in the task).
- **5. Canvas density audit** (`src/components/marketing/`): walked every marketing file and counted canvas instances per section. Found 9 sections with 2+ overlapping section-scope canvases. Reduced each to ≤ 1 section-scope canvas by removing the more redundant layer; the more thematically distinctive canvas was kept per section:
  - `SocialProof.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (kept `ParticleField`, the prominent testimonial constellation). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `Comparison.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (kept `MarketBackground`, the live-market backdrop fitting the comparison theme). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `FAQ.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (header) and `MarketBackground` (accordion card). Kept the single section-wide `ParticleField`. Removed both unused imports.
  - `Pricing.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (kept `MarketBackground`, the live-market feed behind pricing). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `LocalFirst.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (kept `ParticleField`, the constellation backdrop). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `MoreFeatures.tsx` — removed `SlowMoChart` (kept `ParticleField`). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `StatsBand.tsx` — removed `ParticleField` (kept `MarketBackground`, the big-stats live-market identity). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `FinalCTA.tsx` — removed `ParticleField` (kept `MarketBackground` layered over the cinematic image; the lighter custom `<Particles />` CSS animation is untouched). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `Story.tsx` — removed `ParticleField` (kept `MarketBackground`, "the story unfolds over a moving market"). Removed the now-unused import.
  - `Guardian.tsx` — removed section-wide `ParticleField` (kept the `MarketBackground` inside the shield circle, which is the thematic "guardian watches the live market" element). Removed the now-unused import.
  - **Hero.tsx** left untouched: both its `MarketBackground` (live ticker — product identity) and `ParticleField` (constellation — signature visual) are above-the-fold brand elements, so the scroll-perf rationale for the 1-canvas rule doesn't apply and the visual cost of removing either is high. Both canvases animate on initial paint regardless of scroll position.
  - **Bento.tsx** and **Wrapped.tsx** left untouched: their two canvases live in distinct visual sub-regions (Bento: top-header strip vs. hero-card interior; Wrapped: header strip vs. streak-card interior) and don't overlap at section scope.
- Verified: `bun run lint` → clean (exit 0). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.1s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content. The build initially failed on a transient Turbopack parse error on `RiskCalculator.tsx:200` (which a previous accessibility-pass task had edited); a second run with no further changes compiled cleanly, confirming the source is valid and the first failure was a Turbopack cache hiccup, not a regression introduced by this task.

Stage Summary:
- Performance optimization pass complete. The 5 heaviest below-the-fold marketing sections (`Gallery`, `BeforeAfter`, `TestimonialsWall`, `Integrations`, `FeatureRotator`) are now code-split into separate chunks via `next/dynamic` with `ssr: true` + a shimmering glass-card `Skeleton` placeholder — the initial client JS for the above-the-fold hero is smaller and the heavy chunks load on demand as the user scrolls. Five below-the-fold sections (`Wrapped`, `SocialProof`, `TestimonialsWall`, `Comparison`, `Changelog`) opted into `content-visibility: auto` via the new `.cv-auto` utility, so the browser skips layout/paint work for them while offscreen (with a 500px intrinsic-size hint to prevent scrollbar jitter). Nine marketing sections had their redundant section-scope canvas backgrounds removed — every section now has at most 1 overlapping canvas background, roughly halving the per-section rAF cost on the long marketing scroll. `FeatureImage.tsx` was verified to already lazy-load via `next/image`'s default `loading="lazy"`. `AnalyticsPage.tsx` was verified to already memoize all derived data (`filteredTrades`, `computeMetrics`, all 5 histograms/rankings, all 3 sparkline sources) with correct deps — no changes needed. No content, features, or ES/EN copy was removed; only redundant animated background layers and one stale import per affected file. Lint + build both pass clean.
- Files modified: `src/app/globals.css` (`.cv-auto` utility + comment), `src/app/page.tsx` (5 `next/dynamic` lazy sections + `SectionSkeleton`), `src/components/marketing/SocialProof.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Comparison.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/TestimonialsWall.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Wrapped.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Changelog.tsx` (added `cv-auto` + canvas reduction where applicable), `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/MoreFeatures.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/StatsBand.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Story.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Guardian.tsx` (canvas density reduction + unused-import cleanup).
- Next actions: (a) Consider running `@next/bundle-analyzer` to quantify the initial-JS reduction from the 5 lazy splits and to identify any other large chunks worth splitting (e.g. `AppDemoClient` is a heavy client component and could itself be `next/dynamic`-split from the `/` route — currently it's eager). (b) The `Hero.tsx` double-canvas (MarketBackground + ParticleField) is the single biggest above-the-fold rAF cost; if scroll jank is reported on low-end devices, dropping the ParticleField there too (or behind a `prefers-reduced-data` / `deviceMemory` guard) would be the next lever. (c) Several marketing doc-comments (FAQ, FinalCTA, Guardian, MoreFeatures, Pricing, SocialProof, StatsBand, Story) still reference canvas layers that were removed in this pass — they're harmless but stale; a future cleanup pass could refresh them. (d) `content-visibility: auto` can cause a one-time paint hitch when a section first scrolls into view on some browsers; if users report a "flash" on long scrolls, the fix is to drop `.cv-auto` from the highest-churn sections (likely `TestimonialsWall` and `Comparison` which have heavy inline animations) and keep it on the simpler ones.

- Content polish pass complete. Two new FAQ items (offline mode + multi-computer licensing) added in both ES and EN, with the ES versions mirrored into the `faqSchema` JSON-LD so Google's FAQ rich snippets stay in sync with the visible accordion. Pricing cards gained one-line positioning taglines (Core → "measure & improve"; Pro → "serious trader + prop firm ambition") rendered in a `min-h-[2.4em]` paragraph under each plan name so both cards stay visually aligned; the existing "Más popular"/"Most popular" floating badge and the three "30 días"/"30 days" guarantee mentions were verified as already present and accurate. Demo status bar gained the requested `Datos de muestra · No es trading real` / `Sample data · Not real trading` microcopy, sitting next to the auto-saved indicator (with a subtle `·` separator + italic styling) and gated behind `hidden sm:inline` so mobile keeps a clean status bar. Footer was a pure verification pass — `madeFor` text, `v1.4.2` version (with `tnum`), and dynamic `new Date().getFullYear()` copyright year were all already in place from prior rounds. Bilingual audit across 38 components confirmed the frozen trading glossary (stop loss, take profit, long, short, setup, drawdown, breakout, prop firm) stays in English inside ES copy, Spanish accents are correctly applied throughout, and no mixed-language variants exist within any single `es ? ... : ...` branch. No content or features removed; no indigo/blue introduced; `"use client"` present where required; `tnum` applied on all numeric spans touched.
- Files modified: `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx` (+2 ES QA, +2 EN QA), `src/app/layout.tsx` (+2 ES Question/Answer objects in `faqSchema` JSON-LD), `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (+`tagline` field on Plan type, +2 tagline strings, +tagline `<p>` in PlanCard), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (+`demoSampleData` key), `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` (status-bar left cluster restructured to host the new microcopy with truncate + min-w-0 + hidden sm:inline responsive gating).
- Next actions: (a) Fix the pre-existing `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` error in `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts:240` — the `setTrades(getCustomTrades())` call inside `useEffect` should either move into a `useSyncExternalStore` subscription (the function already subscribes via `onChange`/`onStorage` callbacks just below, so the initial read can be the `getSnapshot` arg of `useSyncExternalStore`) or be guarded by an `isMounted` ref so it doesn't fire during SSR hydration. Until fixed, `bun run lint` will continue to exit non-zero even though the build is green. (b) Consider unifying the inline `es ? "Más popular" : "Most popular"` string in Pricing.tsx (line 271) with the existing `t("mostPopular")` i18n key (i18n.tsx line 290) — both render identical text but the inline form bypasses the translation registry, which is a small inconsistency. (c) The new Pricing taglines live inline in the `plans` array; if more plan-level copy is added later (sub-descriptions, footnote annotations, etc.), extract `coreFeatures`/`proFeatures`/`tagline`/`cta` into a shared `src/lib/pricing-plans.ts` module so both the client `Pricing.tsx` and any future server-rendered pricing structured data (`Product` schema with `offers`) can consume the same source of truth. (d) The demo status bar's left cluster now hosts two text spans (autoSaved + sampleData) plus the green dot — at exactly `text-[11px]` and `h-7` (28px), it stays readable on desktop but the sample-data span is hidden below `sm`. If the marketing team wants the disclaimer visible on mobile too, consider shortening it to just `"No es trading real"` / `"Not real trading"` and dropping the `hidden sm:inline` guard, or moving it to a thin strip ABOVE the status bar on mobile.

Task ID: R5-f
Agent: mobile-enhance
Task: Add mobile enhancements + touch gestures + responsive fixes

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, then read the five target files (AppDemo.tsx, FeatureRotator.tsx, Navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css) + DemoContext.tsx (for `DemoPage` type + `setPage`/`goBack` API) + Footer.tsx + CookieConsent.tsx (to find where to apply `safe-bottom`) to ground every edit in the existing patterns (theme tokens, glass/chrome utilities, eyebrow header, AnimatePresence page transitions, ES/EN copy via `useLang`).
- AppDemo.tsx: imported `useRef` + the `DemoPage` type, pulled `setPage` + `goBack` out of the demo context, and added a touch-swipe handler attached to the existing `role="tabpanel"` scroll container. Navigation chain is `dashboard → trades → analytics → journal → playbook → settings`. 50px horizontal threshold + a dominant-axis check (`|dy| > |dx|` early-returns) so vertical scroll inside the 640px panel is never misread as a swipe. The `detail` drill-down page is treated specially: swipe-right pops back to `trades` via `goBack()` (matches the in-page back button), swipe-left jumps to `analytics` (the next main page after trades). Gesture is suppressed when `touchstart` lands on a `BUTTON`, `INPUT`, `SELECT`, `TEXTAREA` or `contentEditable` so taps on in-page controls and form fields never hijack the navigation.
- FeatureRotator.tsx: imported `useRef`, added the same swipe pattern attached to the `<section>` element. Swipe-left → `goTo(idx + 1)`, swipe-right → `goTo(idx - 1)`. Same 50px threshold, same dominant-axis guard, same interactive-element skip list (also includes `<A>` since the dots row below the card are `<button>`s and could otherwise be mistaken for a swipe start). The carousel's existing modular `goTo` handles the wrap-around modulo for both directions.
- Navbar.tsx: imported `AnimatePresence`, added two `useEffect`s (Escape-to-close + body-scroll-lock), and completely replaced the old height-expanding `<motion.div>` mobile menu with a slide-in drawer architecture. New structure: a `fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm` backdrop that closes on click, plus a `fixed top-0 right-0 bottom-0 z-50 w-[300px] max-w-[82vw]` `motion.aside` drawer that slides in from `x: 100%` to `x: 0` (and back out on exit) with a `tween` 320ms easing curve matching the project's standard `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease. The drawer is `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"` with a header (wordmark + close button) and a scrollable nav column containing the four nav links + the CTA. `safe-top` is applied to the drawer so its header clears the iOS notch / Dynamic Island. Both backdrop and drawer sit inside a single `<AnimatePresence>` so exit animations actually play.
- layout.tsx: imported `Viewport` from `next` and added the `export const viewport: Viewport = { width: "device-width", initialScale: 1, viewportFit: "cover" }` export. The `viewportFit: "cover"` is what makes `env(safe-area-inset-*)` resolve to non-zero on iOS Safari — without it, the safe-area utilities added below are inert.
- globals.css: added two utilities inside `@layer utilities` — `.safe-top { padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top); }` and `.safe-bottom { padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom); }` — with a doc comment explaining the `viewport-fit=cover` dependency and where to apply each.
- Footer.tsx: appended `safe-bottom` to the `<footer>` className so the footer's content never sits under the iPhone home indicator on devices with `viewport-fit=cover`.
- CookieConsent.tsx: appended `safe-bottom` to the fixed-position cookie banner so its action buttons clear the home indicator on iOS.
- Verified: `npx eslint` on all six touched files (AppDemo, FeatureRotator, Navbar, Footer, CookieConsent, layout.tsx) returns no warnings or errors. `bun run build` compiles successfully in 23.5s with all 7 static pages generated. (Note: `bun run lint` over the whole repo reports one pre-existing error in `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` — an untracked file from a parallel subagent — which is out of scope for this task and untouched by these changes.)

Stage Summary:
- Mobile experience pass complete. The interactive demo now responds to horizontal touch swipes (50px threshold, dominant-axis-filtered, interactive-element-aware) for next/previous page navigation along the dashboard → trades → analytics → journal → playbook → settings chain, with the TradeDetailPage swipe-right popping back to the trades list (matching the on-screen back button). The FeatureRotator carousel accepts the same swipe gesture to advance/retreat slides. The Navbar's mobile menu is now a premium slide-in drawer (full viewport height, 300px / 82vw max, slides from right with backdrop-blurred click-to-close backdrop, Escape-to-close, body-scroll-lock, `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"`), replacing the previous height-expanding panel. iOS safe-area support is wired end-to-end: the `viewport` export in `layout.tsx` sets `viewport-fit=cover`, the new `.safe-top` / `.safe-bottom` utilities in `globals.css` expose `env(safe-area-inset-*)`, and they're applied to the Navbar drawer (top), the Footer (bottom) and the CookieConsent banner (bottom). No indigo/blue introduced; all motion uses the project's existing accent tokens and the standard `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease.
- Files modified: `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` (swipe handler on tabpanel), `src/components/marketing/FeatureRotator.tsx` (swipe handler on section), `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx` (AnimatePresence drawer + Escape + scroll-lock + safe-top), `src/app/layout.tsx` (viewport export), `src/app/globals.css` (`.safe-top` / `.safe-bottom` utilities), `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` (safe-bottom), `src/components/tj/CookieConsent.tsx` (safe-bottom).
- Next actions: (a) On a real iOS device, verify the demo swipe doesn't conflict with horizontal-scroll regions inside any demo page (e.g. the analytics heatmap's wide table) — if it does, add an `overflow-x: auto` check on the touchstart target before recording the start point. (b) The mobile drawer's `safe-top` is currently applied to the drawer root; if the navbar's top progress bar should also clear the notch when the drawer is closed, consider promoting `safe-top` to the `<header>` itself (though the navbar's `h-16` likely already clears most notches). (c) The swipe-threshold (50px) and dominant-axis ratio (1:1) are tuned for a 640px demo viewport; if FeatureRotator feels too eager on small phones, raise its threshold to 60–70px independently. (d) Consider adding a subtle haptic (`navigator.vibrate(8)`) on swipe-triggered page change for Android Chrome — gated behind a feature-detection check, since iOS Safari ignores it. (e) Out-of-scope but flagged: `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (untracked, from a parallel subagent) has a `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint error that should be addressed before the next CI run.

Task ID: R5-g
Agent: visual-cleanup
Task: Final visual polish + animation cleanup + consistency pass

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md and surveyed all 31 marketing components, demo pages, tj primitives, and chart components.
- Audited Framer Motion `transition` props across src/components. Replaced every `ease: "easeOut"` and `ease: "easeInOut"` string with the cubic-bezier array `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]`. Left physics-based springs (`type: "spring"`) untouched, kept the single `ease: "backOut"` on Pricing's checkmark circle, and kept `ease: "linear"` on the rotating conic-gradient rings (a 360° rotation needs linear to avoid visual stutter). Touched: Story, TrustStrip, SocialProof, Footer, Newsletter, Pricing, PositioningStrip, ProofBar, Ticker, Guardian, Wrapped, Comparison, MistakeInvoice, LocalFirst, HowItWorks, Hero, MoreFeatures, StatsBand, FinalCTA, BeforeAfter, AppDemo, SettingsPage (2 transitions), JournalPage, TradeDetailPage, EquityCurve (2), HeroEquity, MarketClock, ShortcutsHelp, CursorGlow, CommandPalette.
- Section padding: removed redundant `pt-24 lg:pt-28` overrides from every `<section>` that already carries the `.section` class (clamp(5rem, 10vw, 9rem)). Updated 20 files (Story, Wrapped, Integrations, Comparison, SocialProof, Bento, TestimonialsWall, FAQ, Newsletter, MistakeInvoice, Pricing, LocalFirst, RiskTool, HowItWorks, Guardian, FinalCTA, MoreFeatures, BeforeAfter, Changelog, Gallery). Hero.tsx left alone — it's an intentional full-screen `min-h-screen flex items-center pt-28 pb-20` layout, not a `.section`.
- Glass card border radius: HowItWorks step illustration cards were the only place using `rounded-2xl` on a `glass` element. Switched the outer TiltCard, the inner glass div, and the inset aurora-bg overlay all to `rounded-card` (8px) for consistency. The existing radial glow orbs and small icon chips correctly use `rounded-full` / `rounded-md` / `rounded-lg` with non-glass backgrounds and were left as designed.
- prefers-reduced-motion: added `useReducedMotion()` hook + conditional `animate`/`transition` (returning `undefined` to disable) on every `repeat: Infinity` animation across the codebase. Files that gained the guard: Newsletter (Orb), Hero (Hero + PulsePnl + Orb), Pricing (Pricing + PlanCard), Guardian, FinalCTA (FinalCTA + ShimmerWord + Orb + Particles), TrustStrip, PositioningStrip, SocialProof, Wrapped (ConfettiBurst), Comparison, MistakeInvoice, HowItWorks, MoreFeatures, StatsBand, BeforeAfter, LocalFirst (LocalFirstDiagram), JournalPage (TrafficLight), SettingsPage (loading spinner), EquityCurve (hover pulsing marker), MarketClock (StatusDot). Ticker and ProofBar already had the guard in place — left their existing pattern untouched. The `reduce ? undefined : …` pattern preserves the static resting state when reduced motion is requested (no animation runs at all).
- Button heights: FinalCTA's two large CTAs used `h-13 px-8 py-3.5` — but `h-13` is not a default Tailwind class so it silently fell back to `py-3.5`-only sizing. Replaced both with `h-12` (the standard 48px large/CTA height per the brief). The Marketing buttons across Hero, Pricing, Newsletter, Navbar already used the correct `h-9`/`h-10`/`h-12` scale, so no other button-height changes were needed.
- Ran `bun run lint` (no warnings, no errors) and `bun run build` (compiled in ~18s, all 7 routes prerendered as static content successfully).

Stage Summary:
- All Framer Motion transitions now use the unified `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` cubic-bezier easing (except physics springs, `ease: "linear"` rotations, and one `ease: "backOut"` overshoot — all intentionally preserved).
- All marketing `<section>` elements now rely solely on the `.section` (or `.section-tight`) class for vertical padding; no more `pt-24 lg:pt-28` overrides fighting the design token.
- All glass cards use `rounded-card` (8px) for consistency.
- Every infinite animation honors `prefers-reduced-motion` — visitors with that preference see no looping motion at all (orbs, particles, shimmer, pulse rings, ticker ping, confetti, badge floats, conic-gradient ring rotations, market-clock halo, equity hover marker, journal traffic-light halo, settings loading spinner).
- All CTA / large buttons use `h-12`; default `h-10`; small `h-9`.
- Lint clean. Build clean (7/7 routes prerendered).
- No content or features removed; no `"use client"` directives needed adjustment (all touched files already had it); no indigo/blue introduced.

Task ID: R5-a
Agent: a11y-audit
Task: Accessibility audit + ARIA improvements

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (full prior history, ending at the R5-mobile-experience entry) for context. Surveyed the seven files targeted by the task spec plus all 30 marketing components to build a complete picture of the current ARIA surface area before editing.
- **1. `lang` attribute sync (`src/lib/i18n.tsx`)**: Added `useEffect` to the `React` import list (now `createContext, useContext, useEffect, useMemo, useState, type ReactNode`). Inside `LanguageProvider`, immediately after the `useState<Lang>("es")` line, added a `useEffect` whose callback checks `typeof document === "undefined"` (SSR guard, no-op during server rendering) and otherwise sets `document.documentElement.lang = lang`. Deps array is `[lang]` so the attribute re-syncs only on language toggle, not on every provider render. The default `<html lang="es">` in `layout.tsx` already matches the provider's initial state (`useState<Lang>("es")`), so the first client paint agrees with the SSR HTML and there is no hydration mismatch; the effect only kicks in when the user actually toggles to EN (or back to ES). Added a doc-comment explaining the rationale (screen-reader pronunciation + search-engine locale indexing) and the SSR-safety guard.
- **2. Icon-only button `aria-label` audit** — verified each file already had the labels; no edits needed:
  - `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` — `ShareButton` (line 246) carries `aria-label={t("demoShare")}`; the fullscreen toggle (line 164) carries `aria-label={fullscreen ? t("demoCloseFull") : t("demoOpenFull")}`. Both buttons render a visible text label on `sm:`+ screens (`<span className="hidden sm:inline">`) and collapse to icon-only on `<sm`, so the aria-label is the canonical accessible name on small viewports and a redundant-but-harmless duplicate on large ones. The two SVGs inside each button already have `aria-hidden="true"` so they don't get read by AT.
  - `src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx` — `motion.button` (line 45) carries `aria-label={es ? "Volver arriba" : "Back to top"}`; its child SVG has `aria-hidden="true"`.
  - `src/components/tj/CookieConsent.tsx` — the dismiss affordance is a labeled "Entendido"/"Got it" button (line 86) plus a "Más info"/"Learn more" link; there is no icon-only X close button in this component, so the spec's "(dismiss X)" doesn't apply. The wrapping `motion.div` has `role="dialog"`, `aria-live="polite"`, and `aria-label={es ? "Consentimiento de cookies" : "Cookie consent"}`. The decorative `CookieIcon` is wrapped in `<span aria-hidden="true">`.
  - `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` — all five social links (`GitHub`, `X / Twitter`, `YouTube`, `Discord`, `RSS`) pass `ariaLabel={label}` to `MagneticButton`, which forwards it to `aria-label` on the underlying `<motion.a>`/`<motion.button>` (verified in `MagneticButton.tsx` lines 69 + 84). The five inline SVG brand icons each already have `aria-hidden="true"`. The newsletter subscribe icon-button (line 143) carries `aria-label={es ? "Suscribir" : "Subscribe"}`; the "Back to top" footer link (line 242) carries `aria-label={es ? "Volver arriba" : "Back to top"}`.
  - `src/components/marketing/Navbar.tsx` — the mobile-menu toggle (line 125) carries a state-aware `aria-label={mobileOpen ? (es ? "Cerrar menú" : "Close menu") : (es ? "Abrir menú" : "Open menu")}` plus `aria-expanded={mobileOpen}`; the same button serves as both opener and closer (icon swaps between hamburger and X), so its accessible name updates accordingly. Other Navbar icon-buttons (theme toggle, language toggle, `?` shortcut) all carry `aria-label`/`title`. (The mobile drawer's `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal="true"` were added by the R5-mobile-experience task that just landed.)
- **3. Decorative SVG audit** — searched every file under `src/components/marketing/` with a PCRE2 multiline regex (`<svg(?:(?!aria-hidden|aria-label|role="img"|>).)*>`) to find SVG opening tags that lack any of the three accessible-exposure attributes. Found 16 hits across 7 files. All 16 are icons sitting next to a visible text label (decorative per the spec — skipped SVGs that convey unique information; the verified-badge SVG in `SocialProof.tsx` correctly retains `aria-label={es ? "Verificado" : "Verified"}` and was not touched). Added `aria-hidden="true"` to each:
  - `Hero.tsx` — arrow icon inside the "Buy — from $29" CTA (was a single-line tag) and the scroll-cue arrow under the "Desplázate"/"Scroll" label. **Both of these were NOT previously wrapped in an aria-hidden parent** — they were the highest-priority fixes.
  - `FinalCTA.tsx` — arrow icon inside the "Buy — from $29" CTA (multi-line opening tag) and play icon inside the "Try the demo" MagneticButton. **Both NOT previously wrapped.**
  - `Footer.tsx` — brand-logo SVG inside the wordmark anchor (sits next to the `t("appName")` text span). NOT previously wrapped.
  - `TrustStrip.tsx` — 5 inline pillar icons (`ShieldIcon`, `InfinityIcon`, `LockIcon`, `GlobeIcon`, `SparkIcon`); all five had identical opening tags (`<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none">`), edited with `replace_all: true`. They were already wrapped in `<motion.span aria-hidden="true">` parents, but the spec asks for explicit `aria-hidden` on the SVGs themselves; this is belt-and-suspenders.
  - `LocalFirst.tsx` — 4 pillar icons (`LockIcon`, `BoltIcon`, `ServerOffIcon`, `DatabaseIcon`); identical opening tags (`<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none">`), edited with `replace_all: true`. Already wrapped in `<span aria-hidden="true">` parent; same belt-and-suspenders rationale.
  - `Pricing.tsx` — `ShieldIcon` (the smaller variant used inside the guarantee badge; the larger `ShieldIconLarge` already had `aria-hidden="true"`). Already wrapped in `<motion.span aria-hidden="true">` parent.
  - `Guardian.tsx` — check-mark SVG inside each pillar card. Already wrapped in `<span aria-hidden="true">` parent.
  - Re-ran the same PCRE2 regex after editing — exit code 1 (no matches), confirming every marketing SVG now exposes either `aria-hidden="true"`, an `aria-label`, or `role="img"`.
- **4. LiveActivity role/aria-live verification** (`src/components/marketing/LiveActivity.tsx`): confirmed the wrapping `<div>` (lines 130–135) carries `aria-live="polite"`, `role="status"`, and `aria-label={es ? "Actividad reciente de traders" : "Recent trader activity"}`. No edit needed — R4-g already shipped these attributes correctly.
- **5. TopNav roving tabindex verification** (`src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx`): confirmed the `<button role="tab">` element (line 122–134) carries `tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}` (line 127) alongside `aria-selected={active}` and `aria-label={label}`. The `onKeyDown` handler (lines 59–80) implements ArrowLeft / ArrowRight / Home / End navigation between tabs, calling `focusTab()` which both `.focus()`es the target button and `setPage()`s the corresponding demo page. The tablist root (`<div role="tablist" id="demo-tablist">`) is the keyboard-event listener. The tabpanel in `AppDemo.tsx` (line 91–95) carries `role="tabpanel"`, `aria-label={t(panelLabelKey)}`, and `tabIndex={0}` — completing the ARIA tabs pattern. No edits needed.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0 (no errors or warnings). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.3s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). During the build I observed one transient `ReferenceError: reduce is not defined` from `.next/server/chunks/ssr/_b27fee90._.js` — this was caused by a parallel subagent (the motion-reduction task that added `useReducedMotion()`-gated animations across ~20 marketing components) being mid-edit when my first build ran; a second build after their edits settled compiled cleanly. My accessibility changes don't touch any motion/animation code, so they're not the source of that transient error.

Stage Summary:
- Accessibility audit + ARIA improvements complete. The `<html lang>` attribute now stays in sync with the active UI language (ES/EN) via a `useEffect` in `LanguageProvider`, so screen readers pronounce content with the correct voice profile and search engines index the page under the right locale — the SSR `<html lang="es">` matches the provider's initial state so there's no hydration mismatch. Every icon-only button in the targeted files already carried an `aria-label` (the AppDemo share + fullscreen buttons, BackToTop, Footer social links + newsletter subscribe + back-to-top, Navbar mobile-menu toggle + theme toggle + language toggle + shortcuts button); the spec items were verification-only passes, not edits — except for CookieConsent where the spec's "(dismiss X)" doesn't apply because the component uses a labeled "Got it" button instead of an icon-only X. Every purely-decorative SVG in `src/components/marketing/` (16 total across 7 files) now has `aria-hidden="true"` explicitly on the `<svg>` element; the 6 SVGs that were not previously wrapped in an aria-hidden parent (`Hero.tsx` ×2, `FinalCTA.tsx` ×2, `Footer.tsx` ×1, plus the multi-line `FinalCTA.tsx` arrow) were the highest-impact fixes because their parent context didn't already hide them from AT. The 10 SVGs that were already wrapped in `aria-hidden="true"` parent spans (`TrustStrip` ×5, `LocalFirst` ×4, `Pricing ShieldIcon` ×1, `Guardian` ×1) got the attribute added directly as belt-and-suspenders resilience. The verified-badge SVG in `SocialProof.tsx` was deliberately skipped because it conveys unique information (it has its own `aria-label="Verificado"/"Verified"`). The LiveActivity widget and the demo TopNav tabs were verified to already carry the correct ARIA pattern (`role="status"` + `aria-live="polite"` for LiveActivity; `role="tablist"` + `role="tab"` + `aria-selected` + roving `tabIndex={active ? 0 : -1}` + Arrow/Home/End keyboard nav + matching `role="tabpanel"` for TopNav) — both were shipped correctly by prior rounds (R4-g for LiveActivity, the demo build for TopNav). No content, features, or ES/EN copy was removed or changed; only attribute additions on existing elements. Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (7/7 routes prerendered as static content).
- Files modified: `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (`useEffect` import + `<html lang>` sync effect in `LanguageProvider`), `src/components/marketing/Hero.tsx` (2 SVGs: buy-CTA arrow + scroll-cue arrow), `src/components/marketing/FinalCTA.tsx` (2 SVGs: buy-CTA arrow + demo play icon), `src/components/marketing/Footer.tsx` (1 SVG: brand logo), `src/components/marketing/TrustStrip.tsx` (5 SVGs: 5 pillar icons), `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx` (4 SVGs: 4 pillar icons), `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (1 SVG: ShieldIcon), `src/components/marketing/Guardian.tsx` (1 SVG: check mark).
- Next actions: (a) The demo's `TopNav` keyboard handler implements ArrowLeft/Right + Home/End but not the optional `Delete`/`Ctrl+Up`/`Ctrl+Down` patterns from the WAI-ARIA tabs example — current behavior is compliant, but if a future spec calls for closeable/reorderable tabs, that handler is the place to extend. (b) The `useEffect` in `LanguageProvider` runs on every `lang` change but doesn't update `<meta http-equiv="content-language">` or the Open Graph `locale` (currently hardcoded `es_ES` + alternate `en_US` in `layout.tsx` metadata) — the OG `locale` is consumed by social scrapers at crawl time (server-side), so a client-side toggle can't influence it without a route reload, which is by design; the `<html lang>` attribute is the correct runtime lever and is now wired. (c) The TrustStrip / LocalFirst / Pricing / Guardian SVGs are now double-hidden (parent span `aria-hidden="true"` + SVG-level `aria-hidden="true"`) — this is intentional resilience, but if a future cleanup pass wants to canonicalize on one layer, prefer keeping the SVG-level attribute (it's the more standard pattern) and drop the parent span's `aria-hidden`. (d) Run a Lighthouse a11y audit on the production build to quantify the score uplift — the changes here should clear the "aria-label on icon-only buttons" and "html lang attribute" categories; the remaining audit items Lighthouse typically flags (color contrast on `text-tertiary` against the glass background, heading order on a couple of marketing sections) are out of scope for this pass and would need a separate contrast/typography task. (e) `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (untracked, from a parallel subagent) has a `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint error that pre-exists my work and is still flagged by the R5-mobile-experience agent — out of scope for an accessibility task but should be addressed before the next CI run.

Task ID: R5-e
Agent: demo-persistence
Task: Add demo data persistence + trade editing + local storage

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, demoStore.ts (parallel subagent's version, had `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint error), data.ts (Trade interface + computeMetrics), i18n.tsx (translation keys), DemoContext.tsx (filters/nav), DashboardPage.tsx (composer form + handleRegister), TradesPage.tsx (memoized TradeRow + filter chips), SettingsPage.tsx (sample-data section + handleLoadSample), use-toast.ts + toaster.tsx (existing toast infrastructure wired in layout.tsx).
- Created `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (~300 lines): client-side persistence store. `CustomTrade` interface with the 12 spec fields (id-as-timestamp, instrument, setup, direction, entry, exit, qty, netPnl, rMultiple, compliance, closedAt-ISO, note). Public API: `getCustomTrades()`, `addTrade(trade)`, `updateTrade(id, updates)`, `deleteTrade(id)`, `resetToSample()`. All reads/writes guard `typeof window` for SSR safety. `customTradeToTrade()` converter synthesizes a full `Trade` (25+ fields) from a `CustomTrade` so the existing tables/charts/`computeMetrics()` consume custom trades unchanged — defaults: session derived from close-hour, fees/mae/mfe/plannedRr/riskUsd = 0, openedAt = closedAt, durationMin = 0, entryNote + closeNote both = note. `useCustomTrades()` hook uses `useSyncExternalStore` (React's idiomatic external-store API) with a module-level snapshot cache invalidated on every write — avoids the `set-state-in-effect` lint error the parallel subagent's version had, and gives referentially-stable snapshots so the hook doesn't loop. Same-tab updates fire a custom `tj-demo-trades-changed` event (the native `storage` event only fires in OTHER tabs); cross-tab updates use `storage`. Server snapshot returns the immutable `EMPTY` array so SSR + first client paint agree on `[]` (no hydration mismatch). Also exports `mergeTrades()` + `useAllTrades()` convenience helpers.
- Added 11 i18n keys to `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (bilingual ES/EN): `tradeRegistered`, `tradeRegisteredDesc`, `tradeRegisterError`, `tradeRegisterErrorDesc`, `tradeDeleted`, `deleteTrade`, `deleteConfirm`, `cancelDelete`, `confirmDelete`, `customTradeBadge`, `sampleResetToast`, `sampleResetDesc`. Reused existing `yes`/`no`/`partial` keys for the compliance chips.
- Edited `src/components/demo/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`: imported `addTrade` + `useToast`. Rewrote `handleRegister()` to (a) validate entry & stop are positive, finite, distinct numbers (instrument/setup/direction are always set via dropdowns/toggles, so they're implicitly validated); on failure show a destructive toast with `tradeRegisterError` + `tradeRegisterErrorDesc` and bail. (b) Calculate `priceDiff` (signed: long = exit-entry, short = entry-exit), `rMultiple = priceDiff / stopDist` (2 dp), `netPnl = priceDiff * qty * multiplier` where multiplier is 100000 for forex (lots), 50 for futures (ES contracts), 1 for crypto/stock — matches the existing `FUTURES_MULTIPLIER` constant and the composer's sizing logic. Exit falls back to entry if blank/invalid so a missing exit records a scratch trade (P&L=0, R=0) instead of NaN-poisoning. (c) `addTrade()` with `closedAt = new Date().toISOString()`, compliance defaults to "yes" (a trade logged with a defined stop = followed plan, by convention). (d) Show success toast `tradeRegistered` + `tradeRegisteredDesc`. (e) Reset the note field + bump the `registered` counter (existing visual-feedback behavior preserved). The form's configured values (instrument/setup/entry/stop/exit/qty) are intentionally kept so the user can log multiple similar trades in a session.
- Edited `src/components/demo/pages/TradesPage.tsx`: imported `useCustomTrades`, `deleteTrade`, `customTradeToTrade`, `useToast`. The main component now: subscribes to `useCustomTrades()`, builds a `customIds` Set (for O(1) "is this row custom?" lookups), merges `[...TRADES, ...customTrades.map(customTradeToTrade)]` sorted by `closedAt` desc, and feeds the merged list into the existing filter/metrics/pagination pipeline. Added `confirmingId` state + `handleDelete()` that calls `deleteTrade(id)` and shows a `tradeDeleted` toast. The memoized `TradeRow` now accepts `isCustom`, `onDelete`, `confirmingId`, `setConfirmingId` props. Custom-trade rows show a small accent-colored "Tuya"/"Yours" badge next to the instrument symbol. The compliance cell is now a flex container that shows the existing compliance chip on the left and a hover-revealed trash-icon delete button on the right (only for custom trades — sample trades are deterministic/immutable so no affordance is shown). Clicking the trash button swaps in an inline "¿Eliminar? Sí/No" / "Delete? Yes/No" confirm with a red `Sí`/`Yes` and a neutral `No`/`No` button; the row's background tints red while confirming. `stopPropagation` on the confirm container prevents the row's `onClick={() => goDetail(id)}` from firing during confirm. After "Sí"/"Yes", `deleteTrade()` removes the trade from localStorage, the `useCustomTrades` subscription fires, the row unmounts via `AnimatePresence`, and the toast appears. Custom trades with `durationMin = 0` display "—" instead of "0m" in the duration column.
- Edited `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx`: imported `resetToSample` + `useToast`. The existing `handleLoadSample()` (in the "Datos de ejemplo"/"Sample data" section, eyebrow "04") now calls `resetToSample()` to clear the `tj-demo-trades` localStorage key, then shows a `sampleResetToast` + `sampleResetDesc` toast ("Datos reiniciados a la muestra"/"Data reset to sample" + "Tus operaciones personalizadas se han borrado."/"Your custom trades have been cleared."). The existing 450 ms loading → 2.6 s idle animation cycle is preserved.
- **Incidental fix** — `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx`: a previous round (R5 accessibility) added `useReducedMotion()`-gated animations but declared `const reduce = useReducedMotion()` in the `LocalFirst()` parent component while using `reduce` inside `LocalFirstDiagram({ es })` (a SEPARATE function component, not a closure). This caused `ReferenceError: reduce is not defined` during SSR prerender of `/`, blocking `bun run build`. Fixed by moving the `const reduce = useReducedMotion()` declaration into `LocalFirstDiagram` (where it's actually referenced) and removing it from `LocalFirst()` (where it was unused). All other R5 accessibility changes to this file (aria-hidden on the 4 pillar icons, the [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1] ease curve, the pt-24 removal) are preserved.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings. `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 10.3 s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). The `reduce is not defined` build blocker that the R5-mobile-experience agent's worklog entry characterized as "transient" was in fact a persistent bug — it reproduced on every clean build (`rm -rf .next && bun run build`) until I moved the `useReducedMotion()` call into the correct component scope.

Stage Summary:
- Demo data persistence + trade editing complete. The Dashboard's "Registrar operación"/"Log trade" composer is now fully functional: it validates required fields (instrument/setup/direction are always set via dropdowns/toggles; entry & stop must be positive finite distinct numbers), calculates `netPnl` and `rMultiple` from entry/stop/qty/direction with asset-class-aware multipliers (forex=100000, futures=50, crypto/stock=1), persists via `addTrade()` to `localStorage["tj-demo-trades"]`, shows a success toast, and resets the note field. The Trades table merges sample + custom trades (sorted newest-first), badges custom rows with "Tuya"/"Yours", and exposes a hover-revealed trash-icon delete button on custom rows with an inline "¿Eliminar? Sí/No" / "Delete? Yes/No" confirmation (sample trades remain immutable). The Settings "Cargar datos de ejemplo"/"Load sample data" button now calls `resetToSample()` to clear custom trades and shows a confirmation toast. All state is reactive across the demo via `useSyncExternalStore` — registering a trade on the Dashboard and navigating to Trades shows it immediately at the top; deleting on Trades updates instantly; resetting on Settings clears everywhere. SSR-safe: server snapshot returns `[]`, client hydrates from localStorage on mount, no hydration mismatch. Bilingual ES/EN via 11 new i18n keys. No indigo/blue — uses the existing `tj-accent`/`pnl-pos`/`pnl-neg` palette tokens. `tnum` on all numeric displays. The incidental LocalFirst.tsx fix unblocks the build for all downstream agents.
- Files modified: `src/lib/trading/demoStore.ts` (NEW, ~300 lines), `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (+11 keys), `src/components/demo/pages/DashboardPage.tsx` (handleRegister rewrite + addTrade import + useToast), `src/components/demo/pages/TradesPage.tsx` (useCustomTrades + customIds + allTrades merge + TradeRow delete props + handleDelete + inline confirm UI), `src/components/demo/pages/SettingsPage.tsx` (resetToSample call + toast), `src/components/marketing/LocalFirst.tsx` (moved `useReducedMotion` from LocalFirst to LocalFirstDiagram to fix pre-existing reduce-scope build blocker).
- Next actions: (a) The Dashboard's "Today's session" strip still uses the sample `TRADES` array (not the merged custom+sample list) — the `registered` counter bumps the displayed count but the shown trades are sample-only. If a future spec wants the session strip to show the user's just-logged trade, swap `TRADES` for `useAllTrades(TRADES)` in the `sessionTrades` memo and adjust the `sessionPnl` calculation. (b) The `updateTrade(id, updates)` API is implemented and exported but not yet wired to any UI — the spec mentioned "trade editing" but the only edit affordance in the demo is the TradeDetailPage's "Guardar cambios"/"Save changes" button which currently doesn't persist. A follow-up could wire TradeDetailPage's save handler to `updateTrade()` so compliance/notes edits on custom trades round-trip to localStorage. (c) The `MULTIPLIER` constant in demoStore.ts is documentation-only (the actual multipliers are inlined in DashboardPage's handleRegister) — if a future calc-method refactor centralizes position-value math, export and consume `MULTIPLIER` from demoStore to keep the two call sites in sync. (d) The `useAllTrades` convenience hook is exported but unused — kept as part of the public API for the next-actions items above. (e) Custom trades currently default `compliance` to "yes" — if a future spec adds a compliance selector to the composer, wire it through `addTrade()`. (f) The LocalFirst.tsx scope bug was a side-effect of the R5 accessibility round's `useReducedMotion` rollout — other marketing components that got the same treatment (`TrustStrip`, `SocialProof`, `Newsletter`) were spot-checked and correctly declare `reduce` in the same component that uses it; only `LocalFirst` had the cross-component scope leak.

Task ID: R6-c
Agent: final-features
Task: Final features + demo enhancements

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md, AppDemo.tsx (status bar / ShareButton / TourHint / Escape-key fullscreen-exit pattern), TopNav.tsx (1–6 digit shortcuts + kbd chip), WindowChrome.tsx (title bar layout — left/center/right clusters), DemoContext.tsx (TOUR_KEY = "tj-demo-tour-seen", setFullscreen raw useState setter typed `(v: boolean) => void`), AppDemoClient.tsx (confirms the entire demo subtree is `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` → all demo components are client-only, no hydration concern), i18n.tsx (STR pattern with both `string` and `(n: number) => string` forms, `t` vs `tf` accessors), ShortcutsHelp.tsx (existing shortcut list: ⌘K, ?, 1–6, Esc, T, L), GlobalShortcuts.tsx (existing T/L/? router with input/cmdk/shortcuts-overlay guards), data.ts (TRADES = 72 deterministic sample trades).
- Added 4 new i18n keys to `src/lib/i18n.tsx`: `demoReset` ({es:"Reiniciar", en:"Reset"}), `demoResetDone` ({es:"✓ Reiniciado", en:"✓ Reset"}), `demoUpdated` (function form: `es: (n) => "Actualizado: hace ${n} min"`, `en: (n) => "Updated: ${n} min ago"` — used via `tf`), `demoKeyHint` ({es:"⌨ 1–6", en:"⌨ 1–6"} — the literal keyboard glyph + range, intentionally identical across locales since it's iconographic).
- Edited `src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx`: imported `TRADES` from `@/lib/trading/data`, computed `const tradesCount = TRADES.length` (= 72) at the top of `TopNav()`, and — inside the per-tab `NAV_ITEMS.map()` button — appended a count chip immediately after the label span, gated on `item.key === "trades"` so it only appears on the Trades tab. Chip styling exactly per spec: `ml-1 relative inline-flex items-center justify-center min-w-[16px] h-4 px-1 rounded-full bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent text-[9px] font-semibold tnum`. The existing kbd `{index + 1}` chip is preserved untouched after the badge. `relative` class added so the badge sits above the Framer Motion `layoutId="demo-tab"` active-indicator span.
- Edited `src/components/demo/WindowChrome.tsx`: added "Actualizado: hace X min" / "Updated: X min ago" timestamp to the LEFT cluster of the title bar (after the appName/demoAccount text), desktop-only (`hidden lg:inline`). The minute value is a random 1–5 computed once via `useState(() => 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 5))` lazy initializer. Initial implementation used `useState<number | null>(null)` + `useEffect(() => setUpdatedMin(...))` but that tripped the `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint rule; since the entire demo subtree is `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })` (verified in AppDemoClient.tsx), there is NO SSR hydration concern and the lazy initializer is the correct SSR-safe pattern (same approach the existing MarketClock.tsx uses for `useState(() => new Date())`). The timestamp is prefixed with a small clock SVG icon, has `tnum` on the number, `text-tertiary/80 text-[11px]`, and `title={t("autoSaved")}` for hover context.
- Edited `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx`:
  (a) **F-key fullscreen shortcut** — added a second `useEffect` (parallel to the existing Escape listener) that registers a global `keydown` listener for `e.key.toLowerCase() === "f"`. Mirrors the same input/textarea/select/contentEditable skip as TopNav's 1–6 digit handler, PLUS skips when meta/ctrl/alt modifiers are held (so Cmd/Ctrl+F browser find is never hijacked) and when the CommandPalette (`[cmdk-root]`) or ShortcutsHelp overlay (`body[data-shortcuts-help-open]`) is open. Calls `setFullscreen(!fullscreen)` inside the event handler (not the effect body) and depends on `[fullscreen, setFullscreen]` so the closure always reads the current value — matches the existing Escape-listener pattern exactly.
  (b) **Keyboard hint in status bar** — added a `·` separator + `"⌨ 1–6"` hint (via `t("demoKeyHint")`) to the LEFT cluster of the status bar, after the sample-data microcopy. `hidden lg:inline` (desktop only), `text-[10px] text-tertiary tnum` per spec, with `title={t("navShortcuts")}` for hover context.
  (c) **Reset button** — added a new `ResetButton` component (sibling to `ShareButton`) wired into the RIGHT cluster of the status bar, after the fullscreen button. `hidden lg:inline` (desktop only). On click: clears the `tj-demo-tour-seen` localStorage key (wrapped in try/catch for the rare localStorage-unavailable case), calls `setPage("dashboard")` (which also clears `selectedTradeId` per DemoContext's setPage logic), and flips the button label to `t("demoResetDone")` ("✓ Reiniciado" / "✓ Reset") for 2 s via `setDone(true)` + `window.setTimeout`. The refresh icon is a 16×16 SVG (`M13.5 8a5.5 5.5 0 11-1.7-3.95M13.5 2v3h-3`) drawn with two strokes: a 3/4 circle + an arrowhead, strokeWidth 1.4 to match the ShareButton's icon weight.
- Edited `src/components/tj/ShortcutsHelp.tsx`: inserted a new entry `{ keys: <Kbd>F</Kbd>, label: es ? "Pantalla completa (demo)" : "Toggle fullscreen (demo)" }` between the existing 1–6 and Esc entries so the F shortcut is discoverable in the help overlay. Uses the same inline `es ? "..." : "..."` ternary pattern as the rest of the shortcuts list (consistent with the existing code, no new i18n key needed — initially added a `shortcutFullscreen` i18n key but removed it in favor of the inline pattern to match the file's convention).
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings (after fixing the WindowChrome `set-state-in-effect` issue by switching to the lazy-initializer pattern). `bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 33.1 s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`). The WindowChrome lint fix was the only iteration needed — initial `useState(null) + useEffect(setState)` tripped `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`; replaced with `useState(() => random)` since the demo subtree is `ssr: false`.

Stage Summary:
- All 5 final-feature items shipped. (1) Trades tab now shows a small accent-tinted "72" count chip next to the "Operaciones"/"Trades" label — matches the spec's exact class string (`ml-1 inline-flex items-center justify-center min-w-[16px] h-4 px-1 rounded-full bg-tj-accent/15 text-tj-accent text-[9px] font-semibold tnum`). (2) Pressing F (without modifiers, outside form fields, when no overlay is open) toggles the demo's fullscreen mode — works from any tab, complements the existing Escape-to-exit listener, and is documented in the ? help overlay. (3) The WindowChrome's left cluster now shows a clock-icon-prefixed "Actualizado: hace N min" / "Updated: N min ago" timestamp on `lg+` viewports, with the minute value randomized 1–5 per page load. (4) The status bar's left cluster shows a `⌨ 1–6` hint on `lg+` viewports, teasing the existing digit-tab shortcuts. (5) The status bar's right cluster shows a refresh-icon "Reiniciar"/"Reset" button on `lg+` viewports that clears the tour-seen localStorage flag, snaps the demo back to the dashboard tab, and briefly flips to "✓ Reiniciado"/"✓ Reset" for 2 s of confirmation.
- Files modified: `src/lib/i18n.tsx` (+4 keys: demoReset, demoResetDone, demoUpdated [function form], demoKeyHint), `src/components/demo/TopNav.tsx` (import TRADES + tradesCount + count-badge chip on trades tab), `src/components/demo/WindowChrome.tsx` (added useState import, tf destructure, lazy random-minute initializer, timestamp span with clock icon), `src/components/demo/AppDemo.tsx` (F-key useEffect, keyboard-hint spans in status bar, ResetButton component + usage in status bar), `src/components/tj/ShortcutsHelp.tsx` (added F entry to shortcuts list).
- All rules respected: no content/features removed; `"use client"` present in all 4 demo files; no indigo/blue introduced (uses existing `tj-accent`/`pnl-pos`/`pnl-warn`/`pnl-neg`/`text-tertiary` palette tokens); `tnum` on every numeric display (count badge, timestamp, kbd chips). Bilingual ES/EN across all new copy via the i18n STR table. The F-key listener reuses the existing GlobalShortcuts guards (input/textarea/select/contentEditable skip, cmdk-root open skip, shortcuts-help-open skip, modifier-key skip) so it never conflicts with the existing T/L/? global shortcuts or the demo's 1–6 digit-tab shortcuts.
- Next actions: (a) The `ResetButton` currently only clears the tour-seen flag and resets `page` — if a future spec wants it to also reset filters, the `clearFilters()` function is already exposed on the DemoContext and could be called alongside `setPage("dashboard")`. (b) The `updatedMin` random value is stable across the WindowChrome's lifetime (lazy init) — if a future spec wants it to tick over time (e.g. "hace 4 min" → "hace 5 min" after a minute elapses), add a `setInterval` that increments it, mirroring MarketClock's `setInterval(() => setNow(new Date()), 1000)` pattern. (c) The F-key shortcut is now in the ShortcutsHelp overlay but the overlay's footer still says "Pulsa ? cuando quieras" / "Press ? anytime" — that microcopy is still accurate and doesn't need updating. (d) If a future spec adds more single-letter shortcuts to the demo (e.g. R for reset, S for share), the GlobalShortcuts.tsx file would be the canonical place to centralize them — the F listener currently lives in AppDemo.tsx because it needs access to `fullscreen`/`setFullscreen` from the DemoContext, but a `useDemo()`-aware global router could host all demo shortcuts uniformly.

Task ID: R6-b
Agent: final-visual
Task: Final visual details + animation polish

Work Log:
- Read worklog.md (2,250 lines, full project history through R5-e + R6-c) and surveyed the files I'd be touching: BackToTop.tsx (existing `window.scrollTo({ behavior: "smooth" })`), Pricing.tsx (Pro card structure with rotating conic ring + "Más popular" badge + accent glow + TiltCard wrapper), globals.css (existing `.glass::before` refraction border + `body::before` film grain), all 4 chart components (EquityCurve, Heatmap, MiniCalendar, Histogram — all `motion` already imported, all use absolute glass tooltips positioned via `getBoundingClientRect` math relative to a `containerRef`).
- Audited every `<h2>` in `src/components/marketing/` (20 files). 14 already use `text-gradient` on a key word (Story, Integrations, SocialProof, TestimonialsWall, RiskTool, Guardian, Wrapped, Comparison, LocalFirst, HowItWorks, Hero, MoreFeatures, BeforeAfter, Gallery). FinalCTA uses a custom `ShimmerWord` (animated gradient-clip) — left untouched. The remaining 6 headings had no gradient emphasis at all: Newsletter ("Mantente al día"/"Stay in the loop"), Pricing (`t("pricingTitle")` → "Lo compras una vez. Es tuyo para siempre."/"You buy it once. It's yours forever."), Bento (`t("featuresTitle")` → "...30 métricas."/"...30 metrics."), FAQ (`t("faqTitle")` → "Preguntas frecuentes"/"Frequently asked questions"), MistakeInvoice ("Lo que tu indisciplina te cuesta."/"What your indiscipline costs."), Changelog ("En constante evolución."/"Constantly evolving."). Added `text-gradient` on the last key word of each (día/loop, siempre./forever., métricas./metrics., frecuentes/questions, cuesta./costs., evolución./evolving.) — replacing the `t()` calls with inline `es ? <ES JSX> : <EN JSX>` ternaries that wrap the final word in `<span className="text-gradient">`. Verified `t` is still used elsewhere in Pricing/Bento/FAQ so the import remains necessary; the `t("pricingTitle")`/`t("featuresTitle")`/`t("faqTitle")` keys in `src/lib/i18n.tsx` are now unused but left in place (no harm, future-proofs against re-renders that might want the raw string).
- **Glass grain (`globals.css`)**: added a `.glass::after` rule between the existing `.glass::before` (refraction border) and the light-theme overrides. Uses the exact same SVG `fractalNoise` data URI as `body::before` (baseFrequency 0.85, numOctaves 3, stitchTiles) so the material grain matches the page-level grain. `opacity: 0.015` (per spec — even lower than the body's 0.025 because it stacks on top of an already-low-opacity acrylic fill), `mix-blend-mode: overlay` (darkens the card's shadows, lifts its highlights, never shifts the accent hue), `background-repeat: repeat` so each card gets its own tiled micro-texture, `pointer-events: none` so it never intercepts clicks, `border-radius: inherit` so the grain is clipped to the card's rounded corners. The rule lives inside `@layer components` alongside `.glass` and `.glass::before`, so it inherits the same cascade position and is overridable by utilities if a future card needs a different grain.
- **BackToTop RAF scroll + arrow rotation** (`src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx`): full rewrite of the click handler. Replaced `window.scrollTo({ behavior: "smooth" })` with a custom `requestAnimationFrame` tween: `SCROLL_MS = 600`, `easeOutCubic = t => 1 - (1-t)**3`. The tween captures `start = window.scrollY` at click time, then on each frame computes `t = min(1, elapsed/600)`, `eased = easeOutCubic(t)`, `y = start * (1 - eased)`, and `window.scrollTo(0, y)` — producing a fast-start, gently-settling curve. The in-flight RAF id is tracked in `rafRef` (a `useRef<number | null>`) and `cancelTween()` is called before starting a new tween so rapid double-clicks never fight each other; a cleanup `useEffect(() => () => cancelTween(), [...])` cancels on unmount. `prefers-reduced-motion` is read once on mount into `reduceRef` (the page already has the global CSS rule that clamps transitions to ~0ms, so reading it once is sufficient) — reduced-motion users get an instant `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` with no tween and no rotation flourish. The arrow SVG is now a `motion.svg` with `animate={rotating ? { rotate: 180 } : { rotate: 0 }}` — while the tween is in flight (`rotating` state = true), the arrow rotates a full 180° over the same 600 ms with the same `[0.22, 1, 0.36, 1]` ease curve as the rest of the UI; when the tween completes (or on cancel), `setRotating(false)` triggers the `rest` transition (a snappy spring, stiffness 320 / damping 22) that rotates the arrow back to 0 — a small "rewind" flourish. `transformOrigin: 50% 50%` ensures the rotation pivots on the arrow's visual center. The `aria-label`, `AnimatePresence` enter/exit, hover lift, hover glow, and `active:scale-95` are all preserved.
- **Chart hover scale** (`EquityCurve.tsx`, `Heatmap.tsx`, `MiniCalendar.tsx`, `Histogram.tsx`): converted each chart's outer container from `<div>` to `<motion.div>` and added `whileHover={{ scale: 1.01 }}` + `transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 300, damping: 26 }}` + `style={{ transformOrigin: "center" }}`. The 1% scale is intentionally imperceptible at a glance but adds a tactile "the chart is alive" response when the cursor enters. The spring (300/26) settles in ~250 ms with no overshoot, matching the feel of the other hover micro-interactions in the app. The existing `containerRef` (Heatmap/MiniCalendar/Histogram) is preserved on the `motion.div` — `useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)` is compatible with `motion.div` because Framer Motion's `motion.div` renders a real `<div>` under the hood. The hover-tooltips' `getBoundingClientRect()` math still works correctly because both the cell and the container scale together (same coordinate space). The inner cell-level `whileHover={{ scale: 1.08 }}` (Heatmap) and `whileHover={{ scale: 1.06 }}` (MiniCalendar) are preserved and compose with the container-level 1.01 (cell hover → 1.08×1.01 ≈ 1.09 total, but only on the hovered cell). EquityCurve's early-return "Sin datos"/"No data" div is left as a plain `<div>` (no data = no hover affordance needed).
- **PREMIUM watermark** (`src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx`): added a "PREMIUM" watermark to the Pro card only. The watermark is a `<span>` with `fontSize: 8rem`, `font-bold tracking-tighter whitespace-nowrap`, `text-tj-accent -rotate-12`, `opacity: 0.04`, positioned `absolute left-1/2 top-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2` (dead center of the card). At 8rem, "PREMIUM" is ~540px wide vs. the ~370px Pro card — so it bleeds off both edges, which is the intended "luxury security-paper" effect. The bleed is clipped by a wrapper `<div className="absolute inset-0 overflow-hidden pointer-events-none rounded-card">` so it never escapes the card's rounded bounds. The watermark's `zIndex: -1` places it in step 2 of the card's stacking context (negative z-index slot) — above the glass background fill (step 1) but below the in-flow text content (step 3) — so the price, features list, and CTA all paint on top of the watermark. To make this work, I added `isolation: "isolate"` to the Pro card's inline `style` (alongside the existing `boxShadow`). Without `isolation: isolate`, the watermark's `z-index: -1` would escape to the nearest ancestor stacking context — which for the Pro card is the `TiltCard` wrapper (TiltCard applies `transform` via Framer Motion springs, which establishes a 3D stacking context) — and the watermark would render BEHIND the entire card, invisible. `isolation: isolate` traps the negative z-index inside the card's own stacking context so the watermark renders correctly above the glass fill but below the content. The wrapper's `overflow: hidden` clips the watermark's bleed to the card's padding box (the standard containing block for `position: absolute; inset: 0`). The Core card is untouched. No content or features were removed; the "Más popular"/"Most popular" badge and rotating conic ring are preserved.
- **Verification**: `bun run lint` → exit 0, no errors or warnings (the 6 marketing files, the 4 chart files, BackToTop, Pricing, and globals.css all pass). `bun run build` → first attempt failed with a transient `ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '.next/static/.../_buildManifest.js.tmp.xxxxx'` (a known Next.js 16 + Turbopack race condition on stale `.next` caches, unrelated to my changes); `rm -rf .next && bun run build` → ✓ Compiled successfully in 13.6 s, 7/7 routes prerendered as static content (`/`, `/_not-found`, `/icon.svg`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, `/robots.txt`, `/sitemap.xml`).

Stage Summary:
- Final visual polish complete. (1) Six additional `<h2>` headings across the marketing site now use `text-gradient` on their key word — Newsletter ("día"/"loop"), Pricing ("siempre."/"forever."), Bento ("métricas."/"metrics."), FAQ ("frecuentes"/"questions"), MistakeInvoice ("cuesta."/"costs."), Changelog ("evolución."/"evolving.") — joining the 14 headings that already had it. The pattern is consistent: the gradient falls on the LAST word of the heading, so it reads as a deliberate emphasis rather than a random highlight. FinalCTA's `ShimmerWord` was intentionally left alone (it already has an animated gradient). (2) Every `.glass` surface now has a subtle `::after` grain texture (opacity 0.015, overlay blend, same SVG fractalNoise as the body grain) layered between the glass fill and the refraction border — reads as soft material depth rather than noise, even at close viewing distance. (3) The BackToTop button now uses a custom 600 ms `requestAnimationFrame` tween with `easeOutCubic` instead of the browser's native smooth-scroll, and the arrow icon rotates a full 180° during the scroll then springs back to 0 — a small "rewind" flourish synced to the scroll progress. Rapid re-clicks cancel the in-flight tween and restart cleanly; reduced-motion users get an instant jump. (4) All four chart components (EquityCurve, Heatmap, MiniCalendar, Histogram) now lift by 1% on hover via `whileHover={{ scale: 1.01 }}` with a snappy spring (300/26, no overshoot, ~250 ms settle) — adds a tactile "the chart is alive" response without distracting from the data; tooltips' `getBoundingClientRect` math remains correct because cell + container scale together. (5) The Pro pricing card now displays a giant rotated "PREMIUM" watermark (8rem, -12°, opacity 0.04) bleeding across the card behind the content — a luxury/security-paper touch that reinforces the premium tier without competing with the price. The `isolation: isolate` on the Pro card's inline style traps the watermark's `z-index: -1` inside the card's stacking context so it renders above the glass fill but below the in-flow text, even though the card is wrapped in a TiltCard (which establishes its own 3D stacking context via `transformStyle: preserve-3d`).
- Files modified: `src/components/marketing/Newsletter.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Pricing.tsx` (gradient heading + PREMIUM watermark + `isolation: isolate` on Pro card), `src/components/marketing/Bento.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/FAQ.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/MistakeInvoice.tsx`, `src/components/marketing/Changelog.tsx` (gradient headings), `src/app/globals.css` (`.glass::after` grain), `src/components/tj/BackToTop.tsx` (RAF scroll + arrow rotation), `src/components/charts/EquityCurve.tsx`, `src/components/charts/Heatmap.tsx`, `src/components/charts/MiniCalendar.tsx`, `src/components/charts/Histogram.tsx` (hover scale 1.01).
- All rules respected: no content/features removed (the 3 unused i18n keys `pricingTitle`/`featuresTitle`/`faqTitle` are kept in the STR table for safety); `"use client"` already present in all edited `.tsx` files; NO indigo/blue introduced — every accent uses the existing `--accent-base`/`--accent-hover` tokens (`text-tj-accent`, `text-gradient`, `bg-tj-accent/15`); `tnum` not applicable here (no new numeric displays added). Lint clean (exit 0). Build green (7/7 routes prerendered as static content).
- Next actions: (a) The 6 new gradient headings now render `es ? <ES JSX> : <EN JSX>` inline instead of pulling from the i18n STR table — if a future localization pass adds a 3rd language (e.g. FR/DE), those 6 headings would need to grow to a 3-way ternary or migrate to a `t()`-with-ReactNode pattern; the STR keys are still defined so a refactor back to `t()` + a `tNode()` ReactNode-aware accessor is straightforward. (b) The `.glass::after` grain adds one more paint layer per glass card — at ~50 cards on the marketing page this is negligible (the noise is a tiny inline SVG, GPU-composited), but if a future perf pass wants to shave paint time, the grain could be moved to a single shared `background-image` on a `::before`-only pseudo (combining refraction + grain into one layer). (c) The BackToTop arrow rotation uses a single Framer Motion `animate` prop keyed on `rotating` boolean — if a future spec wants the rotation to track scroll progress (e.g. arrow points up at start, rotates to point down at the end), the `rotate` value could be driven by a `useMotionValue` updated in the RAF loop instead of the binary `rotating` state. (d) The chart hover-scale `1.01` is intentionally below the visual-discrimination threshold (~2-3% is the JND for size) so it reads as "alive" rather than "growing" — if a future spec wants a more pronounced lift, bump to `1.02` or `1.03`. (e) The PREMIUM watermark uses `text-tj-accent` so it adapts to the active accent palette (oro/esmeralda/onix/aurora/seda) — currently a single fixed word "PREMIUM" in English even in ES locale; if a future spec wants it localized ("PRémium" → "PREMIUM" is already a loanword in ES trading jargon, so this is fine, but the option exists). (f) The `isolation: isolate` on the Pro card is invisible to users but is a small architectural commitment — it means any future descendant that wants to escape the Pro card's stacking context (e.g. a portal, a fixed-position tooltip) would need to be rendered elsewhere in the tree. The current badge and rotating ring both live inside the card and behave correctly, so this is a non-issue today.
